📘 simpsons-paradox RuleSpeak
›

Show in this document

📘 simpsons-paradox RuleSpeak

Digital mirror of the Simpson's Paradox domain. The entities are Studies, Treatments, Strata, and CaseCells (raw patient counts). SuccessRates, TreatmentSummaries, and TreatmentRankings are derived. The paradox falls out of the DAG as an emergent derived fact — it is never modeled directly.

Declarative business rules rendered from the rulebook — the single source of truth. Every statement below is the plain-language reading of a formula; nothing here is hand-authored prose.

Use the in the top-right corner to turn each part of the documentation on or off — RuleSpeak, English, the raw formula, the author's description, and the links to a rule's inputs and consumers. Your choices are remembered.

Entities

  • Loop 3 rules · 0 links

Loop

A loop is identified by its name.

● Title Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Short title for what this loop adds to the domain model.

● Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

planned | in-progress | complete

● New Concept Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The new first-class domain concept introduced this loop.

● Domain Question Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The natural-language question this loop's concept answers FROM the domain data.

● Mock Data Note Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

What the seed data is designed to show.

● Next Suggestion Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Suggestion for the next loop, written after witnessing the DAG at this loop's end.

↗ Tradition Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-1
In RuleSpeak

A loop's tradition name is the value of LOOKUP(TraditionId, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

In English

LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Tradition IDLoops FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose conceptual vocabulary this loop primarily draws on — tracks which tradition has driven each stage of the instrument's development.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Tradition ID Loops

Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

↗ Tradition Core Conern Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-2
In RuleSpeak

A loop's tradition core conern is the value of LOOKUP(TraditionId, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[CoreConcern]).

In English

LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[CoreConcern]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[CoreConcern])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Tradition IDLoops FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose conceptual vocabulary this loop primarily draws on — tracks which tradition has driven each stage of the instrument's development.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Tradition ID Loops

Lookup: ResearchTraditions.CoreConcern via TraditionId — which intellectual concern drove this loop.

● Commit Hash Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Full git SHA where this loop row first landed in effortless-rulebook.json — the replay anchor for this turn of the Leopold loop.

ƒ Commit Short Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-3
In RuleSpeak

A loop's commit short is computed as the first 7 character(s) of the commit hash.

In English

The first 7 characters of commit hash.

As a formula
= LEFT(Commit Hash, 7)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Commit HashLoops Full git SHA where this loop row first landed in effortless-rulebook.json — the replay anchor for this turn of the Leopold loop.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Commit Hash Loops

First 7 characters of CommitHash for display and git checkout.

● Commit Date Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the commit where this loop landed.

● Commit Message Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Git commit subject line for this loop's landing commit.

● Git Tag Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Optional milestone tag pointing at this loop's landing commit (sp-loop-milestone-*). Used for discovery replay without memorizing SHAs.

Study

A study is identified by its name.

● Title Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable title of the study.

● Source Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Citation or description of the original data source.

● Source URL Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

URL to the original source.

● Publication Year Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Year the study was published. Used for temporal stratification and pre-registration dating.

● Domain Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Research domain: medicine | epidemiology | social-science | education | legal | sports | economics | synthetic. Enables cross-domain SignalPurity stratification.

● Is Synthetic Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.

Σ Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-4
In RuleSpeak

A study's total cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the study.

In English

Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study id.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study ID)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ●Study IDStudies Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973).
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Study ID Studies

Total number of cases (trials) across all CaseCells in this study.

Σ Cell Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-5
In RuleSpeak

A study's cell count is the number of case cells related to the study.

In English

Count of CaseCells whose study is study id.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ●Study IDStudies Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973).
Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Cell Parity Studies Ingestion Compliance Studies Structural Compliant Count IngestionSummary Real Fully Compliant Count IngestionSummary All Studies Structural IngestionSummary All Real Studies Compliant IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study CaseCells ● Study ID Studies

Number of CaseCell rows for this study.

In RuleSpeak

LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

As a formula

●Tradition IDStudies FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose framing best characterizes this study's paradox type. Synthetic studies inherit the tradition of their conceptual origin.

= LOOKUP(Primary Researcher ID, Researchers[ResearcherId], Researchers[Name])

●Study IDStudies Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973).
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on

Lookup: TreatmentRankings.DistortionType for this study — the geometric classification of its paradox.

ƒ Ingestion Cell Parity Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-18
In RuleSpeak

A study is flagged ingestion cell parity if the value of AND(LOOKUP(StudyId, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, CellCount = LOOKUP(StudyId, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2).

In English

AND(LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, {{CellCount}} = LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2).

As a formula
= AND(LOOKUP(Study ID, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, Cell Count = LOOKUP(Study ID, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Study IDStudies Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973). ΣCell CountStudies Count of CaseCells whose study is study id.
Consumers 2 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Compliance Studies Σ Structural Compliant Count IngestionSummary Real Fully Compliant Count IngestionSummary All Studies Structural IngestionSummary All Real Studies Compliant IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Studies ⇢ Study CaseCells

TRUE when CellCount = 2 x StratumCount.

ƒ Ingestion Compliance Calculated 3 hops from ground truth string DR-19
In RuleSpeak

A study's ingestion compliance is computed as the value of IF(IsSynthetic, "exempt-synthetic", IF(AND(IngestionCellParity, LOOKUP(StudyId, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[VariableName]) <> ""), "all", "partial")).

In English

IF({{IsSynthetic}}, "exempt-synthetic", IF(AND({{IngestionCellParity}}, LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[VariableName]) <> ""), "all", "partial")).

As a formula
= IF(Is Synthetic, "exempt-synthetic", IF(AND(Ingestion Cell Parity, LOOKUP(Study ID, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[VariableName]) <> ""), "all", "partial"))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Is SyntheticStudies TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims. ƒIngestion Cell ParityStudies AND(LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, {{CellCount}} = LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2). ●Study IDStudies Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973).
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Real Fully Compliant Count IngestionSummary All Real Studies Compliant IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Synthetic Studies ● Study ID Studies ⇢ Study CaseCells

all | partial | exempt-synthetic.

Treatment

A treatment is identified by its name and is related to a study.

⇢ Study Relationship string

A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

FK to the parent Study.

● Treatment Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Short label used in CaseCells and comparisons (e.g. A, B).

● Description Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Plain-language description of what this treatment is.

Σ Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-20
In RuleSpeak

A treatment's total cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the treatment.

In English

Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study and treatment label is treatment label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatments FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment LabelTreatments Short label used in CaseCells and comparisons (e.g. A, B).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Pooled Success Rate Treatments
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study Treatments ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment Label Treatments

Total cases for this treatment across all strata.

Σ Total Successes Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-21
In RuleSpeak

A treatment's total successes is the total successes across the case cells related to the treatment.

In English

Sum of successes across all CaseCells whose study">study is study and treatment label is treatment label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Successes, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●SuccessesCaseCells Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatments FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment LabelTreatments Short label used in CaseCells and comparisons (e.g. A, B).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Pooled Success Rate Treatments
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study Treatments ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment Label Treatments

Total successes for this treatment across all strata.

ƒ Pooled Success Rate Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-22
In RuleSpeak

The treatment's pooled success rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the total cases is 0
  2. the total successes divided by the total cases otherwise
In English

If total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes divided by total cases).

As a formula
= IF(Total Cases = 0, "", Total Successes / Total Cases)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣTotal CasesTreatments Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label. ΣTotal SuccessesTreatments Sum of successes across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label.
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study Treatments ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment Label Treatments ● Successes CaseCells

Pooled (across all strata) success rate for this treatment: TotalSuccesses / TotalCases.

Strata

A strata is identified by its name and is related to a study.

⇢ Study Relationship string

A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

FK to the parent Study.

● Stratum Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Short label used in CaseCells (e.g. small, large, dept-A).

● Description Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Plain-language description of what defines this stratum.

Σ Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-23
In RuleSpeak

A strata's total cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the strata.

In English

Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study and stratum label is stratum label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStrata FK to the parent Study. ●Stratum LabelCaseCells The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Stratum LabelStrata Short label used in CaseCells (e.g. small, large, dept-A).
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study Strata ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label Strata

Total cases in this stratum across all treatments.

Case Cell

A case cell is identified by its name and is related to a study.

⇢ Study Relationship string

A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

FK to the parent Study.

● Stratum Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel).

● Treatment Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

● Successes Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived.

● Cases Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived.

ƒ Cell Success Rate Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-24
In RuleSpeak

The case cell's cell success rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the cases is 0
  2. the successes divided by the cases otherwise
In English

If cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (successes divided by cases).

As a formula
= IF(Cases = 0, "", Successes / Cases)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ●SuccessesCaseCells Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells

Success rate within this cell: Successes / Cases. The first derived fact in the DAG.

Σ Total Cases for Treatment Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-25
In RuleSpeak

A case cell's total cases for treatment is the total cases across the case cells related to the case cell.

In English

Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Treatment Exposure Fraction CaseCells
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Treatment Label CaseCells

Total cases across ALL strata for this treatment in this study — the denominator for TreatmentExposureFraction.

ƒ Treatment Exposure Fraction Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-26
In RuleSpeak

The case cell's treatment exposure fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the total cases for treatment is 0
  2. the cases divided by the total cases for treatment otherwise
In English

If total cases for treatment equals 0 then "", otherwise (cases divided by total cases for treatment).

As a formula
= IF(Total Cases for Treatment = 0, "", Cases / Total Cases for Treatment)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣTotal Cases for TreatmentCaseCells Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label. ●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Treatment Label CaseCells

Fraction of this treatment's total cases that fall in this stratum: Cases / TotalCasesForTreatment. High imbalance across strata is the mechanism of confounding — when treatments are allocated very differently across strata, the pooled rate conflates treatment effect with stratum difficulty.

ƒ Is Valid Ingestion Cell Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-27
In RuleSpeak

A case cell is considered a valid ingestion cell if all of the following:

    In English

    Cases is greater than 0, successes is at least 0, and successes is at most cases.

    As a formula
    = AND(Cases > 0, Successes >= 0, Successes <= Cases)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ●SuccessesCaseCells Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived.
    Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
    Σ Valid Cell Count IngestionSummary All Cells Valid IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells

    TRUE when Cases>0, Successes>=0, and Successes<=Cases.

    Stratum Summary

    A stratum summary is identified by its name and is related to a study.

    ⇢ Study Relationship string

    A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

    FK to the parent Study.

    ● Stratum Label Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Which stratum (matches Strata.StratumLabel).

    ● Treatment Label Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

    Σ Stratum Successes Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-28
    In RuleSpeak

    A stratum summary's stratum successes is the total successes across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

    In English

    Sum of successes across all CaseCells whose study">study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is treatment label.

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Successes, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
    Inputs 7 fields this rule reads
    ●SuccessesCaseCells Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ●Stratum LabelCaseCells The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Stratum LabelStratumSummaries Which stratum (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment LabelStratumSummaries Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
    Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Stratum Success Rate StratumSummaries Weighted Stratum Rate StratumSummaries Pooled Rate From Weights a TreatmentRankings Pooled Rate From Weights B TreatmentRankings
    Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries

    Total successes in this (stratum, treatment) cell for this study.

    Σ Stratum Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-29
    In RuleSpeak

    A stratum summary's stratum cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

    In English

    Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is treatment label.

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
    Inputs 7 fields this rule reads
    ●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ●Stratum LabelCaseCells The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Stratum LabelStratumSummaries Which stratum (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment LabelStratumSummaries Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
    Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Stratum Success Rate StratumSummaries Weighted Stratum Rate StratumSummaries Pooled Rate From Weights a TreatmentRankings Pooled Rate From Weights B TreatmentRankings
    Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries

    Total cases in this (stratum, treatment) cell for this study.

    ƒ Stratum Success Rate Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-30
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's stratum success rate is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the stratum cases is 0
    2. the stratum successes divided by the stratum cases otherwise
    In English

    If stratum cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes divided by stratum cases).

    As a formula
    = IF(Stratum Cases = 0, "", Stratum Successes / Stratum Cases)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ΣStratum CasesStratumSummaries Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is treatment label. ΣStratum SuccessesStratumSummaries Sum of successes across all CaseCells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is treatment label.
    Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Weighted Stratum Rate StratumSummaries Pooled Rate From Weights a TreatmentRankings Pooled Rate From Weights B TreatmentRankings
    Ground truth at the leaves 8 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Successes CaseCells

    Success rate within this stratum for this treatment: StratumSuccesses / StratumCases. The true per-stratum picture.

    Σ Stratum Successes a Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-31

    A stratum summary's stratum successes a is the total successes across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

    ƒ Stratum Rate B Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-36
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's stratum rate b is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the stratum cases b is 0
    2. the stratum successes b divided by the stratum cases b otherwise
    In English

    If stratum cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes b divided by stratum cases b).

    As a formula
    = IF(Stratum Cases B = 0, "", Stratum Successes B / Stratum Cases B)

    Success rate for treatment B in this stratum.

    ƒ Stratum Winner Calculated 3 hops from ground truth string DR-37
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's stratum winner is determined by the following priority:

    1. the literal “A” if the stratum rate a is greater than the stratum rate b
    2. the literal “B” otherwise
    In English

    If stratum rate a is greater than stratum rate b then "A", otherwise "B".

    As a formula
    = IF(Stratum Rate a > Stratum Rate B, "A", "B")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒStratum Rate aStratumSummaries If stratum cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes a divided by stratum cases a). ƒStratum Rate BStratumSummaries If stratum cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes b divided by stratum cases b).
    Consumers 2 direct +39 transitive rules that read this one

    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells

    Which treatment wins in this stratum: 'A' if StratumRateA > StratumRateB, else 'B'. Used by TreatmentRankings to detect paradox.

    Σ Study Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-38
    In RuleSpeak

    A stratum summary's study">study total cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

    In English

    Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study.

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study)

    Total cases across ALL strata and ALL treatments in this study. The denominator for StratumFraction.

    Σ Stratum Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-39
    In RuleSpeak

    A stratum summary's stratum total cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

    In English

    Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study and stratum label is stratum label.

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label)

    Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

    Total cases in this stratum across both treatments (A + B combined). Numerator for StratumFraction.

    ƒ Stratum Fraction Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-40
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's stratum fraction is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the study total cases is 0
    2. the stratum total cases divided by the study total cases otherwise
    In English

    If study total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum total cases divided by study total cases).

    As a formula
    = IF(Study Total Cases = 0, "", Stratum Total Cases / Study Total Cases)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ΣStudy Total CasesStratumSummaries Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study. ΣStratum Total CasesStratumSummaries Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study and stratum label is stratum label.
    Consumers 2 direct +66 transitive rules that read this one

Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Signal Purity Gap ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

    Fraction of the study's total cases that fall in this stratum: StratumTotalCases / StudyTotalCases. The weight the confounder assigns to each stratum. A large StratumFraction on a low-success stratum pulls the pooled rate down; when that stratum is also over-allocated to one treatment, it creates the reversal.

    ƒ Weighted Stratum Rate Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-41
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's weighted stratum rate is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the stratum fraction is blank
    2. the stratum fraction times the stratum success rate otherwise
    In English

    If stratum fraction is "" then "", otherwise (stratum fraction times stratum success rate).

    As a formula
    = IF(Stratum Fraction = "", "", Stratum Fraction * Stratum Success Rate)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒStratum FractionStratumSummaries If study total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum total cases divided by study total cases). ƒStratum Success RateStratumSummaries If stratum cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes divided by stratum cases).
    Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
    Σ Pooled Rate From Weights a TreatmentRankings Σ Pooled Rate From Weights B TreatmentRankings
    Ground truth at the leaves 8 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Successes CaseCells

    StratumFraction × StratumSuccessRate. The contribution of this (stratum, treatment) row to the study-level pooled rate. Summing WeightedStratumRate across all strata for a given treatment reconstructs that treatment's pooled rate from stratum weights — witnessing the mechanism equation.

    Σ Treatment a Cases Here Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-42
    In RuleSpeak

    A stratum summary's treatment a cases here is the total cases across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

    In English

    Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is "A".

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label, Treatment Label, "A")
    Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
    ●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ●Stratum LabelCaseCells The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Stratum LabelStratumSummaries Which stratum (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
    Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Allocation Fraction a StratumSummaries Allocation Bias StratumSummaries
    Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells

    Cases for treatment A in this stratum — used to compute TreatmentA's allocation fraction in this stratum.

    Σ Treatment B Cases Here Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-43
    In RuleSpeak

    A stratum summary's treatment b cases here is the total cases across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

    In English

    Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is "B".

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label, Treatment Label, "B")
    Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
    ●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ●Stratum LabelCaseCells The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Stratum LabelStratumSummaries Which stratum (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
    Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Allocation Fraction B StratumSummaries Allocation Bias StratumSummaries
    Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells

    Cases for treatment B in this stratum — used to compute TreatmentB's allocation fraction in this stratum.

    Σ Treatment a Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-44
    In RuleSpeak

    A stratum summary's treatment a total cases is the total cases across the stratum summary's case cells that have a treatment label of “A”.

    In English

    Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study and treatment label is "A".

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, "A")
    Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
    ●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
    Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Allocation Fraction a StratumSummaries Allocation Bias StratumSummaries
    Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells

    Total cases for treatment A across all strata in this study — denominator for AllocationFractionA.

    Σ Treatment B Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-45
    In RuleSpeak

    A stratum summary's treatment b total cases is the total cases across the stratum summary's case cells that have a treatment label of “B”.

    In English

    Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study and treatment label is "B".

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, "B")
    Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
    ●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
    Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Allocation Fraction B StratumSummaries Allocation Bias StratumSummaries
    Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells

    Total cases for treatment B across all strata in this study — denominator for AllocationFractionB.

    ƒ Allocation Fraction a Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-46
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's allocation fraction a is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the treatment a total cases is 0
    2. the treatment a cases here divided by the treatment a total cases otherwise
    In English

    If treatment a total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (treatment a cases here divided by treatment a total cases).

    As a formula
    = IF(Treatment a Total Cases = 0, "", Treatment a Cases Here / Treatment a Total Cases)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ΣTreatment a Total CasesStratumSummaries Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is "A". ΣTreatment a Cases HereStratumSummaries Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is "A".
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Allocation Bias StratumSummaries
    Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

    Fraction of treatment A's total cases that land in this stratum: TreatmentACasesHere / TreatmentATotalCases. Equivalent to TreatmentExposureFraction for A.

    ƒ Allocation Fraction B Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-47
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's allocation fraction b is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the treatment b total cases is 0
    2. the treatment b cases here divided by the treatment b total cases otherwise
    In English

    If treatment b total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (treatment b cases here divided by treatment b total cases).

    As a formula
    = IF(Treatment B Total Cases = 0, "", Treatment B Cases Here / Treatment B Total Cases)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ΣTreatment B Total CasesStratumSummaries Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is "B". ΣTreatment B Cases HereStratumSummaries Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is "B".
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Allocation Bias StratumSummaries
    Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

    Fraction of treatment B's total cases that land in this stratum: TreatmentBCasesHere / TreatmentBTotalCases.

    ƒ Allocation Bias Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-48
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's allocation bias is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the allocation fraction a is blank
    2. the allocation fraction a minus the allocation fraction b otherwise
    In English

    If allocation fraction a is "" then "", otherwise (allocation fraction a minus allocation fraction b).

    As a formula
    = IF(Allocation Fraction a = "", "", Allocation Fraction a - Allocation Fraction B)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒAllocation Fraction aStratumSummaries If treatment a total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (treatment a cases here divided by treatment a total cases). ƒAllocation Fraction BStratumSummaries If treatment b total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (treatment b cases here divided by treatment b total cases).
    Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

    AllocationFractionA minus AllocationFractionB: how much more of treatment A (relative to B) is concentrated in this stratum. Positive = A is over-allocated here; negative = B is over-allocated here. When AllocationBias is large and negative in a low-success stratum, treatment A is under-represented in the hard cases — making A look worse in the pooled view than it is in any stratum. This is the confounder's fingerprint as a signed number.

    ƒ Stratum Gap Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-49
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's stratum gap is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the stratum rate a is blank
    2. the stratum rate a minus the stratum rate b otherwise
    In English

    If stratum rate a is "" then "", otherwise (stratum rate a minus stratum rate b).

    As a formula
    = IF(Stratum Rate a = "", "", Stratum Rate a - Stratum Rate B)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒStratum Rate aStratumSummaries If stratum cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes a divided by stratum cases a). ƒStratum Rate BStratumSummaries If stratum cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes b divided by stratum cases b).
    Consumers 1 direct +64 transitive rules that read this one

Strength ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells

    Signed rate difference within this stratum: StratumRateA minus StratumRateB. Positive = A wins here; negative = B wins here; zero = tied. When every stratum has StratumGap > 0 but the pooled gap is negative, the contradiction IS Simpson's Paradox made arithmetically visible in one column. Multiply StratumGap × StratumFraction and sum across strata to get the equal-weight pooled gap (the signal allocation would produce without confounding).

    ƒ Weighted Stratum Gap Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-50
    In RuleSpeak

    The stratum summary's weighted stratum gap is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the stratum gap is blank
    2. the stratum gap times the stratum fraction otherwise
    In English

    If stratum gap is "" then "", otherwise (stratum gap times stratum fraction).

    As a formula
    = IF(Stratum Gap = "", "", Stratum Gap * Stratum Fraction)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒStratum GapStratumSummaries If stratum rate a is "" then "", otherwise (stratum rate a minus stratum rate b). ƒStratum FractionStratumSummaries If study total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum total cases divided by study total cases).
