Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Signal Purity Gap ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells

    StratumGap × StratumFraction: the contribution of this stratum's treatment-rate difference to the equal-weight pooled gap. Summing WeightedStratumGap across all TreatmentA rows for a study gives WeightedStratumGapSum — the pooled gap the study would show under balanced allocation. This is the per-stratum building block of the sign-flip test.

    Model Summary

    A model summary is identified by its name.

    Σ Reversal Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-51
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's reversal count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose is reversal is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Is Reversal, TRUE())
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒIs ReversalTreatmentRankings If is sign flip is "" then "", otherwise is sign flip.
    Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Study Count ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary Type a Fraction ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Number of studies with IsReversal=TRUE (sign-flip / allocation-driven reversal).

    Σ Non Reversal Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-52
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's non reversal count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose is reversal is false().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Is Reversal, FALSE())
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒIs ReversalTreatmentRankings If is sign flip is "" then "", otherwise is sign flip.
    Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Study Count ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary Type a Fraction ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Number of studies with IsReversal=FALSE.

    ƒ Study Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-53
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's study count is computed as the reversal count plus the non reversal count.

    In English

    Reversal count plus non reversal count.

    As a formula
    = Reversal Count + Non Reversal Count
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ΣReversal CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose is reversal is true(). ΣNon Reversal CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose is reversal is false().
    Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary ƒ Type a Fraction ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Total number of TreatmentRankings in this model: ReversalCount + NonReversalCount.

    Σ Explained Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-54
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's explained count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose is paradox explained is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Is Paradox Explained, TRUE())
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatmentRankings Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0.
    Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ⇢ Study StratumVariables ● Affects Treatment Assignment StratumVariables ● Affects Outcome StratumVariables ● Causal Role StratumVariables

    Number of studies with IsParadoxExplained=TRUE (reversal AND confirmed confounder).

    Σ Total Paradox Strength Aggregation 7 hops from ground truth number DR-55
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's total paradox strength is the total paradox strength across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Sum of paradox strength across all TreatmentRankings whose treatment a is "A".

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Paradox Strength, Treatment a, "A")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒParadox StrengthTreatmentRankings If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (pooled gap times (strata won by loser divided by stratum count)). ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

    Sum of ParadoxStrength across all studies. Used to compute average.

    ƒ Avg Paradox Strength Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-56
    In RuleSpeak

    The model summary's avg paradox strength is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the study count is 0
    2. the total paradox strength divided by the study count otherwise
    In English

    If study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (total paradox strength divided by study count).

    As a formula
    = IF(Study Count = 0, "", Total Paradox Strength / Study Count)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒStudy CountModelSummary Reversal count plus non reversal count. ΣTotal Paradox StrengthModelSummary Sum of paradox strength across all TreatmentRankings whose treatment a is "A".
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Average ParadoxStrength across all studies: TotalParadoxStrength / StudyCount. A scalar summary of how paradox-rich this dataset is.

    Σ Type a Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-57
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's type a count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "A".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "A")
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
    Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Type a Fraction ModelSummary ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Studies classified as Type-A: sign flip AND full reversal (ReversalIntensity=1). The canonical Simpson's Paradox — allocation distortion reverses the pooled winner.

    Σ Type B Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-58
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's type b count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "B".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "B")
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Studies classified as Type-B: sign flip but partial reversal (ReversalIntensity<1). Allocation distortion creates a sign flip but the pooled signal partially reflects per-stratum reality.

    Σ Type D Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-59
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's type d count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "D".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "D")
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
    Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary ƒ Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Studies classified as Type-D: no sign flip, negligible distortion. Pooled analysis is trustworthy — allocation is sufficiently balanced.

    ƒ Type a Fraction Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-60
    In RuleSpeak

    The model summary's type a fraction is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the study count is 0
    2. the type a count divided by the study count otherwise
    In English

    If study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (type a count divided by study count).

    As a formula
    = IF(Study Count = 0, "", Type a Count / Study Count)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒStudy CountModelSummary Reversal count plus non reversal count. ΣType a CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "A".
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Fraction of studies that are Type-A (full canonical reversals). TypeACount / StudyCount.

    ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Calculated 9 hops from ground truth string DR-61
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's distortion taxonomy coverage is computed as the literal “A:”, followed by the type a count, followed by the literal “ B:”, followed by the type b count, followed by the literal “ C+:”, followed by the c amplification count, followed by the literal “ C-:”, followed by the c compression count, followed by the literal “ D:”, followed by the type d count.

    In English

    "A:", type a count, " B:", type b count, " C+:", c amplification count, " C-:", c compression count, " D:", joined with type d count.

    As a formula
    = CONCAT("A:", Type a Count, " B:", Type B Count, " C+:", C Amplification Count, " C-:", C Compression Count, " D:", Type D Count)
    Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
    ΣType a CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "A". ΣType B CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "B". ΣC Amplification CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "C+". ΣC Compression CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "C-". ΣType D CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "D".
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Human-readable summary of the four-type distribution: e.g. 'A:2 B:1 C:1 D:0'. The model's self-portrait of its own distortion geometry.

    ƒ Distortion Only Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-62
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's distortion only count is computed as the c amplification count plus the c compression count.

    In English

    C amplification count plus c compression count.

    As a formula
    = C Amplification Count + C Compression Count
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ΣC Amplification CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "C+". ΣC Compression CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "C-".
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Number of studies with real allocation distortion but no sign flip: TypeCCount. The pooled winner is correct but the effect size is biased. Distinct from ZeroStrengthCount, which conflates Type-C (real distortion) with Type-D (neutral).

    Σ C Amplification Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-63
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's c amplification count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "C+".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "C+")
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
    Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary ƒ Distortion Only Count ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Number of studies classified as type C+ (allocation-amplified): pooled winner is correct but pooled gap overstates true advantage. Causes researcher overconfidence — may accept higher cost/risk than true effect warrants.

    Σ C Compression Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-64
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's c compression count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "C-".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "C-")
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
    Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary ƒ Distortion Only Count ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Number of studies classified as type C- (allocation-compressed): pooled winner is correct but pooled gap understates true advantage. Causes researcher underconfidence — effective treatment may be prematurely abandoned.

    Σ Avg Signal Purity Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth number DR-65
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's avg signal purity is an aggregated value computed across the model summary's related records.

    In English

    Sum of signal purity across all TreatmentRankings whose study is "<>" divided by count of TreatmentRankings whose study is "<>".

    As a formula
    = SUMIFS(Signal Purity, Study, "<>") / COUNTIFS(Study, "<>")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒSignal PurityTreatmentRankings If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)). ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study.
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Average SignalPurity across all TreatmentRankings. Below 0.5 means allocation noise exceeds true signal on average — the corpus is predominantly misleading at the pooled level. Above 0.5 means the corpus is predominantly trustworthy.

    Σ Sweep Corrected Gap Max Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth number DR-66
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's sweep corrected gap max is the largest sweep corrected gap across the allocation sweep related to the model summary.

    In English

    Largest sweep corrected gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is "kidney-1986".

    As a formula
    = MAXIFS(Sweep Corrected Gap, Study ID, "kidney-1986")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒSweep Corrected GapAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]). ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Range ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Study ID AllocationSweep

    Maximum SweepCorrectedGap across all AllocationSweep rows. Should equal SweepCorrectedGapMin — the invariant.

    Σ Sweep Corrected Gap Min Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth number DR-67
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's sweep corrected gap min is the smallest sweep corrected gap across the allocation sweep related to the model summary.

    In English

    Smallest sweep corrected gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is "kidney-1986".

    As a formula
    = MINIFS(Sweep Corrected Gap, Study ID, "kidney-1986")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒSweep Corrected GapAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]). ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Range ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Study ID AllocationSweep

    Minimum SweepCorrectedGap across all AllocationSweep rows. Should equal SweepCorrectedGapMax — the invariant.

    ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Range Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-68
    In RuleSpeak

    The model summary's sweep corrected gap range is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the sweep corrected gap max is blank
    2. the sweep corrected gap max minus the sweep corrected gap min otherwise
    In English

    If sweep corrected gap max is "" then "", otherwise (sweep corrected gap max minus sweep corrected gap min).

    As a formula
    = IF(Sweep Corrected Gap Max = "", "", Sweep Corrected Gap Max - Sweep Corrected Gap Min)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ΣSweep Corrected Gap MaxModelSummary Largest sweep corrected gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is "kidney-1986". ΣSweep Corrected Gap MinModelSummary Smallest sweep corrected gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is "kidney-1986".
    Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Study ID AllocationSweep

    SweepCorrectedGapMax − SweepCorrectedGapMin. The allocation-invariance witness: this should be zero (up to floating-point rounding). If nonzero, the corrected gap is not allocation-free.

    Σ Sweep Pooled Gap Range Aggregation 6 hops from ground truth number DR-69
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's sweep pooled gap range is an aggregated value computed across the model summary's related records.

    In English

    Largest sweep pooled gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is "kidney-1986" minus smallest sweep pooled gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is "kidney-1986".

    As a formula
    = MAXIFS(Sweep Pooled Gap, Study ID, "kidney-1986") - MINIFS(Sweep Pooled Gap, Study ID, "kidney-1986")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒSweep Pooled GapAllocationSweep If sweep pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (sweep pooled rate a minus sweep pooled rate b). ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
    Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep

    MAX(SweepPooledGap) − MIN(SweepPooledGap) across all AllocationSweep rows. Expected to be large (≈0.21) — the pooled gap wanders as allocation shifts.

    ● Real Study Count Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Number of real (non-synthetic) published studies in the corpus.

    ● Avg Signal Purity Reversal Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Average SignalPurity across reversal studies (type A and B). Theorem predicts this must be < 0.5.

    ● Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Average SignalPurity across non-reversal studies (type C+, C-, D). Theorem predicts this must be >= 0.5 except edge cases.

    ● Signal Purity Gap Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Separation between reversal and non-reversal average SignalPurity. The larger this gap, the more discriminating the threshold at 0.5.

    ● Medicine Study Count Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Real published studies in the medicine domain.

    ● Epidemiology Study Count Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Real published studies in the epidemiology domain.

    ● Other Domain Study Count Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Real published studies outside medicine and epidemiology (sports, legal, education, social-science).

    ● Domain Diversity Note Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Human-readable domain breakdown. Witnesses corpus diversity — a prerequisite for cross-domain SignalPurity claims.

    ● Synthetic Phase Count Ground truth integer

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Number of parameter grid points in SyntheticPhase. Refreshed from vw_phase_diagram_summary by 05b-customize-data.sql.

    ● Phase Diagram Complete Ground truth boolean

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    TRUE when all five DistortionType cells are populated in the synthetic phase grid. Refreshed from vw_phase_diagram_summary by 05b.

    ● Phase Taxonomy Coverage Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Type counts across the synthetic phase grid. Refreshed from vw_phase_diagram_summary by 05b.

    Σ Ingestion Protocol Item Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-70
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's ingestion protocol item count is the number of ingestion protocol related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of IngestionProtocol whose protocol id is "<>".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Protocol ID, "<>")
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ●Protocol IDIngestionProtocol Unique slug for this contract item.
    Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Protocol ID IngestionProtocol

    Number of rows in IngestionProtocol — the documented contract items.

    ↗ Corpus Passes Ingestion Contract Lookup boolean DR-71
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's corpus passes ingestion contract is the value of LOOKUP("ingestion-v1", IngestionSummary[IngestionSummaryId], IngestionSummary[IngestionContractPasses]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("ingestion-v1", IngestionSummary[IngestionSummaryId], IngestionSummary[IngestionContractPasses]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("ingestion-v1", IngestionSummary[IngestionSummaryId], IngestionSummary[IngestionContractPasses])

    Lookup: IngestionSummary.IngestionContractPasses.

    ↗ Ingestion Witness Note Lookup string DR-72
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's ingestion witness note is the value of LOOKUP("ingestion-v1", IngestionSummary[IngestionSummaryId], IngestionSummary[IngestionWitnessNote]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("ingestion-v1", IngestionSummary[IngestionSummaryId], IngestionSummary[IngestionWitnessNote]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("ingestion-v1", IngestionSummary[IngestionSummaryId], IngestionSummary[IngestionWitnessNote])

    Human-readable ingestion contract witness from IngestionSummary.

    ↗ Catalog Entry Count Lookup integer DR-73
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's catalog entry count is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TotalCatalogEntries]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TotalCatalogEntries]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TotalCatalogEntries])

    Total catalog rows.

    ↗ Pending Import Count Lookup integer DR-74
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's pending import count is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CandidateCount]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CandidateCount]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CandidateCount])

    Candidate studies queued.

    ↗ Ready to Encode Count Lookup integer DR-75
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's ready to encode count is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[ReadyToEncodeCount]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[ReadyToEncodeCount]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[ReadyToEncodeCount])

    Encode-ready candidates.

    ↗ Import Session Ready Lookup boolean DR-76
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's import session ready is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[ImportSessionReady]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[ImportSessionReady]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[ImportSessionReady])

    Bulk import session readiness flag.

    ↗ Catalog Witness Note Lookup string DR-77
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's catalog witness note is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogWitnessNote]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogWitnessNote]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogWitnessNote])

    Import backlog witness string.

    ƒ Latent Type D Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth number DR-78
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's latent type d count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose latent flip potential is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Latent Flip Potential, TRUE())
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒLatent Flip PotentialTreatmentRankings If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.
    Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
    ƒ Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Type-D studies with LatentFlipPotential=TRUE.

    ƒ Stable Type D Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth number DR-79
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's stable type d count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are not latent flip potential.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "D" and latent flip potential is false().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "D", Latent Flip Potential, FALSE())
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒLatent Flip PotentialTreatmentRankings If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Type-D studies without latent flip potential (allocation-stable at observed and counterfactual sweep).

    ƒ Latent Type D Fraction Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-80
    In RuleSpeak

    The model summary's latent type d fraction is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the type d count is 0
    2. the latent type d count divided by the type d count otherwise
    In English

    If type d count equals 0 then "", otherwise (latent type d count divided by type d count).

    As a formula
    = IF(Type D Count = 0, "", Latent Type D Count / Type D Count)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ΣType D CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "D". ƒLatent Type D CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose latent flip potential is true().
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Fraction of Type-D studies with latent flip potential.

    ƒ Cross Zero Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-81
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's cross zero count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose pooled gap crosses zero is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Pooled Gap Crosses Zero, TRUE())
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒPooled Gap Crosses ZeroTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]).
    Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings

    Studies whose pooled gap crosses zero under allocation sweep.

    ƒ Sign Flip Signal Purity Max Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-82
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's sign flip signal purity max is the largest signal purity across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Largest signal purity across all TreatmentRankings whose is sign flip is true().

    As a formula
    = MAXIFS(Signal Purity, Is Sign Flip, TRUE())
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒSignal PurityTreatmentRankings If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Maximum SignalPurity among sign-flip studies. Must stay below 0.5 for the signal-purity theorem.

    ƒ Economics Sign Flip Count Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-83
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's economics sign flip count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are sign flips.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose study domain is "economics" and is sign flip is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "economics", Is Sign Flip, TRUE())
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒStudy DomainTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Sign-flip count in economics domain.

    ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-84
    In RuleSpeak

    The model summary's avg pooled gap latent d is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the latent type d count is 0
    2. the average pooled gap across the treatment rankings related to the model summary otherwise
    In English

    If latent type d count equals 0 then "", otherwise average pooled gap across all TreatmentRankings whose latent flip potential is true().

    As a formula
    = IF(Latent Type D Count = 0, "", AVERAGEIFS(Pooled Gap, Latent Flip Potential, TRUE()))
    Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
    ƒLatent Type D CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose latent flip potential is true(). ƒPooled GapTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b). ƒLatent Flip PotentialTreatmentRankings If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Average PooledGap among Type-D studies with latent flip potential.

    ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-85
    In RuleSpeak

    The model summary's avg pooled gap stable d is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the stable type d count is 0
    2. the average pooled gap across the model summary's treatment rankings that are not latent flip potential otherwise
    In English

    If stable type d count equals 0 then "", otherwise average pooled gap across all TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "D" and latent flip potential is false().

    As a formula
    = IF(Stable Type D Count = 0, "", AVERAGEIFS(Pooled Gap, Distortion Type, "D", Latent Flip Potential, FALSE()))
    Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
    ƒStable Type D CountModelSummary Count of TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "D" and latent flip potential is false(). ƒPooled GapTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b). ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒLatent Flip PotentialTreatmentRankings If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Average PooledGap among allocation-stable Type-D studies.

    ƒ Epidemiology Avg Distortion Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-86
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's epidemiology avg distortion is the average allocation distortion across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    As a formula
    = AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Study Domain, "epidemiology")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads

    In English

    Average allocation distortion across all TreatmentRankings whose study domain is "education".

    As a formula
    = AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Study Domain, "education")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒAllocation DistortionTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒStudy DomainTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]).

    ƒ Confounder Sign Flip Count Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-88
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's confounder sign flip count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are sign flips.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose stratum causal role is "confounder" and is sign flip is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "confounder", Is Sign Flip, TRUE())
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on

    Sign-flip count among confounder-labeled studies.

    ƒ Confounder Latent Only Count Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-89
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's confounder latent only count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are latent only flips.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose stratum causal role is "confounder" and is latent only flip is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "confounder", Is Latent Only Flip, TRUE())
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Latent Only FlipTreatmentRankings If pooled gap crosses zero is "" then "", otherwise pooled gap crosses zero equals true() and is sign flip equals false().
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Latent-only flip count among confounder-labeled studies.

    ƒ Collider Selection Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-90
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's collider selection count is computed as the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary plus the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose stratum causal role is "collider" plus count of TreatmentRankings whose stratum causal role is "selection".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "collider") + COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "selection")
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]).
    Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings

    Studies tagged collider or selection on StratumVariables.

    ƒ Collider Selection Manifest Count Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-91
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's collider selection manifest count is computed as the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are sign flips plus the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are sign flips.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose stratum causal role is "collider" and is sign flip is true() plus count of TreatmentRankings whose stratum causal role is "selection" and is sign flip is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "collider", Is Sign Flip, TRUE()) + COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "selection", Is Sign Flip, TRUE())
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Manifest sign-flips among collider/selection studies.

    ƒ Collider Selection Latent Only Count Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-92
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's collider selection latent only count is computed as the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are latent only flips plus the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are latent only flips.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose stratum causal role is "collider" and is latent only flip is true() plus count of TreatmentRankings whose stratum causal role is "selection" and is latent only flip is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "collider", Is Latent Only Flip, TRUE()) + COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "selection", Is Latent Only Flip, TRUE())
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Latent Only FlipTreatmentRankings If pooled gap crosses zero is "" then "", otherwise pooled gap crosses zero equals true() and is sign flip equals false().
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings
