    Σ Explained Confounder Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-93
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's explained confounder count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose is paradox explained is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Is Paradox Explained, TRUE())
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatmentRankings Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0.
    Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ⇢ Study StratumVariables ● Affects Treatment Assignment StratumVariables ● Affects Outcome StratumVariables ● Causal Role StratumVariables

    Count of TreatmentRankings rows where IsParadoxExplained=TRUE (should equal ConfounderSignFlipCount).

    Σ Contested or Mediator Explained Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-94
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's contested or mediator explained count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are paradox explained and are not adjustment appropriate.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose is sign flip is true(), is paradox explained is true(), and adjustment appropriate is false().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Is Sign Flip, TRUE(), Is Paradox Explained, TRUE(), Adjustment Appropriate, FALSE())
    Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
    ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatmentRankings Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0. ƒAdjustment AppropriateTreatmentRankings Mediator risk count equals 0 and causal claim status is "established".
    Ground truth at the leaves 16 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ⇢ Study StratumVariables ● Affects Treatment Assignment StratumVariables ● Affects Outcome StratumVariables ● Causal Role StratumVariables ● Conditioning Risk StratumVariables

    Count of contested or mediator sign-flip rows where IsParadoxExplained=TRUE (should be zero).

    ƒ Discovery Witness Note Calculated 11 hops from ground truth string DR-95
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's discovery witness note is computed as the literal “sweep: latentD=”, followed by the latent type d fraction, followed by the literal “ purityMax=”, followed by the sign flip signal purity max, followed by the literal “ catalogExact=”, followed by the type prediction match rate, followed by the literal “ identities=”, followed by the confounder identity count.

    In English

    "sweep: latentD=", latent type d fraction, " purityMax=", sign flip signal purity max, " catalogExact=", type prediction match rate, " identities=", joined with confounder identity count.

    As a formula
    = CONCAT("sweep: latentD=", Latent Type D Fraction, " purityMax=", Sign Flip Signal Purity Max, " catalogExact=", Type Prediction Match Rate, " identities=", Confounder Identity Count)
    Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
    ƒLatent Type D FractionModelSummary If type d count equals 0 then "", otherwise (latent type d count divided by type d count). ƒSign Flip Signal Purity MaxModelSummary Largest signal purity across all TreatmentRankings whose is sign flip is true(). ↗Type Prediction Match RateModelSummary LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]). ΣConfounder Identity CountModelSummary Count(confounder identity id).
    Ground truth at the leaves 12 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ● Confounder Identity ID ConfounderIdentities

    Research sweep synthesis rollup (loops 61–66).

    ↗ Type Prediction Match Count Lookup integer DR-96
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's type prediction match count is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchCount]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchCount]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchCount])

    Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.TypePredictionMatchCount.

    ↗ Type Prediction Mismatch Count Lookup integer DR-97
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's type prediction mismatch count is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMismatchCount]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMismatchCount]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMismatchCount])

    Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.TypePredictionMismatchCount.

    ↗ Type Prediction Match Rate Lookup number DR-98
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's type prediction match rate is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate])
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary

    Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.TypePredictionMatchRate.

    ↗ Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Lookup number DR-99
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's sign flip prediction match rate is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate])

    Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.SignFlipPredictionMatchRate.

    ↗ Catalog Prediction Witness Note Lookup string DR-100
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's catalog prediction witness note is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogPredictionWitnessNote]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogPredictionWitnessNote]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogPredictionWitnessNote])

    Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.CatalogPredictionWitnessNote.

    ● High Imbalance Sign Flip Threshold Ground truth number

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    max_stratum_imbalance cutoff for geometry-controlled domain screening (loop-66). Set from corpus median by customize SQL.

    ● Economics High Imbalance Sign Flip Count Ground truth number

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Sign-flip count among economics studies with max_stratum_imbalance >= threshold.

    ● Epidemiology High Imbalance Sign Flip Rate Ground truth number

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Sign-flip rate among high-imbalance epidemiology studies.

    ● Legal High Imbalance Sign Flip Rate Ground truth number

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Sign-flip rate among high-imbalance legal studies.

    ● Sports High Imbalance Sign Flip Rate Ground truth number

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Sign-flip rate among high-imbalance sports studies.

    ƒ C Plus Avg Distortion Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-101
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's c plus avg distortion is the average allocation distortion across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Average allocation distortion across all TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "C+".

    As a formula
    = AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Distortion Type, "C+")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒAllocation DistortionTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Mean AllocationDistortion among Type-C+ studies.

    ƒ C Minus Avg Distortion Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-102
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's c minus avg distortion is the average allocation distortion across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Average allocation distortion across all TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "C-".

    As a formula
    = AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Distortion Type, "C-")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒAllocation DistortionTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Mean AllocationDistortion among Type-C- studies.

    ƒ Type D Avg Distortion Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-103
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's type d avg distortion is the average allocation distortion across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Average allocation distortion across all TreatmentRankings whose distortion type is "D".

    As a formula
    = AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Distortion Type, "D")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒAllocation DistortionTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Mean AllocationDistortion among Type-D studies.

    ƒ Sweep Fragile Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-104
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's sweep fragile count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose is sweep fragile is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Is Sweep Fragile, TRUE())
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒIs Sweep FragileTreatmentRankings Distortion type is "D", signal purity equals 1, allocation distortion equals 0, sweep pooled gap range is greater than 0.3, and allocation fragility is at least 10.
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Count of TreatmentRankings with IsSweepFragile=TRUE.

    ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected a Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-105
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's expansion wave1 economics expected a count is the number of the model summary's candidate study catalog that are imported; have a domain of “economics”; and have a expected distortion type of “A”.

    In English

    Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1", domain is "economics", is imported is true(), and expected distortion type is "A".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Expansion Wave, "expansion-wave-1", Domain, "economics", Is Imported, TRUE(), Expected Distortion Type, "A")
    Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
    ●Expansion WaveCandidateStudyCatalog Catalog wave tag; loop-67 expansion rows = expansion-wave-1. ●DomainCandidateStudyCatalog Domain tag: medicine, epidemiology, legal, sports, education, economics, social-science. ƒIs ImportedCandidateStudyCatalog Ingestion status is "imported". ●Expected Distortion TypeCandidateStudyCatalog Hypothesized DistortionType after classification: A, B, C+, C-, D, or unknown.
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Expansion Wave CandidateStudyCatalog ● Domain CandidateStudyCatalog ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog

    Imported expansion-wave-1 economics catalog rows tagged Expected A.

    ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-106
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's expansion wave1 economics expected AD count is the number of the model summary's candidate study catalog that are imported; have a domain of “economics”; have a expected distortion type of “A”; and have a observed distortion type of “D”.

    In English

    Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1", domain is "economics", is imported is true(), expected distortion type is "A", and observed distortion type is "D".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Expansion Wave, "expansion-wave-1", Domain, "economics", Is Imported, TRUE(), Expected Distortion Type, "A", Observed Distortion Type, "D")
    Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
    ●Expansion WaveCandidateStudyCatalog Catalog wave tag; loop-67 expansion rows = expansion-wave-1. ●DomainCandidateStudyCatalog Domain tag: medicine, epidemiology, legal, sports, education, economics, social-science. ƒIs ImportedCandidateStudyCatalog Ingestion status is "imported". ●Expected Distortion TypeCandidateStudyCatalog Hypothesized DistortionType after classification: A, B, C+, C-, D, or unknown. ↗Observed Distortion TypeCandidateStudyCatalog LOOKUP({{LinkedStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Expansion Wave CandidateStudyCatalog ● Domain CandidateStudyCatalog ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog

    Expansion-wave-1 economics Expected-A imports observed as Type D.

    ƒ Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-107
    In RuleSpeak

    The model summary's economics expected a mismatch rate is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the expansion wave1 economics expected a count is 0
    2. the expansion wave1 economics expected AD count divided by the expansion wave1 economics expected a count otherwise
    In English

    If expansion wave1 economics expected a count equals 0 then "", otherwise (expansion wave1 economics expected ad count divided by expansion wave1 economics expected a count).

    As a formula
    = IF(Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected a Count = 0, "", Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD Count / Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected a Count)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒExpansion Wave1 Economics Expected a CountModelSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1", domain is "economics", is imported is true(), and expected distortion type is "A". ƒExpansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD CountModelSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1", domain is "economics", is imported is true(), expected distortion type is "A", and observed distortion type is "D".
    Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Expansion Wave CandidateStudyCatalog ● Domain CandidateStudyCatalog ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog

    Fraction of expansion-wave-1 economics Expected-A imports observed as Type D.

    ƒ Unanimous Sign Flip Count Calculated 7 hops from ground truth integer DR-108
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's unanimous sign flip count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are stratum unanimouses.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose is sign flip is true() and is stratum unanimous is true().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Is Sign Flip, TRUE(), Is Stratum Unanimous, TRUE())
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒIs Stratum UnanimousTreatmentRankings Strata won by a equals stratum count or strata won by b equals stratum count.
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Sign-flip studies with unanimous stratum direction (IsSignFlip AND IsStratumUnanimous).

    Σ Max Study Sweep Corrected Gap Range Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth number DR-109
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's max study sweep corrected gap range is the largest sweep corrected gap range across the sweep study summary related to the model summary.

    In English

    The largest of sweep corrected gap range.

    As a formula
    = MAX(Sweep Corrected Gap Range)
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒSweep Corrected Gap RangeSweepStudySummary Sweep corrected gap max minus sweep corrected gap min.
    Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

    MAX(SweepStudySummary.SweepCorrectedGapRange) — must stay below 0.0001 for CorrectedGap invariance theorem.

    Σ Corrected Gap Invariant Fail Count Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth integer DR-110
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's corrected gap invariant fail count is the number of sweep study summary related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count of SweepStudySummary whose sweep corrected gap range is ">=0.0001".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Sweep Corrected Gap Range, ">=0.0001")
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ƒSweep Corrected Gap RangeSweepStudySummary Sweep corrected gap max minus sweep corrected gap min.
    Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

    Count of SweepStudySummary rows where SweepCorrectedGapRange >= 0.0001.

    Σ False Positive Explained Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-111
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's false positive explained count is the number of the model summary's false positive explaineds.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose is paradox explained is true() and is sign flip is false() plus count of TreatmentRankings whose is paradox explained is true(), is sign flip is true(), and stratum causal role is "<>confounder".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Is Paradox Explained, TRUE(), Is Sign Flip, FALSE()) + COUNTIFS(Is Paradox Explained, TRUE(), Is Sign Flip, TRUE(), Stratum Causal Role, "<>confounder")
    Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
    ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatmentRankings Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0. ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]).
    Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ⇢ Study StratumVariables ● Affects Treatment Assignment StratumVariables ● Affects Outcome StratumVariables ● Causal Role StratumVariables

    IsParadoxExplained=TRUE rows that are not confounder manifest sign-flips — must be 0 for explained↔confounder theorem.

    Σ Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-112
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's unexplained confounder sign flip count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are not paradox explained and have a stratum causal role of “confounder”.

    In English

    Count of TreatmentRankings whose is sign flip is true(), stratum causal role is "confounder", and is paradox explained is false().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Is Sign Flip, TRUE(), Stratum Causal Role, "confounder", Is Paradox Explained, FALSE())
    Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
    ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatmentRankings Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0.
    Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ⇢ Study StratumVariables ● Affects Treatment Assignment StratumVariables ● Affects Outcome StratumVariables ● Causal Role StratumVariables

    Confounder sign-flips with IsParadoxExplained=FALSE — must be 0 for explained↔confounder theorem.

    ƒ Theorem Count Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-113
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's theorem count is the number of the model summary's conclusions that have a status of “witnessed”.

    In English

    Count of Conclusions whose category is "theorem" and status is "witnessed".

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Category, "theorem", Status, "witnessed")
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ●CategoryConclusions domain | instrument | methodology | open-question ●StatusConclusions witnessed | planned | open
    Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Category Conclusions ● Status Conclusions

    Count of witnessed Conclusions with Category=theorem (portfolio rollup for loop-76).

    ƒ Education Latent Fraction Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-114
    In RuleSpeak

    The model summary's education latent fraction is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the model summary has no treatment rankings that have a distortion type of “D”
    2. the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are latent only flips and have a distortion type of “D” divided by the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that have a distortion type of “D” otherwise
    In English

    If count of TreatmentRankings whose study domain is "education" and distortion type is "D" equals 0 then "", otherwise (count of TreatmentRankings whose study domain is "education", distortion type is "D", and is latent only flip is true() divided by count of TreatmentRankings whose study domain is "education" and distortion type is "D").

    As a formula
    = IF(COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "education", Distortion Type, "D") = 0, "", COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "education", Distortion Type, "D", Is Latent Only Flip, TRUE()) / COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "education", Distortion Type, "D"))
    Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
    ƒStudy DomainTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]). ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒIs Latent Only FlipTreatmentRankings If pooled gap crosses zero is "" then "", otherwise pooled gap crosses zero equals true() and is sign flip equals false().
    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
    ● Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

    Fraction of education Type-D studies with LatentFlipPotential=TRUE.

    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one

    1. an empty string if the model summary has no treatment rankings

    ƒ Economics Study Count Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-121
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's economics study count is the number of the model summary's studies that are not synthetics.

    In English

    Count of Studies whose domain is "economics" and is synthetic is false().

    As a formula
    = COUNTIFS(Domain, "economics", Is Synthetic, FALSE())
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ●DomainStudies Research domain: medicine | epidemiology | social-science | education | legal | sports | economics | synthetic. Enables cross-domain SignalPurity stratification. ●Is SyntheticStudies TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.

    ● Domain Studies ● Is Synthetic Studies

    Real economics studies in corpus.

    Σ Mapped Stratum Variable Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-124
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's mapped stratum variable count is the number of stratum variable identity maps related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count(map id).

    As a formula
    = COUNT(Map ID)
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ●Map IDStratumVariableIdentityMaps Unique slug.
    Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
    ƒ Unmapped Stratum Variable Count ModelSummary ƒ Identity Map Coverage Rate ModelSummary
    Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Map ID StratumVariableIdentityMaps

    Stratum variables with an identity map row.

    ƒ Unmapped Stratum Variable Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-125
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's unmapped stratum variable count is computed as the number of stratum variables related to the model summary minus the mapped stratum variable count.

    In English

    Count(stratum variable id) minus mapped stratum variable count.

    As a formula
    = COUNT(Stratum Variable ID) - Mapped Stratum Variable Count
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ●Stratum Variable IDStratumVariables Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-stone-size). ΣMapped Stratum Variable CountModelSummary Count(map id).
    Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Stratum Variable ID StratumVariables ● Map ID StratumVariableIdentityMaps

    Stratum variables lacking identity map (should be 0).

    ƒ Age Identity Manifest Flip Rate Calculated number DR-126
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's age identity manifest flip rate is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-age-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipRate]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("id-age-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipRate]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("id-age-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipRate])

    Manifest flip rate for id-age-composition cluster (loop-81).

    ƒ Age Identity Study Count Calculated integer DR-127
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's age identity study count is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-age-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("id-age-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("id-age-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])

    Real-study count in id-age-composition cluster (loop-81).

    ƒ Age Identity Latent Fraction Among Type D Calculated number DR-128
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's age identity latent fraction among type d is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-age-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[LatentFractionAmongTypeD]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("id-age-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[LatentFractionAmongTypeD]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("id-age-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[LatentFractionAmongTypeD])

    Type-D latent fraction for id-age-composition cluster (loop-81).

    ƒ Severity Identity Latent Fraction Among Type D Calculated number DR-129
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's severity identity latent fraction among type d is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-disease-severity", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[LatentFractionAmongTypeD]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("id-disease-severity", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[LatentFractionAmongTypeD]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("id-disease-severity", IdentityClusterSummaries[ConfounderIdentity], IdentityClusterSummaries[LatentFractionAmongTypeD])

    Type-D latent fraction for id-disease-severity cluster (loop-81).

    ƒ Identity Map Coverage Rate Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-130
    In RuleSpeak

    The model summary's identity map coverage rate is determined by the following priority:

    1. an empty string if the real study count is 0
    2. the mapped stratum variable count divided by the real study count otherwise
    In English

    If real study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (mapped stratum variable count divided by real study count).

    As a formula
    = IF(Real Study Count=0, "", Mapped Stratum Variable Count / Real Study Count)
    Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
    ●Real Study CountModelSummary Count of Studies whose is synthetic is false(). ΣMapped Stratum Variable CountModelSummary Count(map id).
    Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Real Study Count ModelSummary ● Map ID StratumVariableIdentityMaps

    Fraction of real-study StratumVariables with a ConfounderIdentity map (loop-81 customize SQL).

    ● Identity Cluster Witness Note Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    Loop-80 identity layer witness rollup (customize SQL).

    ƒ Severity Medicine Manifest Flip Rate Calculated number DR-131
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's severity medicine manifest flip rate is computed as the value of LOOKUP("cell-id-disease-severity-x-medicine", IdentityDomainCells[CellId], IdentityDomainCells[ManifestFlipRate]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("cell-id-disease-severity-x-medicine", IdentityDomainCells[CellId], IdentityDomainCells[ManifestFlipRate]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("cell-id-disease-severity-x-medicine", IdentityDomainCells[CellId], IdentityDomainCells[ManifestFlipRate])

    Severity identity manifest flip rate in medicine domain.

    ƒ Severity Epi Manifest Flip Count Calculated integer DR-132
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's severity epi manifest flip count is computed as the value of LOOKUP("cell-id-disease-severity-x-epidemiology", IdentityDomainCells[CellId], IdentityDomainCells[ManifestFlipCount]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("cell-id-disease-severity-x-epidemiology", IdentityDomainCells[CellId], IdentityDomainCells[ManifestFlipCount]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("cell-id-disease-severity-x-epidemiology", IdentityDomainCells[CellId], IdentityDomainCells[ManifestFlipCount])

    Severity identity manifest flip count in epidemiology domain.

    Σ Identity Domain Cell Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-133
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's identity domain cell count is the number of identity domain cells related to the model summary.

    In English

    Count(cell id).

    As a formula
    = COUNT(Cell ID)
    Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
    ●Cell IDIdentityDomainCells Unique slug: identity-id + '-x-' + domain slug.
    Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
    ● Cell ID IdentityDomainCells

    Number of identity×domain cells.

    ƒ Selection Frailty Manifest Flip Count Calculated integer DR-134
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's selection frailty manifest flip count is computed as the value of LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipCount]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipCount]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipCount])

    Manifest flip count for selection-frailty identity.

    ƒ Selection Frailty Study Count Calculated integer DR-135
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's selection frailty study count is computed as the value of LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])

    Study count for selection-frailty identity cluster.

    ƒ Collider Identity Manifest Flip Rate Calculated number DR-136
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's collider identity manifest flip rate is computed as the value of LOOKUP("cluster-id-collider-proxy", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipRate]).

    In English

    LOOKUP("cluster-id-collider-proxy", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipRate]).

    As a formula
    = LOOKUP("cluster-id-collider-proxy", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipRate])

    Manifest flip rate for collider-proxy identity.

    ƒ Geographic Type D Fraction Calculated number DR-137
    In RuleSpeak

    A model summary's geographic type d fraction is computed as the value of IF(LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])=0, "", LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[TypeDCount]) / LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])).

    In English

    IF(LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])=0, "", LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[TypeDCount]) / LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])).

    As a formula
    = IF(LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])=0, "", LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[TypeDCount]) / LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]))

    Type-D fraction for geographic-composition identity.

    Stratum Variable

    A stratum variable is identified by its name and is related to a study.

    ⇢ Study Relationship string

    A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

    FK to the parent Study.

    ● Variable Name Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    The name of the stratifying variable (e.g. stone_size, department, age_group).

    ● Causal Role Ground truth string

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.

    ● Affects Treatment Assignment Ground truth boolean

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    TRUE if this variable causally influences which treatment a patient receives. Required for confounding.

    ● Affects Outcome Ground truth boolean

    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

    TRUE if this variable causally influences the outcome independently of treatment. Required for confounding.

    ƒ Is Confounder Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-138
    In RuleSpeak

    A stratum variable is considered a confounder if all of the following:

      In English

      Affects treatment assignment, affects outcome, and causal role is "confounder".

      As a formula
      = AND(Affects Treatment Assignment, Affects Outcome, Causal Role = "confounder")
      Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
      ●Affects Treatment AssignmentStratumVariables TRUE if this variable causally influences which treatment a patient receives. Required for confounding. ●Affects OutcomeStratumVariables TRUE if this variable causally influences the outcome independently of treatment. Required for confounding. ●Causal RoleStratumVariables The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.
      Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
      Σ Confounders in Study TreatmentRankings Is Paradox Explained TreatmentRankings Explained Count ModelSummary Explained Confounder Count ModelSummary Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary False Positive Explained Count ModelSummary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count ModelSummary
      Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ● Affects Treatment Assignment StratumVariables ● Affects Outcome StratumVariables ● Causal Role StratumVariables

      TRUE when AffectsTreatmentAssignment AND AffectsOutcome AND CausalRole = 'confounder'. A confounder is the classic driver of Simpson's Paradox.

      ● Mechanism Note Ground truth string

      Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

      Plain-language description of how this variable creates the confounding: why it affects both assignment and outcome.

      ● Conditioning Risk Ground truth string

      Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

      The causal hazard posed by conditioning on this variable. Values: confounder (safe to condition — removes bias), mediator (conditioning blocks the causal path of interest — harmful), collider (conditioning opens a spurious backdoor — harmful), proxy (conditioning on a noisy proxy of the true confounder — risky), none (no conditioning risk identified). NULL means unclassified.

      ↗ Confounder Identity Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-139
      In RuleSpeak

      A stratum variable's confounder identity is the value of LOOKUP(StratumVariableId, StratumVariableIdentityMaps[StratumVariable], StratumVariableIdentityMaps[ConfounderIdentity]).

      In English

      LOOKUP({{StratumVariableId}}, StratumVariableIdentityMaps[StratumVariable], StratumVariableIdentityMaps[ConfounderIdentity]).

      As a formula
      = LOOKUP(Stratum Variable ID, StratumVariableIdentityMaps[StratumVariable], StratumVariableIdentityMaps[ConfounderIdentity])
      Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
      ●Stratum Variable IDStratumVariables Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-stone-size).
      Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ● Stratum Variable ID StratumVariables

      Canonical cross-study identity via StratumVariableIdentityMaps (loop-80).

      Treatment Ranking

      A treatment ranking is identified by its name and is related to a study.

      ⇢ Study Relationship string

      A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

      FK to the parent Study.

      ● Treatment a Ground truth string

      Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

      Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

      ● Treatment B Ground truth string

      Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

      Label for the second treatment in the comparison.

      Σ Total Cases a Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-140
      In RuleSpeak

      A treatment ranking's total cases a is the total cases across the case cells related to the treatment ranking.

      In English

      Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study">study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

      As a formula
      = SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a)
      Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
      ●CasesCaseCells Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCaseCells FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCaseCells The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
      Consumers 1 direct +70 transitive rules that read this one

      Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings

      Total cases for TreatmentA across all strata in this study.

      Σ Total Successes a Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-141
      In RuleSpeak

      A treatment ranking's total successes a is the total successes across the case cells related to the treatment ranking.

      In English

      Sum of successes across all CaseCells whose study">study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

      As a formula
      = SUMIFS(Successes, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a)

      Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings

      Total successes for TreatmentA across all strata.

      ƒ Pooled Rate a Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-142
      In RuleSpeak

      The treatment ranking's pooled rate a is determined by the following priority:

      1. an empty string if the total cases a is 0
      2. the total successes a divided by the total cases a otherwise
      In English

      If total cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes a divided by total cases a).

      As a formula
      = IF(Total Cases a = 0, "", Total Successes a / Total Cases a)
      Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
      ΣTotal Cases aTreatmentRankings Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ΣTotal Successes aTreatmentRankings Sum of successes across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a.
      Consumers 3 direct +67 transitive rules that read this one

ModelSummary Explained Confounder Count ModelSummary
      Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

      Total successes for TreatmentB across all strata.

      ƒ Pooled Rate B Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-145
      In RuleSpeak

      The treatment ranking's pooled rate b is determined by the following priority:

      1. an empty string if the total cases b is 0
      2. the total successes b divided by the total cases b otherwise
      In English

      If total cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes b divided by total cases b).

      As a formula
      = IF(Total Cases B = 0, "", Total Successes B / Total Cases B)
      Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
      ΣTotal Cases BTreatmentRankings Sum of cases across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment b. ΣTotal Successes BTreatmentRankings Sum of successes across all CaseCells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment b.
      Consumers 3 direct +67 transitive rules that read this one
      ƒ Pooled Winner TreatmentRankings ƒ Pooled Gap TreatmentRankings ƒ Signed Pooled Gap TreatmentRankings Strata Won by Loser TreatmentRankings Corrected Vs Pooled Agreement TreatmentRankings Avg
