ModelSummary
      Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ● Successes CaseCells

      Pooled success rate for TreatmentB: TotalSuccessesB / TotalCasesB.

      ƒ Pooled Winner Calculated 3 hops from ground truth string DR-146
      In RuleSpeak

      The treatment ranking's pooled winner is determined by the following priority:

      1. an empty string if any of these hold:
        • the pooled rate a is blank
        • the pooled rate b is blank
      2. the literal “tie” if the absolute value of the pooled rate a minus the pooled rate b is less than 0.0000001
      3. the treatment a if the pooled rate a is greater than the pooled rate b
      4. the treatment b otherwise
      In English

      If pooled rate a is "" or pooled rate b is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b) is less than 0.0000001 then "tie", otherwise if pooled rate a is greater than pooled rate b then treatment a, otherwise treatment b.

      As a formula
      = IF(OR(Pooled Rate a = "", Pooled Rate B = ""), "", IF(ABS(Pooled Rate a - Pooled Rate B) < 0.0000001, "tie", IF(Pooled Rate a > Pooled Rate B, Treatment a, Treatment B)))
      Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
      ƒPooled Rate aTreatmentRankings If total cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes a divided by total cases a). ƒPooled Rate BTreatmentRankings If total cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes b divided by total cases b). ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment BTreatmentRankings Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
      Consumers 2 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
      ƒ Strata Won by Loser TreatmentRankings ƒ Corrected Vs Pooled Agreement TreatmentRankings Paradox Strength TreatmentRankings Reversal Intensity TreatmentRankings Total Paradox Strength ModelSummary Threshold Margin TreatmentRankings Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary
      Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

      Which treatment wins when strata are ignored. Returns tie when pooled rates are equal within floating-point tolerance.

      Σ Stratum Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-147
      In RuleSpeak

      A treatment ranking's stratum count is the number of stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

      In English

      Count of StratumSummaries whose study">study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

      As a formula
      = COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a)
      Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
      ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratumSummaries Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
      Consumers 5 direct +33 transitive rules that read this one
      ƒ Per Stratum Winner TreatmentRankings ƒ Is Stratum Unanimous TreatmentRankings ƒ Paradox Strength TreatmentRankings ƒ Reversal Intensity TreatmentRankings ƒ Threshold Margin TreatmentRankings Unanimous Sign Flip Count ModelSummary Distortion Type TreatmentRankings Total Paradox Strength ModelSummary Type a Count ModelSummary Type B Count ModelSummary Type D Count ModelSummary C Amplification Count ModelSummary C Compression Count ModelSummary Stable Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary C Plus Avg Distortion ModelSummary C Minus Avg Distortion ModelSummary Type D Avg Distortion ModelSummary Education Latent Fraction ModelSummary Sports Latent Fraction ModelSummary Education Type D Count ModelSummary Education Latent D Count ModelSummary Sports Type D Count ModelSummary Sports Latent D Count ModelSummary Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Corrected Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Latent Flip Potential TreatmentRankings Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary Type a Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
      Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings

      Number of strata in this study. Counted from StratumSummaries filtered to TreatmentA rows (one row per stratum).

      Σ Strata Won by a Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth integer DR-148
      In RuleSpeak

      A treatment ranking's strata won by a is the number of stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

      In English

      Count of StratumSummaries whose study">study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment a.

      As a formula
      = COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a, Stratum Winner, Treatment a)
      Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
      ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratumSummaries Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ƒStratum WinnerStratumSummaries If stratum rate a is greater than stratum rate b then "A", otherwise "B".
      Consumers 3 direct +36 transitive rules that read this one
      ƒ Per Stratum Winner TreatmentRankings ƒ Is Stratum Unanimous TreatmentRankings ƒ Strata Won by Loser TreatmentRankings Unanimous Sign Flip Count ModelSummary Distortion Type TreatmentRankings Paradox Strength TreatmentRankings Reversal Intensity TreatmentRankings Type a Count ModelSummary Type B Count ModelSummary Type D Count ModelSummary C Amplification Count ModelSummary C Compression Count ModelSummary Stable Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary C Plus Avg Distortion ModelSummary C Minus Avg Distortion ModelSummary Type D Avg Distortion ModelSummary Education Latent Fraction ModelSummary Sports Latent Fraction ModelSummary Education Type D Count ModelSummary Education Latent D Count ModelSummary Sports Type D Count ModelSummary Sports Latent D Count ModelSummary Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Corrected Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Latent Flip Potential TreatmentRankings Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Total Paradox Strength ModelSummary Threshold Margin TreatmentRankings Type a Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
      Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells

      Number of strata where A wins: count StratumSummaries rows whose TreatmentLabel=TreatmentA AND StratumWinner=TreatmentA. These are the one-per-stratum sentinel rows.

      Σ Strata Won by B Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth integer DR-149
      In RuleSpeak

      A treatment ranking's strata won by b is the number of stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

      In English

      Count of StratumSummaries whose study">study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment b.

      As a formula
      = COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a, Stratum Winner, Treatment B)
      Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
      ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratumSummaries Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ƒStratum WinnerStratumSummaries If stratum rate a is greater than stratum rate b then "A", otherwise "B". ●Treatment BTreatmentRankings Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
      Consumers 3 direct +36 transitive rules that read this one
      ƒ Per Stratum Winner TreatmentRankings ƒ Is Stratum Unanimous TreatmentRankings ƒ Strata Won by Loser TreatmentRankings Unanimous Sign Flip Count ModelSummary Distortion Type TreatmentRankings Paradox Strength TreatmentRankings Reversal Intensity TreatmentRankings Type a Count ModelSummary Type B Count ModelSummary Type D Count ModelSummary C Amplification Count ModelSummary C Compression Count ModelSummary Stable Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary C Plus Avg Distortion ModelSummary C Minus Avg Distortion ModelSummary Type D Avg Distortion ModelSummary Education Latent Fraction ModelSummary Sports Latent Fraction ModelSummary Education Type D Count ModelSummary Education Latent D Count ModelSummary Sports Type D Count ModelSummary Sports Latent D Count ModelSummary Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Corrected Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Latent Flip Potential TreatmentRankings Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Total Paradox Strength ModelSummary Threshold Margin TreatmentRankings Type a Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
      Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

      Number of strata where B wins: count StratumSummaries rows whose TreatmentLabel=TreatmentA AND StratumWinner=TreatmentB.

      ƒ Per Stratum Winner Calculated 5 hops from ground truth string DR-150
      In RuleSpeak

      The treatment ranking's per stratum winner is determined by the following priority:

      1. the treatment a if the strata won by a is the stratum count
      2. the treatment b if the strata won by b is the stratum count
      3. the literal “none” otherwise
      In English

      If strata won by a equals stratum count then treatment a, otherwise if strata won by b equals stratum count then treatment b, otherwise "none".

      As a formula
      = IF(Strata Won by a = Stratum Count, Treatment a, IF(Strata Won by B = Stratum Count, Treatment B, "none"))
      Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
      ΣStrata Won by aTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment a. ΣStratum CountTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ΣStrata Won by BTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment b. ●Treatment BTreatmentRankings Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
      Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
      ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

      The treatment that wins in every stratum — 'A' if StrataWonByA = StratumCount, 'B' if StrataWonByB = StratumCount, else 'none' (no unanimous per-stratum winner).

      ƒ Is Stratum Unanimous Calculated 5 hops from ground truth boolean DR-151
      In RuleSpeak

      A treatment ranking is considered a stratum unanimous if all of the following:

        In English

        Strata won by a equals stratum count or strata won by b equals stratum count.

        As a formula
        = OR(Strata Won by a = Stratum Count, Strata Won by B = Stratum Count)
        Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
        ΣStrata Won by aTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment a. ΣStratum CountTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ΣStrata Won by BTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment b.
        Consumers 2 direct +29 transitive rules that read this one
        ƒ Unanimous Sign Flip Count ModelSummary ƒ Distortion Type TreatmentRankings Type a Count ModelSummary Type B Count ModelSummary Type D Count ModelSummary C Amplification Count ModelSummary C Compression Count ModelSummary Stable Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary C Plus Avg Distortion ModelSummary C Minus Avg Distortion ModelSummary Type D Avg Distortion ModelSummary Education Latent Fraction ModelSummary Sports Latent Fraction ModelSummary Education Type D Count ModelSummary Education Latent D Count ModelSummary Sports Type D Count ModelSummary Sports Latent D Count ModelSummary Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Corrected Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Latent Flip Potential TreatmentRankings Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Type a Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
        Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
        ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

        TRUE when one treatment wins every stratum (StrataWonByA = StratumCount OR StrataWonByB = StratumCount). Maps A/B policy split: unanimous → stratify-immediately geometry; heterogeneous → investigate-confounder.

        ƒ Is Reversal Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-152
        In RuleSpeak

        The treatment ranking's is reversal is determined by the following priority:

        1. an empty string if the is sign flip is blank
        2. the is sign flip otherwise
        In English

        If is sign flip is "" then "", otherwise is sign flip.

        As a formula
        = IF(Is Sign Flip = "", "", Is Sign Flip)
        Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
        ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
        Consumers 3 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one
        Σ Reversal Count ModelSummary Σ Non Reversal Count ModelSummary ƒ Is Paradox Explained TreatmentRankings Study Count ModelSummary Explained Count ModelSummary Explained Confounder Count ModelSummary Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary False Positive Explained Count ModelSummary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary Type a Fraction ModelSummary
        Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
        ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

        TRUE when the equal-weight pooled signal (WeightedStratumGapSum) and the actual pooled signal (SignedPooledGap) point in opposite directions — allocation has flipped the direction of the aggregate conclusion. This is Simpson's Paradox as a derived fact.

        Σ Confounders in Study Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-153
        In RuleSpeak

        A treatment ranking's confounders in study">study is the number of the treatment ranking's stratum variables that are confounders.

        In English

        Count of StratumVariables whose study">study is study and is confounder is true().

        As a formula
        = COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Is Confounder, TRUE())
        Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
        ⇢StudyStratumVariables FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ƒIs ConfounderStratumVariables Affects treatment assignment, affects outcome, and causal role is "confounder".
        Consumers 1 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
        ƒ Is Paradox Explained TreatmentRankings Explained Count ModelSummary Explained Confounder Count ModelSummary Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary False Positive Explained Count ModelSummary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count ModelSummary
        Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
        ⇢ Study StratumVariables ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Affects Treatment Assignment StratumVariables ● Affects Outcome StratumVariables ● Causal Role StratumVariables

        Count of StratumVariables in this study whose IsConfounder = TRUE. When > 0, the paradox has a causal explanation.

        ƒ Is Paradox Explained Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-154
        In RuleSpeak

        A treatment ranking is considered paradox explained if all of the following:

          In English

          Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0.

          As a formula
          = AND(Is Reversal, Confounders in Study > 0)
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ƒIs ReversalTreatmentRankings If is sign flip is "" then "", otherwise is sign flip. ΣConfounders in StudyTreatmentRankings Count of StratumVariables whose study is study and is confounder is true().
          Consumers 5 direct rules that read this one
          Σ Explained Count ModelSummary Σ Explained Confounder Count ModelSummary Σ Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary Σ False Positive Explained Count ModelSummary Σ Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ⇢ Study StratumVariables ● Affects Treatment Assignment StratumVariables ● Affects Outcome StratumVariables ● Causal Role StratumVariables

          TRUE when IsReversal is present AND at least one confirmed confounder exists in the study.

          ƒ Pooled Gap Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-155
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's pooled gap is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the pooled rate a is blank
          2. the absolute value of the pooled rate a minus the pooled rate b otherwise
          In English

          If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).

          As a formula
          = IF(Pooled Rate a = "", "", ABS(Pooled Rate a - Pooled Rate B))
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ƒPooled Rate aTreatmentRankings If total cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes a divided by total cases a). ƒPooled Rate BTreatmentRankings If total cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes b divided by total cases b).
          Consumers 4 direct +4 transitive rules that read this one
          ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary ƒ Paradox Strength TreatmentRankings ƒ Allocation Fragility TreatmentRankings Total Paradox Strength ModelSummary Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          Absolute difference between the two pooled rates: |PooledRateA - PooledRateB|. The size of the aggregate misleading signal.

          ƒ Strata Won by Loser Calculated 5 hops from ground truth integer DR-156
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's strata won by loser is determined by the following priority:

          1. the strata won by b if the pooled winner is the treatment a
          2. the strata won by a otherwise
          In English

          If pooled winner equals treatment a then strata won by b, otherwise strata won by a.

          As a formula
          = IF(Pooled Winner = Treatment a, Strata Won by B, Strata Won by a)
          Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
          ƒPooled WinnerTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" or pooled rate b is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b) is less than 0.0000001 then "tie", otherwise if pooled rate a is greater than pooled rate b then treatment a, otherwise treatment b. ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ΣStrata Won by BTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment b. ΣStrata Won by aTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment a.
          Consumers 2 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
          ƒ Paradox Strength TreatmentRankings ƒ Reversal Intensity TreatmentRankings Total Paradox Strength ModelSummary Threshold Margin TreatmentRankings Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

          Number of strata won by the pooled loser — the counter-signal. For full reversals this equals StratumCount; for partial paradoxes it is between 0 and StratumCount.

          ƒ Paradox Strength Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-157
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's paradox strength is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the stratum count is 0
          2. the pooled gap times the strata won by loser divided by the stratum count otherwise
          In English

          If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (pooled gap times (strata won by loser divided by stratum count)).

          As a formula
          = IF(Stratum Count = 0, "", Pooled Gap * (Strata Won by Loser / Stratum Count))
          Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
          ΣStratum CountTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒPooled GapTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b). ƒStrata Won by LoserTreatmentRankings If pooled winner equals treatment a then strata won by b, otherwise strata won by a.
          Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
          Σ Total Paradox Strength ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

          Scalar severity of the paradox: PooledGap × (StrataWonByLoser / StratumCount). Zero when no strata go against the pooled winner. Positive for partial paradoxes. Maximum when every stratum contradicts the pooled result.

          Σ Pooled Rate From Weights a Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth number DR-158
          In RuleSpeak

          A treatment ranking's pooled rate from weights a is the total weighted stratum rate across the stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

          In English

          Sum of weighted stratum rate across all StratumSummaries whose study">study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

          As a formula
          = SUMIFS(Weighted Stratum Rate, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a)
          Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
          ƒWeighted Stratum RateStratumSummaries If stratum fraction is "" then "", otherwise (stratum fraction times stratum success rate). ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratumSummaries Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
          Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings

          Pooled rate for TreatmentA reconstructed as a weighted average of stratum rates: SUM(WeightedStratumRate) across all stratum rows for TreatmentA. Must match PooledRateA — this witnesses the mechanism equation: the pooled rate IS a weighted average of stratum rates, weighted by StratumFraction.

          Σ Pooled Rate From Weights B Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth number DR-159
          In RuleSpeak

          A treatment ranking's pooled rate from weights b is the total weighted stratum rate across the stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

          In English

          Sum of weighted stratum rate across all StratumSummaries whose study">study is study and treatment label is treatment b.

          As a formula
          = SUMIFS(Weighted Stratum Rate, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment B)
          Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
          ƒWeighted Stratum RateStratumSummaries If stratum fraction is "" then "", otherwise (stratum fraction times stratum success rate). ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratumSummaries Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment BTreatmentRankings Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
          Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          Pooled rate for TreatmentB reconstructed as a weighted average. Must match PooledRateB. If it does, the mechanism equation is verified: reversal is purely a consequence of differential weighting.

          ƒ Reversal Intensity Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-160
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's reversal intensity is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the stratum count is 0
          2. the strata won by loser divided by the stratum count otherwise
          In English

          If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (strata won by loser divided by stratum count).

          As a formula
          = IF(Stratum Count = 0, "", Strata Won by Loser / Stratum Count)
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ΣStratum CountTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒStrata Won by LoserTreatmentRankings If pooled winner equals treatment a then strata won by b, otherwise strata won by a.
          Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
          ƒ Threshold Margin TreatmentRankings
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

          StrataWonByLoser / StratumCount: the fraction of strata that go against the pooled winner. Zero for no paradox, 1.0 for full reversal, between 0 and 1 for partial. This is the allocation-side measure of how deeply the confounding has penetrated the stratified view.

          ƒ Threshold Margin Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-161
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's threshold margin is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the stratum count is 0
          2. the reversal intensity minus 0.5 otherwise
          In English

          If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (reversal intensity minus 0.5).

          As a formula
          = IF(Stratum Count = 0, "", Reversal Intensity - 0.5)
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ΣStratum CountTreatmentRankings Count of StratumSummaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒReversal IntensityTreatmentRankings If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (strata won by loser divided by stratum count).
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries

          ReversalIntensity minus 0.5: positive when more than half the strata go against the pooled winner (reversal is robust), zero at the tipping point (exactly half), negative when fewer than half oppose the pooled winner (reversal is fragile or absent). A study with ThresholdMargin > 0 is robustly paradoxical; one with ThresholdMargin < 0 has a weak or absent paradox.

          ƒ Signed Pooled Gap Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-162
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's signed pooled gap is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the pooled rate a is blank
          2. the pooled rate a minus the pooled rate b otherwise
          In English

          If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).

          As a formula
          = IF(Pooled Rate a = "", "", Pooled Rate a - Pooled Rate B)
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ƒPooled Rate aTreatmentRankings If total cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes a divided by total cases a). ƒPooled Rate BTreatmentRankings If total cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes b divided by total cases b).
          Consumers 4 direct +56 transitive rules that read this one

ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          Signed difference between pooled rates: PooledRateA minus PooledRateB. Positive = A wins pooled; negative = B wins pooled. Unlike PooledGap (which is absolute), this preserves direction — essential for detecting sign flips between the equal-weight and actual pooled signals.

          Σ Weighted Stratum Gap Sum Aggregation 5 hops from ground truth number DR-163
          In RuleSpeak

          A treatment ranking's weighted stratum gap sum is the total weighted stratum gap across the stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

          In English

          Sum of weighted stratum gap across all StratumSummaries whose study">study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

          As a formula
          = SUMIFS(Weighted Stratum Gap, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a)
          Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
          ƒWeighted Stratum GapStratumSummaries If stratum gap is "" then "", otherwise (stratum gap times stratum fraction). ⇢StudyStratumSummaries FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratumSummaries Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
          Consumers 3 direct +60 transitive rules that read this one

Count ModelSummary Explained Count ModelSummary Explained Confounder Count ModelSummary Type a Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Signal Purity Gap ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings

          Sum of WeightedStratumGap (= StratumGap × StratumFraction) across all StratumSummaries rows where TreatmentLabel = TreatmentA for this study. This is the equal-weight pooled gap — the gap the pooled analysis would produce if allocation were perfectly balanced across strata. When this has the opposite sign to SignedPooledGap, the allocation has not merely compressed the signal: it has flipped it entirely.

          ƒ Is Sign Flip Calculated 6 hops from ground truth boolean DR-164
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's is sign flip is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the weighted stratum gap sum is blank
          2. the signed pooled gap is less than 0 if the weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0
          3. the signed pooled gap is greater than 0 otherwise
          In English

          If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.

          As a formula
          = IF(Weighted Stratum Gap Sum = "", "", IF(Weighted Stratum Gap Sum > 0, Signed Pooled Gap < 0, Signed Pooled Gap > 0))
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ΣWeighted Stratum Gap SumTreatmentRankings Sum of weighted stratum gap across all StratumSummaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒSigned Pooled GapTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).
          Consumers 13 direct +41 transitive rules that read this one

ModelSummary Avg Paradox Strength ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          TRUE when WeightedStratumGapSum and SignedPooledGap have opposite signs. Same condition as IsReversal; retained as the coordinate-level name in the DAG.

          ƒ Allocation Distortion Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-165
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's allocation distortion is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the weighted stratum gap sum is blank
          2. the absolute value of the weighted stratum gap sum minus the signed pooled gap otherwise
          In English

          If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap).

          As a formula
          = IF(Weighted Stratum Gap Sum = "", "", ABS(Weighted Stratum Gap Sum - Signed Pooled Gap))
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ΣWeighted Stratum Gap SumTreatmentRankings Sum of weighted stratum gap across all StratumSummaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒSigned Pooled GapTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).
          Consumers 8 direct +30 transitive rules that read this one
          ƒ Epidemiology Avg Distortion ModelSummary ƒ Education Avg Distortion ModelSummary ƒ C Plus Avg Distortion ModelSummary ƒ C Minus Avg Distortion ModelSummary ƒ Type D Avg Distortion ModelSummary ƒ Distortion Type TreatmentRankings ƒ Signal Purity TreatmentRankings ƒ Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Type a Count ModelSummary Type B Count ModelSummary Type D Count ModelSummary C Amplification Count ModelSummary C Compression Count ModelSummary Stable Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary Education Latent Fraction ModelSummary Sports Latent Fraction ModelSummary Education Type D Count ModelSummary Education Latent D Count ModelSummary Sports Type D Count ModelSummary Sports Latent D Count ModelSummary Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Corrected Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Latent Flip Potential TreatmentRankings Avg Signal Purity ModelSummary Avg Signal Purity Reversal ModelSummary Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal ModelSummary Sign Flip Signal Purity Max ModelSummary Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary Type a Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Signal Purity Gap ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          The magnitude of how far the allocation has bent the pooled signal: |WeightedStratumGapSum − SignedPooledGap|. Zero means the allocation is neutral — the pooled analysis faithfully represents the equal-weight stratum evidence. A large AllocationDistortion means the allocation is doing most of the work: the pooled number is mostly noise from how cases were assigned, not signal about which treatment is better. This measure does NOT require a sign flip to be nonzero — it captures any allocation-induced distortion, not just reversals.

          ƒ Distortion Type Calculated 7 hops from ground truth string DR-166
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's distortion type is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the allocation distortion is blank
          2. the literal “A” if the is stratum unanimous flag is set, otherwise the literal “B” if the is sign flip flag is set
          3. the literal “C+” if all of these hold:
            • it is not the case that the is sign flip flag is set
            • the allocation distortion is greater than 0.01
            • the absolute value of the signed pooled gap is greater than the absolute value of the corrected gap plus 0.001
          4. the literal “C-” if all of these hold:
            • it is not the case that the is sign flip flag is set
            • the allocation distortion is greater than 0.01
            • the absolute value of the signed pooled gap is less than the absolute value of the corrected gap minus 0.001
          5. the literal “D” otherwise
          In English

          If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".

          As a formula
          = IF(Allocation Distortion = "", "", IF(Is Sign Flip, IF(Is Stratum Unanimous, "A", "B"), IF(AND(NOT(Is Sign Flip), Allocation Distortion > 0.01, ABS(Signed Pooled Gap) > ABS(Corrected Gap) + 0.001), "C+", IF(AND(NOT(Is Sign Flip), Allocation Distortion > 0.01, ABS(Signed Pooled Gap) < ABS(Corrected Gap) - 0.001), "C-", "D"))))
          Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
          ƒAllocation DistortionTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒIs Stratum UnanimousTreatmentRankings Strata won by a equals stratum count or strata won by b equals stratum count. ƒSigned Pooled GapTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b). ƒCorrected GapTreatmentRankings Weighted stratum gap sum.
          Consumers 20 direct +9 transitive rules that read this one
          Σ Type a Count ModelSummary Σ Type B Count ModelSummary Σ Type D Count ModelSummary Σ C Amplification Count ModelSummary Σ C Compression Count ModelSummary ƒ Stable Type D Count ModelSummary ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary ƒ C Plus Avg Distortion ModelSummary ƒ C Minus Avg Distortion ModelSummary ƒ Type D Avg Distortion ModelSummary ƒ Education Latent Fraction ModelSummary ƒ Sports Latent Fraction ModelSummary ƒ Education Type D Count ModelSummary ƒ Education Latent D Count ModelSummary ƒ Sports Type D Count ModelSummary ƒ Sports Latent D Count ModelSummary ƒ Policy Implication TreatmentRankings ƒ Corrected Policy Implication TreatmentRankings ƒ Latent Flip Potential TreatmentRankings ƒ Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Type a Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          Geometric classification (five types A/B/C+/C-/D). Sign-flip types: A = unanimous stratum direction (IsStratumUnanimous); B = heterogeneous stratum directions. Non-flip: C+/C-/D by distortion ratio.

          ƒ Policy Implication Calculated 8 hops from ground truth string DR-167
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's policy implication is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the distortion type is blank
          2. the literal “stratify-immediately” if the distortion type is the literal “A”
          3. the literal “investigate-confounder” if the distortion type is the literal “B”
          4. the literal “check-allocation-bias” if any of these hold:
            • the distortion type is the literal “C+”
            • the distortion type is the literal “C-”
          5. the literal “pooled-analysis-trustworthy” otherwise
          In English

          If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise if distortion type is "A" then "stratify-immediately", otherwise if distortion type is "B" then "investigate-confounder", otherwise if distortion type is "C+" or distortion type is "C-" then "check-allocation-bias", otherwise "pooled-analysis-trustworthy".

          As a formula
          = IF(Distortion Type = "", "", IF(Distortion Type = "A", "stratify-immediately", IF(Distortion Type = "B", "investigate-confounder", IF(OR(Distortion Type = "C+", Distortion Type = "C-"), "check-allocation-bias", "pooled-analysis-trustworthy"))))
          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
          ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          The researcher action implied by the geometric classification. Derived from DistortionType: type A (full reversal) → 'stratify-immediately' — the pooled conclusion is directionally wrong; do not act on it without stratifying. Type B (partial sign-flip) → 'investigate-confounder' — the equal-weight signal opposes the pooled signal, but the per-stratum picture is mixed; the causal mechanism needs examination before trusting either estimate. Type C (compression without flip) → 'check-allocation-bias' — the pooled winner is probably correct but the effect size is distorted; report adjusted or standardized margins rather than the raw pooled gap. Type D (neutral) → 'pooled-analysis-trustworthy' — allocation is not materially distorting the signal; the pooled conclusion is safe with respect to this stratification.

          ƒ Corrected Gap Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-168
          In RuleSpeak

          A treatment ranking's corrected gap is computed as the weighted stratum gap sum.

          In English

          Weighted stratum gap sum.

          As a formula
          = Weighted Stratum Gap Sum
          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
          ΣWeighted Stratum Gap SumTreatmentRankings Sum of weighted stratum gap across all StratumSummaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a.
          Consumers 4 direct +35 transitive rules that read this one
          ƒ Distortion Type TreatmentRankings ƒ Corrected Winner TreatmentRankings ƒ Allocation Direction TreatmentRankings ƒ Signal Purity TreatmentRankings Type a Count ModelSummary Type B Count ModelSummary Type D Count ModelSummary C Amplification Count ModelSummary C Compression Count ModelSummary Stable Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary C Plus Avg Distortion ModelSummary C Minus Avg Distortion ModelSummary Type D Avg Distortion ModelSummary Education Latent Fraction ModelSummary Sports Latent Fraction ModelSummary Education Type D Count ModelSummary Education Latent D Count ModelSummary Sports Type D Count ModelSummary Sports Latent D Count ModelSummary Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Corrected Policy Implication TreatmentRankings Latent Flip Potential TreatmentRankings Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Corrected Vs Pooled Agreement TreatmentRankings Avg Signal Purity Reversal ModelSummary Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal ModelSummary Avg Signal Purity ModelSummary Sign Flip Signal Purity Max ModelSummary Type a Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Distortion Only Count ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary Signal Purity Gap ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings

          The allocation-corrected treatment gap: WeightedStratumGapSum, i.e. what SignedPooledGap would be if allocation were equal across strata. Positive means A is favoured in the corrected world; negative means B is favoured.

          ƒ Corrected Winner Calculated 7 hops from ground truth string DR-169
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's corrected winner is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the corrected gap is blank
          2. the literal “tie” if the absolute value of the corrected gap is less than 0.0001
          3. the treatment a if the corrected gap is greater than 0
          4. the treatment b if the corrected gap is less than 0
          5. the literal “tie” otherwise
          In English

          If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of corrected gap is less than 0.0001 then "tie", otherwise if corrected gap is greater than 0 then treatment a, otherwise if corrected gap is less than 0 then treatment b, otherwise "tie".

          As a formula
          = IF(Corrected Gap = "", "", IF(ABS(Corrected Gap) < 0.0001, "tie", IF(Corrected Gap > 0, Treatment a, IF(Corrected Gap < 0, Treatment B, "tie"))))
          Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
          ƒCorrected GapTreatmentRankings Weighted stratum gap sum. ●Treatment aTreatmentRankings Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment BTreatmentRankings Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
          Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
          ƒ Corrected Vs Pooled Agreement TreatmentRankings
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          Which treatment wins once allocation bias is removed. Derived from CorrectedGap: positive → TreatmentA; negative → TreatmentB; near-zero → tie.

          ƒ Corrected Vs Pooled Agreement Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-170
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's corrected vs pooled agreement is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the corrected winner is blank
          2. the corrected winner is the pooled winner otherwise
          In English

          If corrected winner is "" then "", otherwise corrected winner equals pooled winner.

          As a formula
          = IF(Corrected Winner = "", "", Corrected Winner = Pooled Winner)
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ƒCorrected WinnerTreatmentRankings If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of corrected gap is less than 0.0001 then "tie", otherwise if corrected gap is greater than 0 then treatment a, otherwise if corrected gap is less than 0 then treatment b, otherwise "tie". ƒPooled WinnerTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" or pooled rate b is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b) is less than 0.0000001 then "tie", otherwise if pooled rate a is greater than pooled rate b then treatment a, otherwise treatment b.
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          TRUE when the allocation-corrected winner matches the pooled winner. FALSE when removing allocation bias would change which treatment appears to win — the machine-readable definition of a reversal recovery.

          ƒ Corrected Policy Implication Calculated 8 hops from ground truth string DR-171
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's corrected policy implication is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the distortion type is blank
          2. the literal “use-corrected-winner” if the distortion type is the literal “A”
          3. the literal “use-corrected-winner-with-caution” if the distortion type is the literal “B”
          4. the literal “check-allocation-bias” if any of these hold:
            • the distortion type is the literal “C+”
            • the distortion type is the literal “C-”
          5. the literal “pooled-analysis-trustworthy” otherwise
          In English

          If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise if distortion type is "A" then "use-corrected-winner", otherwise if distortion type is "B" then "use-corrected-winner-with-caution", otherwise if distortion type is "C+" or distortion type is "C-" then "check-allocation-bias", otherwise "pooled-analysis-trustworthy".

          As a formula
          = IF(Distortion Type = "", "", IF(Distortion Type = "A", "use-corrected-winner", IF(Distortion Type = "B", "use-corrected-winner-with-caution", IF(OR(Distortion Type = "C+", Distortion Type = "C-"), "check-allocation-bias", "pooled-analysis-trustworthy"))))
          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
          ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          The allocation-aware researcher action: what to do when the corrected verdict is available. Derived from DistortionType and CorrectedWinner together, making the instrument self-consistent with Reversal Recovery (loop-27). Type A (full sign-flip, unanimous per-stratum reversal): CorrectedWinner is the true signal — 'use-corrected-winner'. The pooled analysis was directionally wrong; act on CorrectedWinner, not PooledWinner. Type B (partial sign-flip, non-unanimous): CorrectedWinner points against the pooled signal but strata disagree among themselves — 'use-corrected-winner-with-caution'. Trust the corrected direction but acknowledge residual uncertainty. Type C (compression, no sign flip): PooledWinner is directionally correct; the distortion is in the magnitude, not the direction — 'check-allocation-bias'. Same as PolicyImplication. Type D (neutral): allocation is not materially distorting the signal — 'pooled-analysis-trustworthy'. Same as PolicyImplication. For Types C and D, CorrectedPolicyImplication and PolicyImplication always agree; for Types A and B, CorrectedPolicyImplication supersedes PolicyImplication with the allocation-corrected verdict.

          ƒ Allocation Direction Calculated 7 hops from ground truth string DR-172
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's allocation direction is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the corrected gap is blank
          2. the literal “reversal” if the is sign flip flag is set
          3. the literal “amplification” if the absolute value of the signed pooled gap is greater than the absolute value of the corrected gap plus 0.001
          4. the literal “compression” if the absolute value of the signed pooled gap is less than the absolute value of the corrected gap minus 0.001
          5. the literal “neutral” otherwise
          In English

          If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then "reversal", otherwise if the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "amplification", otherwise if the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "compression", otherwise "neutral".

          As a formula
          = IF(Corrected Gap = "", "", IF(Is Sign Flip, "reversal", IF(ABS(Signed Pooled Gap) > ABS(Corrected Gap) + 0.001, "amplification", IF(ABS(Signed Pooled Gap) < ABS(Corrected Gap) - 0.001, "compression", "neutral"))))
          Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
          ƒCorrected GapTreatmentRankings Weighted stratum gap sum. ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒSigned Pooled GapTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).
          Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
          ● Avg Signal Purity Reversal ModelSummary ● Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal ModelSummary Signal Purity Gap ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          Whether the allocation is reversing, amplifying, or compressing the true treatment signal (CorrectedGap). 'reversal': allocation changed the sign of the pooled gap relative to CorrectedGap — worst case; pooled winner is wrong (types A and B). 'amplification': allocation inflated the pooled gap beyond CorrectedGap in the same direction — pooled winner is correct but effect size is overstated (type C+). 'compression': allocation shrank the pooled gap below CorrectedGap in the same direction — pooled winner is correct but effect size is understated (type C-). 'neutral': allocation had negligible effect on the pooled gap (type D). Policy consequences: reversal → wrong decision; amplification → overconfidence; compression → underconfidence / premature abandonment; neutral → trustworthy.

          ƒ Signal Purity Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-173
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's signal purity is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the corrected gap is blank
          2. 1 if the absolute value of the corrected gap plus the allocation distortion is 0
          3. the absolute value of the corrected gap divided by the absolute value of the corrected gap plus the allocation distortion otherwise
          In English

          If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)).

          As a formula
          = IF(Corrected Gap = "", "", IF(ABS(Corrected Gap) + Allocation Distortion = 0, 1, ABS(Corrected Gap) / (ABS(Corrected Gap) + Allocation Distortion)))
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ƒCorrected GapTreatmentRankings Weighted stratum gap sum. ƒAllocation DistortionTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap).
          Consumers 5 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
          Σ Avg Signal Purity ModelSummary ● Avg Signal Purity Reversal ModelSummary ● Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal ModelSummary ƒ Sign Flip Signal Purity Max ModelSummary ƒ Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Signal Purity Gap ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          The fraction of total observed variation (|CorrectedGap| + AllocationDistortion) that is genuine treatment signal rather than allocation noise. SignalPurity = |CorrectedGap| / (|CorrectedGap| + AllocationDistortion). Range [0,1]: 1.0 = allocation contributed nothing (type D); 0 = corrected gap is zero (pure noise). Critical threshold is 0.5: SignalPurity < 0.5 means allocation noise exceeds true signal. The signal-purity theorem (loop-44): AllocationDirection='reversal' → SignalPurity < 0.5. Equivalently: a sign flip requires the confound to produce more distortion than the treatment produced signal. This is the mechanistic account of why reversals happen: not study-design accidents but algebraic necessities.

          ƒ Pooled Gap Crosses Zero Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-174
          In RuleSpeak

          A treatment ranking is flagged pooled gap crosses zero if the value of LOOKUP(Study, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]).

          In English

          LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]).

          As a formula
          = LOOKUP(Study, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero])
          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
          ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study.
          Consumers 3 direct +13 transitive rules that read this one
          ƒ Cross Zero Count ModelSummary ƒ Latent Flip Potential TreatmentRankings ƒ Is Latent Only Flip TreatmentRankings Latent Type D Count ModelSummary Stable Type D Count ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary Education Latent D Count ModelSummary Sports Latent D Count ModelSummary Confounder Latent Only Count ModelSummary Collider Selection Latent Only Count ModelSummary Education Latent Fraction ModelSummary Sports Latent Fraction ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings

          Lookup: SweepStudySummary.PooledGapCrossesZero via Study. TRUE when reweighting allocation would change the sign of the pooled gap.

          ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Range Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-175
          In RuleSpeak

          A treatment ranking's sweep pooled gap range is computed as the value of LOOKUP(Study, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange]).

          In English

          LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange]).

          As a formula
          = LOOKUP(Study, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange])
          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
          ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study.
          Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
          ƒ Allocation Fragility TreatmentRankings ƒ Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings

          Lookup: SweepStudySummary.SweepPooledGapRange via Study. Width of pooled-gap wander under allocation sweep.

          ƒ Latent Flip Potential Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-176
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's latent flip potential is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if the distortion type is blank
          2. the AND of the distortion type is the literal “D” and the pooled gap crosses zero is the TRUE otherwise
          In English

          If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.

          As a formula
          = IF(Distortion Type = "", "", AND(Distortion Type = "D", Pooled Gap Crosses Zero = TRUE))
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒPooled Gap Crosses ZeroTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]).
          Consumers 6 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
          ƒ Latent Type D Count ModelSummary ƒ Stable Type D Count ModelSummary ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Latent D ModelSummary ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Stable D ModelSummary ƒ Education Latent D Count ModelSummary ƒ Sports Latent D Count ModelSummary Latent Type D Fraction ModelSummary Discovery Witness Note ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          TRUE when DistortionType=D but PooledGapCrossesZero=TRUE — SAFE at observed allocation but flip-capable under counterfactual reweighting.

          ƒ Allocation Fragility Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-177
          In RuleSpeak

          The treatment ranking's allocation fragility is determined by the following priority:

          1. an empty string if any of these hold:
            • the sweep pooled gap range is blank
            • the pooled gap is blank
            • the pooled gap is 0
          2. the sweep pooled gap range divided by the absolute value of the pooled gap otherwise
          In English

          If sweep pooled gap range is "", pooled gap is "", or pooled gap equals 0 then "", otherwise (sweep pooled gap range divided by the absolute value of pooled gap).

          As a formula
          = IF(OR(Sweep Pooled Gap Range = "", Pooled Gap = "", Pooled Gap = 0), "", Sweep Pooled Gap Range / ABS(Pooled Gap))
          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
          ƒSweep Pooled Gap RangeTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange]). ƒPooled GapTreatmentRankings If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).
          Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
          ƒ Is Sweep Fragile TreatmentRankings Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary
          Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
          ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

          SweepPooledGapRange divided by |PooledGap|. High values mean small observed effect but wide counterfactual wander.

          ƒ Is Sweep Fragile Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-178
          In RuleSpeak

          A treatment ranking is considered a sweep fragile if all of the following:

            In English

            Distortion type is "D", signal purity equals 1, allocation distortion equals 0, sweep pooled gap range is greater than 0.3, and allocation fragility is at least 10.

            As a formula
            = AND(Distortion Type = "D", Signal Purity = 1, Allocation Distortion = 0, Sweep Pooled Gap Range > 0.3, Allocation Fragility >= 10)
            Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
            ƒDistortion TypeTreatmentRankings If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒSignal PurityTreatmentRankings If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)). ƒAllocation DistortionTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒSweep Pooled Gap RangeTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange]). ƒAllocation FragilityTreatmentRankings If sweep pooled gap range is "", pooled gap is "", or pooled gap equals 0 then "", otherwise (sweep pooled gap range divided by the absolute value of pooled gap).
            Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
            ƒ Sweep Fragile Count ModelSummary
            Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
            ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

            Type-D study that is pooled-safe but ultra-fragile under allocation reweighting: signal_purity=1, allocation_distortion=0, sweep_pooled_gap_range>0.3, allocation_fragility>=10.

            ƒ Study Domain Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-179

            As a formula
            = LOOKUP(Study, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain])

            ↗ Stratum Causal Role Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-180
