            ƒ Is Latent Only Flip Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-181
            In RuleSpeak

            The treatment ranking's is latent only flip is determined by the following priority:

            1. an empty string if the pooled gap crosses zero is blank
            2. the AND of the pooled gap crosses zero is true and the is sign flip is false otherwise
            In English

            If pooled gap crosses zero is "" then "", otherwise pooled gap crosses zero equals true() and is sign flip equals false().

            As a formula
            = IF(Pooled Gap Crosses Zero = "", "", AND(Pooled Gap Crosses Zero = TRUE(), Is Sign Flip = FALSE()))
            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
            ƒPooled Gap Crosses ZeroTreatmentRankings LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatmentRankings If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
            Consumers 4 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
            ƒ Confounder Latent Only Count ModelSummary ƒ Collider Selection Latent Only Count ModelSummary ƒ Education Latent Fraction ModelSummary ƒ Sports Latent Fraction ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary
            Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
            ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Cases CaseCells ⇢ Study CaseCells ⇢ Study StratumSummaries ● Stratum Label CaseCells ● Stratum Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment Label CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Treatment Label StratumSummaries ● Treatment a TreatmentRankings ● Treatment B TreatmentRankings

            TRUE when allocation sweep crosses zero but observed allocation shows no sign flip — latent-only reversal.

            Σ Confirmed Causal Role Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-182
            In RuleSpeak

            A treatment ranking's confirmed causal role count is the number of the treatment ranking's stratum variables that have a causal role of “confounder”.

            In English

            Count of StratumVariables whose study">study is study and causal role is "confounder".

            As a formula
            = COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Causal Role, "confounder")
            Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
            ⇢StudyStratumVariables FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Causal RoleStratumVariables The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.
            Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
            ƒ Causal Claim Status TreatmentRankings Adjustment Appropriate TreatmentRankings Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary
            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
            ⇢ Study StratumVariables ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Causal Role StratumVariables

            Count of StratumVariables in this study whose CausalRole is "confounder" — includes variables that confound the outcome regardless of whether allocation was balanced.

            Σ Mediator Risk Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-183
            In RuleSpeak

            A treatment ranking's mediator risk count is the number of the treatment ranking's mediator risks.

            In English

            Count of StratumVariables whose study">study is study and conditioning risk is "mediator" plus count of StratumVariables whose study is study and conditioning risk is "collider".

            As a formula
            = COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Conditioning Risk, "mediator") + COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Conditioning Risk, "collider")
            Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
            ⇢StudyStratumVariables FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Conditioning RiskStratumVariables The causal hazard posed by conditioning on this variable. Values: confounder (safe to condition — removes bias), mediator (conditioning blocks the causal path of interest — harmful), collider (conditioning opens a spurious backdoor — harmful), proxy (conditioning on a noisy proxy of the true confounder — risky), none (no conditioning risk identified). NULL means unclassified.
            Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
            ƒ Adjustment Appropriate TreatmentRankings Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary
            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
            ⇢ Study StratumVariables ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Conditioning Risk StratumVariables

            Count of StratumVariables in this study whose ConditioningRisk is mediator or collider — the hazardous conditioning types.

            Σ Contested Stratum Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-184
            In RuleSpeak

            A treatment ranking's contested stratum count is the number of the treatment ranking's stratum variables that have a causal role of “contested”.

            In English

            Count of StratumVariables whose study">study is study and causal role is "contested".

            As a formula
            = COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Causal Role, "contested")
            Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
            ⇢StudyStratumVariables FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Causal RoleStratumVariables The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.
            Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
            ƒ Causal Claim Status TreatmentRankings Adjustment Appropriate TreatmentRankings Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary
            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
            ⇢ Study StratumVariables ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Causal Role StratumVariables

            Count of StratumVariables in this study whose CausalRole is contested — the causal interpretation is disputed.

            Σ Unknown Causal Role Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-185
            In RuleSpeak

            A treatment ranking's unknown causal role count is the number of the treatment ranking's stratum variables that have a causal role of “unknown”.

            In English

            Count of StratumVariables whose study">study is study and causal role is "unknown".

            As a formula
            = COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Causal Role, "unknown")
            Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
            ⇢StudyStratumVariables FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatmentRankings FK to the parent Study. ●Causal RoleStratumVariables The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.
            Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
            ƒ Causal Claim Status TreatmentRankings Adjustment Appropriate TreatmentRankings Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary
            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
            ⇢ Study StratumVariables ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Causal Role StratumVariables

            Count of StratumVariables in this study whose CausalRole is unknown — no causal account has been established.

            ƒ Causal Claim Status Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-186
            In RuleSpeak

            The treatment ranking's causal claim status is determined by the following priority:

            1. the literal “contested” if the contested stratum count is greater than 0
            2. the literal “geometric-only” if the unknown causal role count is greater than 0
            3. the literal “established” if the confirmed causal role count is greater than 0
            4. the literal “geometric-only” otherwise
            In English

            If contested stratum count is greater than 0 then "contested", otherwise if unknown causal role count is greater than 0 then "geometric-only", otherwise if confirmed causal role count is greater than 0 then "established", otherwise "geometric-only".

            As a formula
            = IF(Contested Stratum Count > 0, "contested", IF(Unknown Causal Role Count > 0, "geometric-only", IF(Confirmed Causal Role Count > 0, "established", "geometric-only")))
            Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
            ΣContested Stratum CountTreatmentRankings Count of StratumVariables whose study is study and causal role is "contested". ΣUnknown Causal Role CountTreatmentRankings Count of StratumVariables whose study is study and causal role is "unknown". ΣConfirmed Causal Role CountTreatmentRankings Count of StratumVariables whose study is study and causal role is "confounder".
            Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
            ƒ Adjustment Appropriate TreatmentRankings Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary
            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
            ⇢ Study StratumVariables ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Causal Role StratumVariables

            The epistemic status of the causal interpretation for this study. Values: established (all stratum variables are confirmed confounders with known mechanisms), contested (at least one stratum variable has a disputed causal role — e.g. Berkeley 1973 where department choice may be endogenous), geometric-only (no stratum variable has an established causal role — the distortion is a geometric fact, not a causal claim). Derived from StratumVariables.CausalRole and ConditioningRisk.

            ƒ Adjustment Appropriate Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-187
            In RuleSpeak

            A treatment ranking is flagged adjustment appropriate if all of the following:

              In English

              Mediator risk count equals 0 and causal claim status is "established".

              As a formula
              = AND(Mediator Risk Count = 0, Causal Claim Status = "established")
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ΣMediator Risk CountTreatmentRankings Count of StratumVariables whose study is study and conditioning risk is "mediator" plus count of StratumVariables whose study is study and conditioning risk is "collider". ƒCausal Claim StatusTreatmentRankings If contested stratum count is greater than 0 then "contested", otherwise if unknown causal role count is greater than 0 then "geometric-only", otherwise if confirmed causal role count is greater than 0 then "established", otherwise "geometric-only".
              Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
              Σ Contested or Mediator Explained Count ModelSummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ⇢ Study StratumVariables ⇢ Study TreatmentRankings ● Conditioning Risk StratumVariables ● Causal Role StratumVariables

              TRUE only when it is epistemically safe to act on CorrectedGap as the causal treatment effect estimate: no mediator/collider conditioning risk AND causal claim is established (not contested or geometric-only). FALSE for the birth-weight paradox (mediator), berkeley-1973 (contested), and synthetic/degenerate studies (geometric-only).

              ● Adjustment Rationale Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Human-authored note explaining why AdjustmentAppropriate is TRUE or FALSE for this study. Covers mediator/collider hazards, contested causal roles, and cases where geometric correction is valid but causal interpretation is uncertain.

              Invariant Check

              An invariant check is identified by its name.

              ● Algebraic Statement Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The mathematical assertion in compact symbolic form.

              ● Natural Language Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Plain-English statement of what property must hold and why it is an invariant of the model.

              ● Source Table Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The DAG table whose rows are checked (TreatmentRankings or StratumSummaries).

              ● Filter Expression Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Condition selecting the universe of rows this invariant applies to. Empty means all rows.

              ● Assertion Expression Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The per-row condition that must be TRUE for every row in scope.

              ● SQL Filter Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Optional SQL WHERE clause that selects the universe of rows in scope for this invariant (e.g. distortion_type = 'D'). Empty string means all rows.

              ● SQL Assertion Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The SQL WHERE clause (without WHERE keyword) that counts PASSING rows from vw_treatment_rankings for this invariant. Used by run_invariant_checks() to evaluate each assertion dynamically from the rulebook table.

              ● Pass Count Ground truth integer

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Rows where the invariant holds. Populated by 05b-customize-data.sql from a live query against vw_treatment_rankings.

              ● Fail Count Ground truth integer

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Rows where the invariant is violated. Any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.

              ƒ Universe Count Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-188
              In RuleSpeak

              An invariant check's universe count is computed as the pass count plus the fail count.

              In English

              Pass count plus fail count.

              As a formula
              = Pass Count + Fail Count
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ●Pass CountInvariantChecks Rows where the invariant holds. Populated by 05b-customize-data.sql from a live query against vw_treatment_rankings. ●Fail CountInvariantChecks Rows where the invariant is violated. Any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Pass Count InvariantChecks ● Fail Count InvariantChecks

              Total rows in scope (PassCount + FailCount).

              ƒ Is Green Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-189
              In RuleSpeak

              An invariant check is considered a green if the fail count is 0.

              In English

              Fail count equals 0.

              As a formula
              = Fail Count = 0
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Fail CountInvariantChecks Rows where the invariant is violated. Any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.
              Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
              ƒ Status Label InvariantChecks
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Fail Count InvariantChecks

              TRUE when FailCount = 0.

              ƒ Status Label Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-190
              In RuleSpeak

              The invariant check's status label is determined by the following priority:

              1. the literal “PASS” if the is green flag is set
              2. the literal “FAIL(”, followed by the fail count, followed by the literal “)” otherwise
              In English

              If is green then "PASS", otherwise "FAIL(", fail count, joined with ")".

              As a formula
              = IF(Is Green, "PASS", CONCAT("FAIL(", Fail Count, ")"))
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ƒIs GreenInvariantChecks Fail count equals 0. ●Fail CountInvariantChecks Rows where the invariant is violated. Any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Fail Count InvariantChecks

              PASS or FAIL(n) summary for display.

              ● Severity Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              critical | warning — critical means any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.

              ● Protects Conclusion Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion whose witness depends on this invariant holding. If this invariant fails, that conclusion is undermined.

              ↗ Tradition Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-191
              In RuleSpeak

              An invariant check's tradition name is the value of LOOKUP(TraditionId, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Tradition IDInvariantChecks FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The tradition whose theoretical commitments this algebraic invariant protects — a failed invariant would falsify that tradition's key claim.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Tradition ID InvariantChecks

              Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

              ↗ Tradition Key Claim Summary Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-192
              In RuleSpeak

              An invariant check's tradition key claim summary is the value of LOOKUP(TraditionId, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[KeyClaim]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[KeyClaim]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[KeyClaim])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Tradition IDInvariantChecks FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The tradition whose theoretical commitments this algebraic invariant protects — a failed invariant would falsify that tradition's key claim.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Tradition ID InvariantChecks

              Lookup: ResearchTraditions.KeyClaim via TraditionId — a failed invariant would falsify this tradition's key claim.

              ↗ Protects Conclusion Title Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-193
              In RuleSpeak

              An invariant check's protects conclusion title is the value of LOOKUP(ProtectsConclusion, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Title]).

              LOOKUP({{ProtectsConclusion}}, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Title]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Protects Conclusion, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Title])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Protects ConclusionInvariantChecks FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion whose witness depends on this invariant holding. If this invariant fails, that conclusion is undermined.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Protects Conclusion InvariantChecks

              Lookup: Conclusions.Title via ProtectsConclusion.

              ↗ Protects Conclusion Category Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-194
              In RuleSpeak

              An invariant check's protects conclusion category is the value of LOOKUP(ProtectsConclusion, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Category]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{ProtectsConclusion}}, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Category]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Protects Conclusion, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Category])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Protects ConclusionInvariantChecks FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion whose witness depends on this invariant holding. If this invariant fails, that conclusion is undermined.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Protects Conclusion InvariantChecks

              Lookup: Conclusions.Category via ProtectsConclusion.

              Methodology

              A methodology is identified by its name.

              ● Phase Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Broad phase: axiom | decomposition | derivation | hydration | validation | taxonomy | conclusion

              ● Title Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Short title of the methodological principle or step.

              ● Statement Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              One-to-three sentence statement of the principle, precise enough to be applied or falsified.

              ● Why It Matters Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Why this step or principle is load-bearing for this model — what breaks if you skip it.

              ● Illustrated by Loop Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              LoopId(s) where this principle was concretely demonstrated (comma-separated).

              ● Pioneering Researcher Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. The researcher most associated with developing or popularising this methodological principle in the Simpson's Paradox context.

              ↗ Tradition Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-195
              In RuleSpeak

              A methodology's tradition name is the value of LOOKUP(TraditionId, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Tradition IDMethodology FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition that introduced or most relies on this methodological approach.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Tradition ID Methodology

              Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

              ↗ Tradition Primary Venue Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-196
              In RuleSpeak

              In English

              LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[PrimaryVenue]).

              As a formula

              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads

              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on

              In English

              LOOKUP({{PioneeringResearcher}}, Researchers[ResearcherId], Researchers[Name]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Pioneering Researcher, Researchers[ResearcherId], Researchers[Name])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Pioneering ResearcherMethodology FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. The researcher most associated with developing or popularising this methodological principle in the Simpson's Paradox context.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Pioneering Researcher Methodology

              Lookup: Researchers.Name via PioneeringResearcher.

              ↗ Pioneering Researcher Affiliation Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-198
              In RuleSpeak

              A methodology's pioneering researcher affiliation is the value of LOOKUP(PioneeringResearcher, Researchers[ResearcherId], Researchers[Affiliation]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{PioneeringResearcher}}, Researchers[ResearcherId], Researchers[Affiliation]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Pioneering Researcher, Researchers[ResearcherId], Researchers[Affiliation])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Pioneering ResearcherMethodology FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. The researcher most associated with developing or popularising this methodological principle in the Simpson's Paradox context.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Pioneering Researcher Methodology

              Lookup: Researchers.Affiliation via PioneeringResearcher.

              Σ Loop Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-199
              In RuleSpeak

              A methodology's loop count is the number of loops related to the methodology.

              In English

              Count of Loops whose tradition id is tradition id.

              As a formula
              = COUNTIFS(Tradition ID, Tradition ID)
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ●Tradition IDLoops FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose conceptual vocabulary this loop primarily draws on — tracks which tradition has driven each stage of the instrument's development. ●Tradition IDMethodology FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition that introduced or most relies on this methodological approach.
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Tradition ID Loops ● Tradition ID Methodology

              Count of Loops whose TraditionId matches this methodology entry's TraditionId — how many build iterations this methodological tradition has driven.

              Conclusion

              A conclusion is identified by its name.

              ● Category Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              domain | instrument | methodology | open-question

              ● Status Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              witnessed | planned | open

              ● Title Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              One-line statement of the conclusion.

              ● Evidence Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The specific DB query result, loop outcome, or argument that establishes or will establish this conclusion.

              ● Witnessed in Loop Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              LoopId where this conclusion was first witnessed (if status=witnessed).

              ● Target Loop Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              LoopId that is designed to witness this conclusion (if status=planned).

              ● Challenges Researcher Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. A researcher whose position this conclusion refutes or qualifies (e.g. conc-03 refutes Blyth's unanimity criterion as the primary definition).

              ↗ Tradition Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-200
              In RuleSpeak

              A conclusion's tradition name is the value of LOOKUP(TraditionId, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Tradition IDConclusions FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The tradition whose theoretical framework this conclusion primarily vindicates, refines, or challenges.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Tradition ID Conclusions

              Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

              ↗ Tradition Key Claim Summary Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-201

              In English

              LOOKUP({{ResearcherId}}, Researchers[ResearcherId], Researchers[Name]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Researcher ID, Researchers[ResearcherId], Researchers[Name])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads

              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on

              Σ Invariant Protecting Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-210
              In RuleSpeak

              A conclusion's invariant protecting count is the number of invariant checks related to the conclusion.

              In English

              Count of InvariantChecks whose protects conclusion is conclusion id.

              As a formula
              = COUNTIFS(Protects Conclusion, Conclusion ID)
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ●Protects ConclusionInvariantChecks FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion whose witness depends on this invariant holding. If this invariant fails, that conclusion is undermined. ●Conclusion IDConclusions Unique slug (e.g. conc-01-paradox-is-derived).
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Protects Conclusion InvariantChecks ● Conclusion ID Conclusions

              Count of InvariantChecks whose ProtectsConclusion FK points to this conclusion — how many algebraic invariants stand behind this finding.

              UI Screen

              A UI screen is identified by its name.

              ● Title Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Human-readable screen title shown in the UI.

              ● Route Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              URL path pattern (e.g. /, /study/:studyId, /compare).

              ● Primary Entity Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The main rulebook entity this screen reads from (the vw_* view name without prefix).

              ● Purpose Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              One sentence: what a user can learn or do on this screen.

              ● Key Fields Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Comma-separated list of the most important fields displayed on this screen.

              ● User Action Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Primary user action available on this screen (e.g. select a study, drill into a stratum).

              ● Primary Conclusion Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion this screen most directly visualises or communicates to the user.

              ↗ Tradition Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-211
              In RuleSpeak

              = LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name])

              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on

              Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on

              UI Component

              A UI component is identified by its name.

              ● Component Name Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Code-level component name (PascalCase, e.g. DistortionBadge).

              ● Used on Screens Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Comma-separated ScreenIds where this component appears.

              ● Primary Entity Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The rulebook entity this component reads (the vw_* view).

              ● Description Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              What this component renders and what decisions a developer must make to implement it.

              ● Required Fields Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Comma-separated rulebook fields this component must receive as props.

              ● Visual Spec Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Plain-English description of the visual output: shape, color coding, interactivity.

              Instrument Spec

              An instrument spec is identified by its name.

              ● Component Type Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              input | derived | classification | adapter-requirement

              ● Field Name Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The field or variable name in the DAG.

              ● Data Type Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              integer | decimal | boolean | string | categorical

              ● Source Table Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The DAG table where this field is computed or stored.

              ● Formula Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              The ERB formula or derivation rule for this component.

              ● Natural Language Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Plain-English statement of what this component represents or requires.

              ● Adapter Note Ground truth string

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              What a researcher must supply or verify to satisfy this component when hydrating a new study.

              Allocation Sweep

              An allocation sweep is identified by its name.

              ● Alloc Fraction a Ground truth number

              Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

              Fraction of the sweep-stratum cases assigned to treatment A. Ranges from 0.05 to 0.95. The sweep stratum is the one whose allocation was most confounded in the original study.

              ƒ Is Original Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-215
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep is considered an original if the value of ABS(AllocFractionA - LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[OriginalAllocFractionA])) < 0.03.

              In English

              ABS({{AllocFractionA}} - LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[OriginalAllocFractionA])) < 0.03.

              As a formula
              = ABS(Alloc Fraction a - LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[OriginalAllocFractionA])) < 0.03
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ●Alloc Fraction aAllocationSweep Fraction of the sweep-stratum cases assigned to treatment A. Ranges from 0.05 to 0.95. The sweep stratum is the one whose allocation was most confounded in the original study. ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep ● Study ID AllocationSweep

              TRUE for the row closest to the actual original study allocation.

              ƒ N Sweep Stratum Total Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-216
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's n sweep stratum total is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
              Consumers 2 direct +9 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ N Sweep a AllocationSweep ƒ N Sweep B AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Rate a AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Rate B AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep

              Total cases in the sweep stratum (the stratum whose allocation is being varied). Fixed per study.

              ƒ N Sweep a Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-217
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's n sweep a is computed as the n sweep stratum total times the alloc fraction a rounded to the nearest whole number.

              In English

              (n sweep stratum total times alloc fraction a) rounded to the nearest whole number.

              As a formula
              = ROUND(N Sweep Stratum Total * Alloc Fraction a)
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ƒN Sweep Stratum TotalAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal]). ●Alloc Fraction aAllocationSweep Fraction of the sweep-stratum cases assigned to treatment A. Ranges from 0.05 to 0.95. The sweep stratum is the one whose allocation was most confounded in the original study.
              Consumers 2 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ N Sweep B AllocationSweep ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate a AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Rate B AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep

              Cases in the sweep stratum assigned to treatment A = ROUND(NSweepStratumTotal * AllocFractionA).

              ƒ N Sweep B Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-218
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's n sweep b is computed as the n sweep stratum total minus the n sweep a.

              In English

              N sweep stratum total minus n sweep a.

              As a formula
              = N Sweep Stratum Total - N Sweep a
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ƒN Sweep Stratum TotalAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal]). ƒN Sweep aAllocationSweep (n sweep stratum total times alloc fraction a) rounded to the nearest whole number.
              Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep

              Cases in the sweep stratum assigned to treatment B = NSweepStratumTotal - NSweepA.

              ƒ N Fixed a Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-219
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's n fixed a is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedA]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedA]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedA])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
              Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate a AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep

              Total A-treatment cases in all NON-swept strata (held fixed across sweep rows). For kidney-1986 = 87 (small-stratum A). For berkeley-1973 = 1302 (depts B+C+D male). For compressed-synthetic = 80 (easy-stratum A). For balanced-synthetic = 100 (hard-stratum A).

              ƒ N Fixed B Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-220
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's n fixed b is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedB]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedB]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedB])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
              Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep

              Total B-treatment cases in all NON-swept strata (held fixed across sweep rows). For kidney-1986 = 270 (small-stratum B). For berkeley-1973 = 993 (depts B+C+D female). For compressed-synthetic = 120 (easy-stratum B). For balanced-synthetic = 100 (hard-stratum B).

              ƒ Sweep Rate a Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-221
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's sweep rate a is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateA]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateA]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateA])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads

              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep

              Per-cell success rate for treatment A in the sweep stratum. Fixed per study.

              ƒ Sweep Rate B Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-222
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's sweep rate b is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateB]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateB]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateB])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
              Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep

              Per-cell success rate for treatment B in the sweep stratum. Fixed per study.

              ƒ Fixed Rate a Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-223
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's fixed rate a is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateA]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateA]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateA])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
              Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate a AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep

              Weighted success rate for treatment A across all fixed (non-swept) strata. Constant per study.

              ƒ Fixed Rate B Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-224
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's fixed rate b is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateB]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateB]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateB])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
              Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep

              Weighted success rate for treatment B across all fixed (non-swept) strata. Constant per study.

              ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate a Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-225
              In RuleSpeak

              The allocation sweep's sweep pooled rate a is determined by the following priority:

              1. an empty string if the n sweep a plus the n fixed a is 0
              2. the n sweep a times the sweep rate a plus the n fixed a times the fixed rate a divided by the n sweep a plus the n fixed a otherwise
              In English

              If (n sweep a plus n fixed a) equals 0 then "", otherwise (((n sweep a times sweep rate a) plus (n fixed a times fixed rate a)) divided by (n sweep a plus n fixed a)).

              As a formula
              = IF(N Sweep a + N Fixed a = 0, "", (N Sweep a * Sweep Rate a + N Fixed a * Fixed Rate a) / (N Sweep a + N Fixed a))
              Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
              ƒN Sweep aAllocationSweep (n sweep stratum total times alloc fraction a) rounded to the nearest whole number. ƒN Fixed aAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedA]). ƒSweep Rate aAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateA]). ƒFixed Rate aAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateA]).
              Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep

              Pooled success rate for treatment A = (NSweepA * SweepRateA + NFixedA * FixedRateA) / (NSweepA + NFixedA). Changes as AllocFractionA varies.

              ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-226
              In RuleSpeak

              The allocation sweep's sweep pooled rate b is determined by the following priority:

              1. an empty string if the n sweep b plus the n fixed b is 0
              2. the n sweep b times the sweep rate b plus the n fixed b times the fixed rate b divided by the n sweep b plus the n fixed b otherwise
              In English

              If (n sweep b plus n fixed b) equals 0 then "", otherwise (((n sweep b times sweep rate b) plus (n fixed b times fixed rate b)) divided by (n sweep b plus n fixed b)).

              As a formula
              = IF(N Sweep B + N Fixed B = 0, "", (N Sweep B * Sweep Rate B + N Fixed B * Fixed Rate B) / (N Sweep B + N Fixed B))
              Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
              ƒN Sweep BAllocationSweep N sweep stratum total minus n sweep a. ƒN Fixed BAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedB]). ƒSweep Rate BAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateB]). ƒFixed Rate BAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateB]).
              Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep

              Pooled success rate for treatment B = (NSweepB * SweepRateB + NFixedB * FixedRateB) / (NSweepB + NFixedB). Changes as AllocFractionA varies.

              ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Calculated 5 hops from ground truth number DR-227
              In RuleSpeak

              The allocation sweep's sweep pooled gap is determined by the following priority:

              1. an empty string if the sweep pooled rate a is blank
              2. the sweep pooled rate a minus the sweep pooled rate b otherwise
              In English

              If sweep pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (sweep pooled rate a minus sweep pooled rate b).

              As a formula
              = IF(Sweep Pooled Rate a = "", "", Sweep Pooled Rate a - Sweep Pooled Rate B)
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ƒSweep Pooled Rate aAllocationSweep If (n sweep a plus n fixed a) equals 0 then "", otherwise (((n sweep a times sweep rate a) plus (n fixed a times fixed rate a)) divided by (n sweep a plus n fixed a)). ƒSweep Pooled Rate BAllocationSweep If (n sweep b plus n fixed b) equals 0 then "", otherwise (((n sweep b times sweep rate b) plus (n fixed b times fixed rate b)) divided by (n sweep b plus n fixed b)).
              Consumers 4 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
              Σ Sweep Pooled Gap Range ModelSummary ƒ Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Max SweepStudySummary ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep

              SweepPooledRateA minus SweepPooledRateB. Wanders as AllocFractionA varies because pooled rates mix stratum sizes unequally. Crosses zero at some allocation (the paradox reversal point).

              ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-228
              In RuleSpeak

              An allocation sweep's sweep corrected gap is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
              Consumers 6 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
              Σ Sweep Corrected Gap Max ModelSummary Σ Sweep Corrected Gap Min ModelSummary ƒ Allocation Distortion Witness AllocationSweep ƒ Corrected Gap Constant SweepStudySummary ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Max SweepStudySummary ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Min SweepStudySummary Sweep Corrected Gap Range ModelSummary Sweep Corrected Gap Range SweepStudySummary Max Study Sweep Corrected Gap Range ModelSummary Corrected Gap Invariant Fail Count ModelSummary Invariant Witness SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep

              Allocation-free signal: sum over strata of (n_s/N) * (rate_As - rate_Bs). Constant across the sweep by construction — the allocation variable does not appear. This is the conserved quantity under re-stratification.

              ƒ Allocation Distortion Witness Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-229
              In RuleSpeak

              The allocation sweep's allocation distortion witness is determined by the following priority:

              1. an empty string if the sweep pooled gap is blank
              2. the sweep pooled gap minus the sweep corrected gap otherwise
              In English

              If sweep pooled gap is "" then "", otherwise (sweep pooled gap minus sweep corrected gap).

              As a formula
              = IF(Sweep Pooled Gap = "", "", Sweep Pooled Gap - Sweep Corrected Gap)
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ƒSweep Pooled GapAllocationSweep If sweep pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (sweep pooled rate a minus sweep pooled rate b). ƒSweep Corrected GapAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]).
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep

              SweepPooledGap minus SweepCorrectedGap. The allocation distortion: how much the pooled signal has been inflated or deflated by differential stratum allocation. Zero only when allocation is balanced.

              Sweep Study Summary

              A sweep study summary is identified by its name.

              ƒ Distortion Type Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-230
              In RuleSpeak

              A sweep study summary's distortion type label is computed as the value of LOOKUP(SweepStudyId, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

              In English

              LOOKUP({{SweepStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

              As a formula
              = LOOKUP(Sweep Study ID, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType])
              Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
              ●Sweep Study IDSweepStudySummary FK to Studies.StudyId. One row per study in the AllocationSweep.
              Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

              DistortionType for this study (A, B, C, or D) — provides context for what the sweep should show.

              ƒ Corrected Gap Constant Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-231
              In RuleSpeak

              A sweep study summary's corrected gap constant is the smallest sweep corrected gap across the allocation sweep related to the sweep study summary.

              In English

              Smallest sweep corrected gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is sweep study id.

              As a formula
              = MINIFS(Sweep Corrected Gap, Study ID, Sweep Study ID)
              Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
              ƒSweep Corrected GapAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]). ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus. ●Sweep Study IDSweepStudySummary FK to Studies.StudyId. One row per study in the AllocationSweep.
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

              The allocation-free corrected gap for this study, computed analytically.

              ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Max Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-232
              In RuleSpeak

              A sweep study summary's sweep corrected gap max is the largest sweep corrected gap across the allocation sweep related to the sweep study summary.

              In English

              Largest sweep corrected gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is sweep study id.

              As a formula
              = MAXIFS(Sweep Corrected Gap, Study ID, Sweep Study ID)
              Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
              ƒSweep Corrected GapAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]). ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus. ●Sweep Study IDSweepStudySummary FK to Studies.StudyId. One row per study in the AllocationSweep.
              Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Range SweepStudySummary Max Study Sweep Corrected Gap Range ModelSummary Corrected Gap Invariant Fail Count ModelSummary Invariant Witness SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

              Maximum SweepCorrectedGap across all allocation fractions for this study.

              ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Min Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-233
              In RuleSpeak

              A sweep study summary's sweep corrected gap min is the smallest sweep corrected gap across the allocation sweep related to the sweep study summary.

              In English

              Smallest sweep corrected gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is sweep study id.

              As a formula
              = MINIFS(Sweep Corrected Gap, Study ID, Sweep Study ID)
              Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
              ƒSweep Corrected GapAllocationSweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]). ●Study IDAllocationSweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus. ●Sweep Study IDSweepStudySummary FK to Studies.StudyId. One row per study in the AllocationSweep.
              Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
              ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Range SweepStudySummary Max Study Sweep Corrected Gap Range ModelSummary Corrected Gap Invariant Fail Count ModelSummary Invariant Witness SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

              Minimum SweepCorrectedGap across all allocation fractions for this study.

              ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Range Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-234
              In RuleSpeak

              A sweep study summary's sweep corrected gap range is computed as the sweep corrected gap max minus the sweep corrected gap min.

              In English

              Sweep corrected gap max minus sweep corrected gap min.

              As a formula
              = Sweep Corrected Gap Max - Sweep Corrected Gap Min
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ƒSweep Corrected Gap MaxSweepStudySummary Largest sweep corrected gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is sweep study id. ƒSweep Corrected Gap MinSweepStudySummary Smallest sweep corrected gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is sweep study id.
              Consumers 3 direct rules that read this one
              Σ Max Study Sweep Corrected Gap Range ModelSummary Σ Corrected Gap Invariant Fail Count ModelSummary ƒ Invariant Witness SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

              Range of SweepCorrectedGap across the sweep = Max - Min. Should be effectively zero (< 0.0001) for all studies if CorrectedGap is truly allocation-free.

              ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Max Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-235
              In RuleSpeak

              As a formula
              = MAXIFS(Sweep Pooled Gap, Study ID, Sweep Study ID)

One row per study in the AllocationSweep.

              ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Range SweepStudySummary ƒ Pooled Gap Crosses Zero SweepStudySummary
              Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

              Minimum SweepPooledGap across all allocation fractions for this study.

              ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Range Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-237
              In RuleSpeak

              A sweep study summary's sweep pooled gap range is computed as the sweep pooled gap max minus the sweep pooled gap min.

              In English

              Sweep pooled gap max minus sweep pooled gap min.

              As a formula
              = Sweep Pooled Gap Max - Sweep Pooled Gap Min
              Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
              ƒSweep Pooled Gap MaxSweepStudySummary Largest sweep pooled gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is sweep study id. ƒSweep Pooled Gap MinSweepStudySummary Smallest sweep pooled gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is sweep study id.
              Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
              ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

              Range of SweepPooledGap across the sweep = Max - Min. Should be large for Type A/B studies (pooled gap wanders) and smaller for Type D (balanced allocation = less wandering).

              ƒ Pooled Gap Crosses Zero Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-238
              In RuleSpeak

              A sweep study summary is flagged pooled gap crosses zero if all of the following:

                In English

                Sweep pooled gap min is less than 0 and sweep pooled gap max is greater than 0.

                As a formula
                = AND(Sweep Pooled Gap Min < 0, Sweep Pooled Gap Max > 0)
                Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                ƒSweep Pooled Gap MinSweepStudySummary Smallest sweep pooled gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is sweep study id. ƒSweep Pooled Gap MaxSweepStudySummary Largest sweep pooled gap across all AllocationSweep whose study id is sweep study id.
                Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Alloc Fraction a AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

                TRUE when SweepPooledGapMin < 0 and SweepPooledGapMax > 0 — the pooled gap changes sign somewhere in the sweep, meaning the paradox can be created or destroyed by pure allocation.

                ƒ Invariant Witness Calculated 4 hops from ground truth string DR-239
                In RuleSpeak

                The sweep study summary's invariant witness is determined by the following priority:

                1. the literal “PASS: CorrectedGap invariant across allocation sweep” if the sweep corrected gap range is less than 0.0001
                2. the literal “FAIL: CorrectedGap varies — formula error” otherwise
                In English

                If sweep corrected gap range is less than 0.0001 then "PASS: CorrectedGap invariant across allocation sweep", otherwise "FAIL: CorrectedGap varies — formula error".

                As a formula
                = IF(Sweep Corrected Gap Range < 0.0001, "PASS: CorrectedGap invariant across allocation sweep", "FAIL: CorrectedGap varies — formula error")
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ƒSweep Corrected Gap RangeSweepStudySummary Sweep corrected gap max minus sweep corrected gap min.
                Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Study ID AllocationSweep ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

                Summary witness statement: PASS if CorrectedGap is invariant (range < 0.0001), FAIL otherwise.

                ƒ Sweep Stratum Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-240
                In RuleSpeak

                A sweep study summary's sweep stratum label is computed as the value of LOOKUP(SweepStudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepStratumLabel]).

                In English

                LOOKUP({{SweepStudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepStratumLabel]).

                As a formula
                = LOOKUP(Sweep Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepStratumLabel])
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ●Sweep Study IDSweepStudySummary FK to Studies.StudyId. One row per study in the AllocationSweep.
                Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Sweep Study ID SweepStudySummary

                Which stratum is being swept for this study.

                Research Tradition

                A research tradition is identified by its name.

                ● Era Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                Approximate active period, e.g. "1899–1972".

                ● Core Concern Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                The central question this tradition asks about the paradox.

                ● Primary Venue Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                Where this tradition publishes (journal, conference, book series).

                ● Key Claim Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                The tradition's signature thesis or resolution strategy.

                ● Open Question Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                What this tradition leaves unresolved or contested.

                ● Verified by Deep Research Ground truth boolean

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                True if the 2026-06-28 adversarial deep-research pass confirmed the tradition at high confidence.

                ● Illustrated by Study Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                FK → Studies.StudyId. The study in this model that most vividly illustrates this tradition's characteristic concern or resolution strategy.

                ● Supporting Conclusion Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion in this model that most directly validates or advances this tradition's central claim about Simpson's Paradox.

                ↗ Illustrated by Study Title Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-241
                In RuleSpeak

                A research tradition's illustrated by study title is the value of LOOKUP(IllustratedByStudy, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Title]).

                In English

                LOOKUP({{IllustratedByStudy}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Title]).

                As a formula
                = LOOKUP(Illustrated by Study, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Title])
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ●Illustrated by StudyResearchTraditions FK → Studies.StudyId. The study in this model that most vividly illustrates this tradition's characteristic concern or resolution strategy.
                Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Illustrated by Study ResearchTraditions

                Lookup: Studies.Title via IllustratedByStudy.

                ↗ Illustrated by Study Source Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-242

                As a formula
                = LOOKUP(Illustrated by Study, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Source])
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ●Illustrated by StudyResearchTraditions FK → Studies.StudyId. The study in this model that most vividly illustrates this tradition's characteristic concern or resolution strategy.
                Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on

                Lookup: Studies.Source via IllustratedByStudy — the citation for the study that best illustrates this tradition.

                ↗ Illustrated by Study Distortion Type Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-243
                In RuleSpeak

                A research tradition's illustrated by study distortion type is the value of LOOKUP(IllustratedByStudy, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

                In English

                LOOKUP({{IllustratedByStudy}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

                As a formula

                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
