                Σ Researcher Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-246
                In RuleSpeak

                A research tradition's researcher count is the number of researchers related to the research tradition.

                In English

                Count of Researchers whose tradition id is tradition id.

                As a formula
                = COUNTIFS(Tradition ID, Tradition ID)
                Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                ●Tradition IDResearchers FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId ●Tradition IDResearchTraditions Slug identifier, e.g. tradition-historical.
                Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Tradition ID Researchers ● Tradition ID ResearchTraditions

                Count of researchers in this tradition.

                Σ Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-247
                In RuleSpeak

                A research tradition's study count is the number of studies related to the research tradition.

                In English

                Count of Studies whose tradition id is tradition id.

                As a formula
                = COUNTIFS(Tradition ID, Tradition ID)
                Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                ●Tradition IDStudies FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose framing best characterizes this study's paradox type. Synthetic studies inherit the tradition of their conceptual origin. ●Tradition IDResearchTraditions Slug identifier, e.g. tradition-historical.
                Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Tradition ID Studies ● Tradition ID ResearchTraditions

                Count of studies in this model whose TraditionId matches this tradition.

                Σ Loop Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-248
                In RuleSpeak

                A research tradition's loop count is the number of loops related to the research tradition.

                In English

                Count of Loops whose tradition id is tradition id.

                As a formula
                = COUNTIFS(Tradition ID, Tradition ID)
                Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                ●Tradition IDLoops FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose conceptual vocabulary this loop primarily draws on — tracks which tradition has driven each stage of the instrument's development. ●Tradition IDResearchTraditions Slug identifier, e.g. tradition-historical.
                Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Tradition ID Loops ● Tradition ID ResearchTraditions

                Count of Leopold Loops that drew primarily on this tradition's conceptual vocabulary.

                Researcher

                A researcher is identified by its name.

                ● Active Period Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                Approximate years of relevant work, e.g. "1899".

                ● Affiliation Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                Primary institutional affiliation for the relevant work.

                ● Key Contribution Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                One-sentence description of their specific contribution to the paradox literature.

                ● Signature Work Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                The canonical paper or book most associated with their contribution.

                ● Relation to Hannan Pearl Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                How this researcher's line of work relates to or differs from Hannan (aggregation bias) and Pearl (DAG/do-calculus).

                ● Verified by Deep Research Ground truth boolean

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                True if the 2026-06-28 adversarial deep-research pass confirmed this person at high confidence.

                ● Confidence Level Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                high | medium | unverified — matches deep-research vote outcome.

                ● Illustrates Conclusion Ground truth string

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion in this model that this researcher's work directly supports, challenges, or frames.

                Synthetic Phase

                A synthetic phase is identified by its name.

                ● Param Stratum Fraction Ground truth number

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                w — fraction of total cases in the hard (stratum-1) subgroup. Range 0.30–0.75 in this grid.

                ● Param Stratum Gap1 Ground truth number

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                g1 — signed treatment advantage (rate_A − rate_B) in the hard stratum. Positive means A wins stratum 1.

                ● Param Stratum Gap2 Ground truth number

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                g2 — signed treatment advantage in the easy stratum. Negative g2 enables type-B (partial reversal) cells.

                ● Param Allocation Bias Ground truth number

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                b — allocation bias in stratum 1: extra fraction of stratum-1 cases assigned to treatment A above 50/50. Range 0.00–0.95.

                ƒ Phase S1 Total Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-256
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase s1 total is computed as 200 times the param stratum fraction rounded to the nearest whole number.

                In English

                (200 times param stratum fraction) rounded to the nearest whole number.

                As a formula
                = ROUND(200 * Param Stratum Fraction)
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ●Param Stratum FractionSyntheticPhase w — fraction of total cases in the hard (stratum-1) subgroup. Range 0.30–0.75 in this grid.
                Consumers 2 direct +10 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase NA1 SyntheticPhase ƒ Phase NB1 SyntheticPhase Phase NA2 SyntheticPhase Phase Pooled Rate a SyntheticPhase Phase NB2 SyntheticPhase Phase Pooled Rate B SyntheticPhase Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase

                Total cases in stratum 1 = ROUND(200 × ParamStratumFraction).

                ƒ Phase NA1 Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-257
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase NA1 is computed as the phase s1 total times 0.5 plus the param allocation bias divided by 2 rounded to the nearest whole number.

                In English

                (phase s1 total times (0.5 plus (param allocation bias divided by 2))) rounded to the nearest whole number.

                As a formula
                = ROUND(Phase S1 Total * (0.5 + Param Allocation Bias / 2))
                Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase S1 TotalSyntheticPhase (200 times param stratum fraction) rounded to the nearest whole number. ●Param Allocation BiasSyntheticPhase b — allocation bias in stratum 1: extra fraction of stratum-1 cases assigned to treatment A above 50/50. Range 0.00–0.95.
                Consumers 3 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase NB1 SyntheticPhase ƒ Phase NA2 SyntheticPhase ƒ Phase Pooled Rate a SyntheticPhase Phase NB2 SyntheticPhase Phase Pooled Rate B SyntheticPhase Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase

                Treatment-A cases in stratum 1.

                ƒ Phase NB1 Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-258
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase NB1 is computed as the phase s1 total minus the phase NA1.

                In English

                Phase s1 total minus phase na1.

                As a formula
                = Phase S1 Total - Phase NA1
                Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase S1 TotalSyntheticPhase (200 times param stratum fraction) rounded to the nearest whole number. ƒPhase NA1SyntheticPhase (phase s1 total times (0.5 plus (param allocation bias divided by 2))) rounded to the nearest whole number.
                Consumers 2 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase NB2 SyntheticPhase ƒ Phase Pooled Rate B SyntheticPhase Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase

                Treatment-B cases in stratum 1.

                ƒ Phase NA2 Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-259
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase NA2 is computed as 100 minus the phase NA1.

                In English

                100 minus phase na1.

                As a formula
                = 100 - Phase NA1
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase NA1SyntheticPhase (phase s1 total times (0.5 plus (param allocation bias divided by 2))) rounded to the nearest whole number.
                Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Pooled Rate a SyntheticPhase Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase

                Treatment-A cases in stratum 2.

                ƒ Phase NB2 Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-260
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase NB2 is computed as 100 minus the phase NB1.

                In English

                100 minus phase nb1.

                As a formula
                = 100 - Phase NB1
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase NB1SyntheticPhase Phase s1 total minus phase na1.
                Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Pooled Rate B SyntheticPhase Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase

                Treatment-B cases in stratum 2.

                ƒ Phase Rate A1 Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-261
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase rate a1 is computed as 0.45 plus the param stratum gap1 divided by 2.

                In English

                0.45 plus (param stratum gap1 divided by 2).

                As a formula
                = 0.45 + Param Stratum Gap1 / 2
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ●Param Stratum Gap1SyntheticPhase g1 — signed treatment advantage (rate_A − rate_B) in the hard stratum. Positive means A wins stratum 1.
                Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Pooled Rate a SyntheticPhase Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase

                Success rate for A in hard stratum.

                ƒ Phase Rate B1 Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-262
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase rate b1 is computed as 0.45 minus the param stratum gap1 divided by 2.

                In English

                0.45 minus (param stratum gap1 divided by 2).

                As a formula
                = 0.45 - Param Stratum Gap1 / 2
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ●Param Stratum Gap1SyntheticPhase g1 — signed treatment advantage (rate_A − rate_B) in the hard stratum. Positive means A wins stratum 1.
                Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one

                As a formula
                = 0.75 + Param Stratum Gap2 / 2
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads

                Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Pooled Rate a SyntheticPhase Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase

                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase rate b2 is computed as 0.75 minus the param stratum gap2 divided by 2.

                As a formula
                = 0.75 - Param Stratum Gap2 / 2
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ●Param Stratum Gap2SyntheticPhase g2 — signed treatment advantage in the easy stratum. Negative g2 enables type-B (partial reversal) cells.
                Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Pooled Rate B SyntheticPhase Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase

                Success rate for B in easy stratum.

                ƒ Phase Pooled Rate a Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-265
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase pooled rate a is computed as the phase NA1 times the phase rate a1 plus the phase NA2 times the phase rate a2 divided by 100.

                In English

                ((phase na1 times phase rate a1) plus (phase na2 times phase rate a2)) divided by 100.

                As a formula
                = (Phase NA1 * Phase Rate A1 + Phase NA2 * Phase Rate A2) / 100
                Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase NA1SyntheticPhase (phase s1 total times (0.5 plus (param allocation bias divided by 2))) rounded to the nearest whole number. ƒPhase Rate A1SyntheticPhase 0.45 plus (param stratum gap1 divided by 2). ƒPhase NA2SyntheticPhase 100 minus phase na1. ƒPhase Rate A2SyntheticPhase 0.75 plus (param stratum gap2 divided by 2).
                Consumers 1 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase

                Pooled success rate for treatment A.

                ƒ Phase Pooled Rate B Calculated 5 hops from ground truth number DR-266
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase pooled rate b is computed as the phase NB1 times the phase rate b1 plus the phase NB2 times the phase rate b2 divided by 100.

                In English

                ((phase nb1 times phase rate b1) plus (phase nb2 times phase rate b2)) divided by 100.

                As a formula
                = (Phase NB1 * Phase Rate B1 + Phase NB2 * Phase Rate B2) / 100
                Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase NB1SyntheticPhase Phase s1 total minus phase na1. ƒPhase Rate B1SyntheticPhase 0.45 minus (param stratum gap1 divided by 2). ƒPhase NB2SyntheticPhase 100 minus phase nb1. ƒPhase Rate B2SyntheticPhase 0.75 minus (param stratum gap2 divided by 2).
                Consumers 1 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Signed Pooled Gap SyntheticPhase Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase

                Pooled success rate for treatment B.

                ƒ Phase Signed Pooled Gap Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-267
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase signed pooled gap is computed as the phase pooled rate a minus the phase pooled rate b.

                In English

                Phase pooled rate a minus phase pooled rate b.

                As a formula
                = Phase Pooled Rate a - Phase Pooled Rate B
                Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase Pooled Rate aSyntheticPhase ((phase na1 times phase rate a1) plus (phase na2 times phase rate a2)) divided by 100. ƒPhase Pooled Rate BSyntheticPhase ((phase nb1 times phase rate b1) plus (phase nb2 times phase rate b2)) divided by 100.
                Consumers 4 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Strata Won by Loser SyntheticPhase ƒ Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase ƒ Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase ƒ Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase

                Pooled rate gap (A minus B).

                ƒ Phase Corrected Gap Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-268
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase corrected gap is computed as the param stratum fraction times the param stratum gap1 plus 1 minus the param stratum fraction times the param stratum gap2.

                In English

                (param stratum fraction times param stratum gap1) plus ((1 minus param stratum fraction) times param stratum gap2).

                As a formula
                = Param Stratum Fraction * Param Stratum Gap1 + (1 - Param Stratum Fraction) * Param Stratum Gap2
                Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
                ●Param Stratum FractionSyntheticPhase w — fraction of total cases in the hard (stratum-1) subgroup. Range 0.30–0.75 in this grid. ●Param Stratum Gap1SyntheticPhase g1 — signed treatment advantage (rate_A − rate_B) in the hard stratum. Positive means A wins stratum 1. ●Param Stratum Gap2SyntheticPhase g2 — signed treatment advantage in the easy stratum. Negative g2 enables type-B (partial reversal) cells.
                Consumers 3 direct rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase is Sign Flip SyntheticPhase ƒ Phase Allocation Distortion SyntheticPhase ƒ Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase

                Allocation-free equal-weight gap = w×g1 + (1−w)×g2.

                ƒ Phase Strata Won by Loser Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-269
                In RuleSpeak

                The synthetic phase's phase strata won by loser is determined by the following priority:

                1. the count of the following that hold: the param stratum gap1 is less than 0 and the param stratum gap2 is less than 0 if the phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0
                2. the count of the following that hold: the param stratum gap1 is greater than 0 and the param stratum gap2 is greater than 0 otherwise
                In English

                If phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0 then (if param stratum gap1 is less than 0 then 1, otherwise 0 plus if param stratum gap2 is less than 0 then 1, otherwise 0), otherwise (if param stratum gap1 is greater than 0 then 1, otherwise 0 plus if param stratum gap2 is greater than 0 then 1, otherwise 0).

                As a formula
                = IF(Phase Signed Pooled Gap > 0, IF(Param Stratum Gap1 < 0, 1, 0) + IF(Param Stratum Gap2 < 0, 1, 0), IF(Param Stratum Gap1 > 0, 1, 0) + IF(Param Stratum Gap2 > 0, 1, 0))
                Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase Signed Pooled GapSyntheticPhase Phase pooled rate a minus phase pooled rate b. ●Param Stratum Gap1SyntheticPhase g1 — signed treatment advantage (rate_A − rate_B) in the hard stratum. Positive means A wins stratum 1. ●Param Stratum Gap2SyntheticPhase g2 — signed treatment advantage in the easy stratum. Negative g2 enables type-B (partial reversal) cells.
                Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Reversal Intensity SyntheticPhase Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase

                Count of strata whose per-stratum winner disagrees with the pooled winner.

                ƒ Phase Reversal Intensity Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-270
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase reversal intensity is computed as the phase strata won by loser divided by 2.

                In English

                Phase strata won by loser divided by 2.

                As a formula
                = Phase Strata Won by Loser / 2
                Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase Strata Won by LoserSyntheticPhase If phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0 then (if param stratum gap1 is less than 0 then 1, otherwise 0 plus if param stratum gap2 is less than 0 then 1, otherwise 0), otherwise (if param stratum gap1 is greater than 0 then 1, otherwise 0 plus if param stratum gap2 is greater than 0 then 1, otherwise 0).
                Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase

                PhaseStrataWonByLoser / 2 for this 2-stratum template.

                ƒ Phase is Sign Flip Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-271
                In RuleSpeak

                The synthetic phase's phase is sign flip is determined by the following priority:

                1. the phase signed pooled gap is less than 0 if the phase corrected gap is greater than 0
                2. the phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0 otherwise
                In English

                If phase corrected gap is greater than 0 then phase signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0.

                As a formula
                = IF(Phase Corrected Gap > 0, Phase Signed Pooled Gap < 0, Phase Signed Pooled Gap > 0)
                Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase Corrected GapSyntheticPhase (param stratum fraction times param stratum gap1) plus ((1 minus param stratum fraction) times param stratum gap2). ƒPhase Signed Pooled GapSyntheticPhase Phase pooled rate a minus phase pooled rate b.
                Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase

                TRUE when equal-weight and pooled gaps have opposite signs.

                ƒ Phase Allocation Distortion Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-272
                In RuleSpeak

                A synthetic phase's phase allocation distortion is computed as the absolute value of the phase corrected gap minus the phase signed pooled gap.

                In English

                The absolute value of (phase corrected gap minus phase signed pooled gap).

                As a formula
                = ABS(Phase Corrected Gap - Phase Signed Pooled Gap)
                Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase Corrected GapSyntheticPhase (param stratum fraction times param stratum gap1) plus ((1 minus param stratum fraction) times param stratum gap2). ƒPhase Signed Pooled GapSyntheticPhase Phase pooled rate a minus phase pooled rate b.
                Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                ƒ Phase Distortion Type SyntheticPhase
                Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase

                |PhaseCorrectedGap − PhaseSignedPooledGap|.

                ƒ Phase Distortion Type Calculated 9 hops from ground truth string DR-273
                In RuleSpeak

                The synthetic phase's phase distortion type is determined by the following priority:

                1. the literal “A” if all of these hold:
                  • the phase is sign flip flag is set
                  • the phase reversal intensity is 1
                2. the literal “B” if all of these hold:
                  • the phase is sign flip flag is set
                  • the phase reversal intensity is less than 1
                3. the literal “C+” if all of these hold:
                  • it is not the case that the phase is sign flip flag is set
                  • the phase allocation distortion is greater than 0.01
                  • the absolute value of the phase signed pooled gap is greater than the absolute value of the phase corrected gap plus 0.001
                4. the literal “C-” if all of these hold:
                  • it is not the case that the phase is sign flip flag is set
                  • the phase allocation distortion is greater than 0.01
                  • the absolute value of the phase signed pooled gap is less than the absolute value of the phase corrected gap minus 0.001
                5. the literal “D” otherwise
                In English

                If phase is sign flip and phase reversal intensity equals 1 then "A", otherwise if phase is sign flip and phase reversal intensity is less than 1 then "B", otherwise if not phase is sign flip, phase allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of phase signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of phase corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not phase is sign flip, phase allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of phase signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of phase corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".

                As a formula
                = IF(AND(Phase is Sign Flip, Phase Reversal Intensity = 1), "A", IF(AND(Phase is Sign Flip, Phase Reversal Intensity < 1), "B", IF(AND(NOT(Phase is Sign Flip), Phase Allocation Distortion > 0.01, ABS(Phase Signed Pooled Gap) > ABS(Phase Corrected Gap) + 0.001), "C+", IF(AND(NOT(Phase is Sign Flip), Phase Allocation Distortion > 0.01, ABS(Phase Signed Pooled Gap) < ABS(Phase Corrected Gap) - 0.001), "C-", "D"))))
                Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
                ƒPhase is Sign FlipSyntheticPhase If phase corrected gap is greater than 0 then phase signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒPhase Reversal IntensitySyntheticPhase Phase strata won by loser divided by 2. ƒPhase Allocation DistortionSyntheticPhase The absolute value of (phase corrected gap minus phase signed pooled gap). ƒPhase Signed Pooled GapSyntheticPhase Phase pooled rate a minus phase pooled rate b. ƒPhase Corrected GapSyntheticPhase (param stratum fraction times param stratum gap1) plus ((1 minus param stratum fraction) times param stratum gap2).
                Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Param Stratum Fraction SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap1 SyntheticPhase ● Param Stratum Gap2 SyntheticPhase ● Param Allocation Bias SyntheticPhase

                Five-type geometric classification at this parameter point.

                Phase Diagram Summary

                A phase diagram summary is identified by its name.

                ƒ Phase Point Count Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-274
                In RuleSpeak

                A phase diagram summary's phase point count is computed as the phase type a count plus the phase type b count plus the phase type c plus count plus the phase type c minus count plus the phase type d count.

                In English

                (((phase type a count plus phase type b count) plus phase type c plus count) plus phase type c minus count) plus phase type d count.

                As a formula
                = Phase Type a Count + Phase Type B Count + Phase Type C Plus Count + Phase Type C Minus Count + Phase Type D Count
                Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
                ●Phase Type a CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type A. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type B CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type B. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Plus CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type C+. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Minus CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type C-. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type D CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type D. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.
                Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                ● Phase Type a Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type B Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type C Plus Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type C Minus Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type D Count PhaseDiagramSummary

                Total grid points in SyntheticPhase (sum of type counts). Refreshed from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

                ● Phase Type a Count Ground truth integer

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                Grid points classified as type A. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

                ● Phase Type B Count Ground truth integer

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                Grid points classified as type B. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

                ● Phase Type C Plus Count Ground truth integer

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                Grid points classified as type C+. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

                ● Phase Type C Minus Count Ground truth integer

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                Grid points classified as type C-. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

                ● Phase Type D Count Ground truth integer

                Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                Grid points classified as type D. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

                ƒ All Five Types Present Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-275
                In RuleSpeak

                A phase diagram summary is flagged all five types present if all of the following:

                  In English

                  Phase type a count is greater than 0, phase type b count is greater than 0, phase type c plus count is greater than 0, phase type c minus count is greater than 0, and phase type d count is greater than 0.

                  As a formula
                  = AND(Phase Type a Count > 0, Phase Type B Count > 0, Phase Type C Plus Count > 0, Phase Type C Minus Count > 0, Phase Type D Count > 0)
                  Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
                  ●Phase Type a CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type A. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type B CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type B. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Plus CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type C+. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Minus CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type C-. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type D CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type D. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.
                  Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                  ƒ Phase Witness Note PhaseDiagramSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Phase Type a Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type B Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type C Plus Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type C Minus Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type D Count PhaseDiagramSummary

                  TRUE when every DistortionType cell has at least one grid point.

                  ƒ Phase Taxonomy Witness Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-276
                  In RuleSpeak

                  A phase diagram summary's phase taxonomy witness is computed as the literal “A:”, followed by the phase type a count, followed by the literal “ B:”, followed by the phase type b count, followed by the literal “ C+:”, followed by the phase type c plus count, followed by the literal “ C-:”, followed by the phase type c minus count, followed by the literal “ D:”, followed by the phase type d count.

                  In English

                  "A:", phase type a count, " B:", phase type b count, " C+:", phase type c plus count, " C-:", phase type c minus count, " D:", joined with phase type d count.

                  As a formula
                  = CONCAT("A:", Phase Type a Count, " B:", Phase Type B Count, " C+:", Phase Type C Plus Count, " C-:", Phase Type C Minus Count, " D:", Phase Type D Count)
                  Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
                  ●Phase Type a CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type A. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type B CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type B. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Plus CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type C+. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Minus CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type C-. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type D CountPhaseDiagramSummary Grid points classified as type D. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.
                  Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Phase Type a Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type B Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type C Plus Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type C Minus Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type D Count PhaseDiagramSummary

                  Human-readable witness string of type counts across the grid.

                  ƒ Phase Witness Note Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-277
                  In RuleSpeak

                  The phase diagram summary's phase witness note is determined by the following priority:

                  1. the literal “PASS: all five distortion types populated in parameter space” if the all five types present flag is set
                  2. the literal “FAIL: missing distortion type in grid” otherwise
                  In English

                  If all five types present then "PASS: all five distortion types populated in parameter space", otherwise "FAIL: missing distortion type in grid".

                  As a formula
                  = IF(All Five Types Present, "PASS: all five distortion types populated in parameter space", "FAIL: missing distortion type in grid")
                  Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                  ƒAll Five Types PresentPhaseDiagramSummary Phase type a count is greater than 0, phase type b count is greater than 0, phase type c plus count is greater than 0, phase type c minus count is greater than 0, and phase type d count is greater than 0.
                  Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Phase Type a Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type B Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type C Plus Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type C Minus Count PhaseDiagramSummary ● Phase Type D Count PhaseDiagramSummary

                  PASS/FAIL summary for taxonomy completeness.

                  Ingestion Protocol

                  An ingestion protocol is identified by its name.

                  ● Rule Type Ground truth string

                  Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                  required-input | structural | inclusion | exclusion | adapter-metadata

                  ● Target Table Ground truth string

                  Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                  Rulebook table this item applies to.

                  ● Field Name Ground truth string

                  Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                  Column name when RuleType=required-input.

                  ● Requirement Statement Ground truth string

                  Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                  Plain-English statement of the requirement.

                  ● Mechanical Check Ground truth string

                  Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                  How to verify compliance before classification.

                  ● Sort Order Ground truth integer

                  Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                  Display order in the ingestion checklist.

                  Ingestion Summary

                  An ingestion summary is identified by its name.

                  Σ Protocol Item Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-278
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary's protocol item count is the number of ingestion protocol related to the ingestion summary.

                  In English

                  Count of IngestionProtocol whose protocol id is "<>".

                  As a formula
                  = COUNTIFS(Protocol ID, "<>")
                  Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                  ●Protocol IDIngestionProtocol Unique slug for this contract item.
                  Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                  ƒ Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Protocol ID IngestionProtocol

                  Total contract items.

                  Σ Corpus Cell Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-279
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary's corpus cell count is the number of case cells related to the ingestion summary.

                  In English

                  Count of CaseCells whose case cell id is "<>".

                  As a formula
                  = COUNTIFS(Case Cell ID, "<>")
                  Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                  ●Case Cell IDCaseCells Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-small-A).
                  Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
                  ƒ All Cells Valid IngestionSummary ƒ Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Case Cell ID CaseCells

                  Total CaseCell rows.

                  Σ Valid Cell Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-280
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary's valid cell count is the number of case cells related to the ingestion summary.

                  In English

                  Count of CaseCells whose is valid ingestion cell is true().

                  As a formula
                  = COUNTIFS(Is Valid Ingestion Cell, TRUE())
                  Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                  ƒIs Valid Ingestion CellCaseCells Cases is greater than 0, successes is at least 0, and successes is at most cases.
                  Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
                  ƒ All Cells Valid IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Cases CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells

                  Valid CaseCells.

                  ƒ All Cells Valid Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-281
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary is flagged all cells valid if the valid cell count is the corpus cell count.

                  In English

                  Valid cell count equals corpus cell count.

                  As a formula
                  = Valid Cell Count = Corpus Cell Count
                  Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                  ΣValid Cell CountIngestionSummary Count of CaseCells whose is valid ingestion cell is true(). ΣCorpus Cell CountIngestionSummary Count of CaseCells whose case cell id is "<>".
                  Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
                  ƒ Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Cases CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Case Cell ID CaseCells

                  Every cell passes validity predicate.

                  Σ Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-282
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary's study count is the number of studies related to the ingestion summary.

                  In English

                  Count of Studies whose study id is "<>".

                  As a formula
                  = COUNTIFS(Study ID, "<>")
                  Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                  ●Study IDStudies Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973).
                  Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
                  ƒ All Studies Structural IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Study ID Studies

                  Total studies.

                  Σ Structural Compliant Count Aggregation 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-283
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary's structural compliant count is the number of studies related to the ingestion summary.

                  In English

                  Count of Studies whose ingestion cell parity is true().

                  As a formula
                  = COUNTIFS(Ingestion Cell Parity, TRUE())
                  Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                  ƒIngestion Cell ParityStudies AND(LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, {{CellCount}} = LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2).
                  Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
                  ƒ All Studies Structural IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Study ID Studies ⇢ Study CaseCells

                  Studies with IngestionCellParity.

                  ƒ All Studies Structural Calculated 4 hops from ground truth boolean DR-284
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary is flagged all studies structural if the structural compliant count is the study count.

                  In English

                  Structural compliant count equals study count.

                  As a formula
                  = Structural Compliant Count = Study Count
                  Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                  ΣStructural Compliant CountIngestionSummary Count of Studies whose ingestion cell parity is true(). ΣStudy CountIngestionSummary Count of Studies whose study id is "<>".
                  Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
                  ƒ Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Study ID Studies ⇢ Study CaseCells

                  All studies structurally valid.

                  Σ Real Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-285
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary's real study count is the number of studies related to the ingestion summary.

                  In English

                  Count of Studies whose is synthetic is false().

                  As a formula
                  = COUNTIFS(Is Synthetic, FALSE())
                  Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                  ●Is SyntheticStudies TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.
                  Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
                  ƒ All Real Studies Compliant IngestionSummary ƒ Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Is Synthetic Studies

                  Non-synthetic studies.

                  Σ Real Fully Compliant Count Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth integer DR-286
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary's real fully compliant count is the number of studies related to the ingestion summary.

                  In English

                  Count of Studies whose ingestion compliance is "all".

                  As a formula
                  = COUNTIFS(Ingestion Compliance, "all")
                  Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                  ƒIngestion ComplianceStudies IF({{IsSynthetic}}, "exempt-synthetic", IF(AND({{IngestionCellParity}}, LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[VariableName]) <> ""), "all", "partial")).
                  Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
                  ƒ All Real Studies Compliant IngestionSummary ƒ Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Is Synthetic Studies ● Study ID Studies ⇢ Study CaseCells

                  Real studies with IngestionCompliance=all.

                  ƒ All Real Studies Compliant Calculated 5 hops from ground truth boolean DR-287
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary is flagged all real studies compliant if the real fully compliant count is the real study count.

                  In English

                  Real fully compliant count equals real study count.

                  As a formula
                  = Real Fully Compliant Count = Real Study Count
                  Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                  ΣReal Fully Compliant CountIngestionSummary Count of Studies whose ingestion compliance is "all". ΣReal Study CountIngestionSummary Count of Studies whose is synthetic is false().
                  Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
                  ƒ Ingestion Contract Passes IngestionSummary Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary
                  Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                  ● Is Synthetic Studies ● Study ID Studies ⇢ Study CaseCells

                  All real studies fully compliant.

                  ƒ Ingestion Contract Passes Calculated 6 hops from ground truth boolean DR-288
                  In RuleSpeak

                  An ingestion summary is flagged ingestion contract passes if all of the following:

                    In English

                    All cells valid, all studies structural, and all real studies compliant.

                    As a formula
                    = AND(All Cells Valid, All Studies Structural, All Real Studies Compliant)
                    Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
                    ƒAll Cells ValidIngestionSummary Valid cell count equals corpus cell count. ƒAll Studies StructuralIngestionSummary Structural compliant count equals study count. ƒAll Real Studies CompliantIngestionSummary Real fully compliant count equals real study count.
                    Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                    ƒ Ingestion Witness Note IngestionSummary
                    Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                    ● Cases CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Case Cell ID CaseCells ● Study ID Studies ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Is Synthetic Studies

                    Full contract passes.

                    ƒ Ingestion Witness Note Calculated 7 hops from ground truth string DR-289
                    In RuleSpeak

                    The ingestion summary's ingestion witness note is determined by the following priority:

                    1. the literal “PASS: ”, followed by the protocol item count, followed by the literal “ contract items; ”, followed by the corpus cell count, followed by the literal “ cells valid; ”, followed by the real fully compliant count, followed by a slash, followed by the real study count, followed by the literal “ real studies fully compliant” if the ingestion contract passes flag is set
                    2. the literal “FAIL: ingestion contract violated” otherwise
                    In English

                    If ingestion contract passes then "PASS: ", protocol item count, " contract items; ", corpus cell count, " cells valid; ", real fully compliant count, "/", real study count, joined with " real studies fully compliant", otherwise "FAIL: ingestion contract violated".

                    As a formula
                    = IF(Ingestion Contract Passes, CONCAT("PASS: ", Protocol Item Count, " contract items; ", Corpus Cell Count, " cells valid; ", Real Fully Compliant Count, "/", Real Study Count, " real studies fully compliant"), "FAIL: ingestion contract violated")
                    Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
                    ƒIngestion Contract PassesIngestionSummary All cells valid, all studies structural, and all real studies compliant. ΣProtocol Item CountIngestionSummary Count of IngestionProtocol whose protocol id is "<>". ΣCorpus Cell CountIngestionSummary Count of CaseCells whose case cell id is "<>". ΣReal Fully Compliant CountIngestionSummary Count of Studies whose ingestion compliance is "all". ΣReal Study CountIngestionSummary Count of Studies whose is synthetic is false().
                    Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                    ● Cases CaseCells ● Successes CaseCells ● Case Cell ID CaseCells ● Study ID Studies ⇢ Study CaseCells ● Is Synthetic Studies ● Protocol ID IngestionProtocol

                    PASS/FAIL summary.

                    Candidate Study Catalog

                    A candidate study catalog is identified by its name.

                    ● Title Ground truth string

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    Human-readable study title.

                    ● Citation Ground truth string

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    Primary publication citation.

                    ● Source URL Ground truth string

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    URL to source paper or data.

                    ● Domain Ground truth string

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    Domain tag: medicine, epidemiology, legal, sports, education, economics, social-science.

                    ● Stratum Variable Name Ground truth string

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    Pre-registered stratifying variable name for encoding.

                    ● Expected Distortion Type Ground truth string

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    Hypothesized DistortionType after classification: A, B, C+, C-, D, or unknown.

                    ● Ingestion Status Ground truth string

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    imported | candidate | blocked | rejected

                    ● Priority Ground truth integer

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    1=high priority encode next; 5=blocked/duplicate.

                    ● Stratum Count Estimate Ground truth integer

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    Expected number of strata (minimum 2).

                    ● Data Source Note Ground truth string

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    Where to find cell counts and encoding notes.

                    ● Publication Year Ground truth integer

                    Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                    Publication year when known.

                    ƒ Is Ready to Encode Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-290
                    In RuleSpeak

                    A candidate study catalog is considered a ready to encode if all of the following:

                      In English

                      Ingestion status is "candidate", proposed study id is not "", citation is not "", and stratum variable name is not "".

                      As a formula
                      = AND(Ingestion Status = "candidate", Proposed Study ID <> "", Citation <> "", Stratum Variable Name <> "")
                      Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
                      ●Ingestion StatusCandidateStudyCatalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected ●Proposed Study IDCandidateStudyCatalog Stable slug to assign in Studies when imported. ●CitationCandidateStudyCatalog Primary publication citation. ●Stratum Variable NameCandidateStudyCatalog Pre-registered stratifying variable name for encoding.
                      Consumers 2 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
                      Σ Ready to Encode Count CorpusCatalogSummary Σ Encode Pipeline Ready Count CorpusCatalogSummary Import Session Ready CorpusCatalogSummary Catalog Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                      Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                      ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Proposed Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Citation CandidateStudyCatalog ● Stratum Variable Name CandidateStudyCatalog

                      TRUE when candidate has citation, proposed id, and stratum variable — ready for an import session.

                      ƒ Is Imported Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-291
                      In RuleSpeak

                      A candidate study catalog is considered imported if the ingestion status is the literal “imported”.

                      In English

                      Ingestion status is "imported".

                      As a formula
                      = Ingestion Status = "imported"
                      Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                      ●Ingestion StatusCandidateStudyCatalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
                      Consumers 8 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
                      ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected a Count ModelSummary ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD Count ModelSummary ƒ Expansion Wave2 Study Count ModelSummary ƒ Type Prediction Match CandidateStudyCatalog ƒ Observed Sign Flip Type CandidateStudyCatalog ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match CandidateStudyCatalog Σ Type Prediction Mismatch Count CorpusCatalogSummary Σ Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count CorpusCatalogSummary Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate ModelSummary Domain Diversity Note ModelSummary Type Prediction Match Count CorpusCatalogSummary Sign Flip Prediction Match Count CorpusCatalogSummary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary Type Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary
                      Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                      ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                      TRUE when already in corpus.

                      ↗ Observed Distortion Type Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-292
                      In RuleSpeak

                      A candidate study catalog's observed distortion type is the value of LOOKUP(LinkedStudyId, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

                      In English

                      LOOKUP({{LinkedStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

                      As a formula
                      = LOOKUP(Linked Study ID, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType])
                      Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                      ●Linked Study IDCandidateStudyCatalog Studies.StudyId when IngestionStatus=imported.
                      Consumers 3 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one
                      ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD Count ModelSummary ƒ Type Prediction Match CandidateStudyCatalog ƒ Observed Sign Flip Type CandidateStudyCatalog Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate ModelSummary Type Prediction Match Count CorpusCatalogSummary Type Prediction Mismatch Count CorpusCatalogSummary Sign Flip Prediction Match CandidateStudyCatalog Type Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary Sign Flip Prediction Match Count CorpusCatalogSummary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary
                      Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                      ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog

                      Actual DistortionType from TreatmentRankings when imported.

                      ƒ Type Prediction Match Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-293
                      In RuleSpeak

                      The candidate study catalog's type prediction match is determined by the following priority:

                      1. the observed distortion type is the expected distortion type if the is imported flag is set
                      2. an empty string otherwise
                      In English

                      If is imported then observed distortion type equals expected distortion type, otherwise "".

                      As a formula
                      = IF(Is Imported, Observed Distortion Type = Expected Distortion Type, "")
                      Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
                      ƒIs ImportedCandidateStudyCatalog Ingestion status is "imported". ↗Observed Distortion TypeCandidateStudyCatalog LOOKUP({{LinkedStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]). ●Expected Distortion TypeCandidateStudyCatalog Hypothesized DistortionType after classification: A, B, C+, C-, D, or unknown.
                      Consumers 2 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
                      Σ Type Prediction Match Count CorpusCatalogSummary Σ Type Prediction Mismatch Count CorpusCatalogSummary Type Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                      Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                      ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog

                      TRUE when observed type matches expected (imported rows only).

                      ƒ Expected Sign Flip Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-294
                      In RuleSpeak

                      A candidate study catalog is flagged expected sign flip if all of the following:

                        In English

                        Expected distortion type is "A" or expected distortion type is "B".

                        As a formula
                        = OR(Expected Distortion Type = "A", Expected Distortion Type = "B")
                        Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                        ●Expected Distortion TypeCandidateStudyCatalog Hypothesized DistortionType after classification: A, B, C+, C-, D, or unknown.
                        Consumers 2 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
                        ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match CandidateStudyCatalog Σ Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count CorpusCatalogSummary Sign Flip Prediction Match Count CorpusCatalogSummary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                        Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                        ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog

                        TRUE when curator tagged ExpectedDistortionType as A or B (sign-flip expectation).

                        ƒ Observed Sign Flip Type Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-295
                        In RuleSpeak

                        The candidate study catalog's observed sign flip type is determined by the following priority:

                        1. the OR of the observed distortion type is the literal “A” and the observed distortion type is the literal “B” if the is imported flag is set
                        2. an empty string otherwise
                        In English

                        If is imported then observed distortion type is "A" or observed distortion type is "B", otherwise "".

                        As a formula
                        = IF(Is Imported, OR(Observed Distortion Type = "A", Observed Distortion Type = "B"), "")
                        Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                        ƒIs ImportedCandidateStudyCatalog Ingestion status is "imported". ↗Observed Distortion TypeCandidateStudyCatalog LOOKUP({{LinkedStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).
                        Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
                        ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match CandidateStudyCatalog Sign Flip Prediction Match Count CorpusCatalogSummary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                        Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                        ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog

                        TRUE when observed DistortionType is A or B (imported rows only).

                        ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-296
                        In RuleSpeak

                        The candidate study catalog's sign flip prediction match is determined by the following priority:

                        1. the observed sign flip type if all of these hold:
                          • the is imported flag is set
                          • the expected sign flip flag is set
                        2. an empty string otherwise
                        In English

                        If is imported and expected sign flip then observed sign flip type, otherwise "".

                        As a formula
                        = IF(AND(Is Imported, Expected Sign Flip), Observed Sign Flip Type, "")
                        Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
                        ƒIs ImportedCandidateStudyCatalog Ingestion status is "imported". ƒExpected Sign FlipCandidateStudyCatalog Expected distortion type is "A" or expected distortion type is "B". ƒObserved Sign Flip TypeCandidateStudyCatalog If is imported then observed distortion type is "A" or observed distortion type is "B", otherwise "".
                        Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
                        Σ Sign Flip Prediction Match Count CorpusCatalogSummary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                        Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                        ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog

                        TRUE when Expected A∪B and observed is A∪B — weaker flip-type prediction test.

                        ● Data Acquisition Status Ground truth string

                        Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                        not_started | downloaded | manual_only | blocked

                        ● Reversal Mechanism Ground truth string

                        Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                        Plain-language reversal mechanism from expansion plan.

                        ● Paradox Confirmation Ground truth string

                        Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                        confirmed | plausible | pending | not_applicable

                        ● Expansion Wave Ground truth string

                        Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                        Catalog wave tag; loop-67 expansion rows = expansion-wave-1.

                        ƒ Is Data Ready Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-297
                        In RuleSpeak

                        A candidate study catalog is considered a data ready if all of the following:

                          In English

                          Data acquisition status is "downloaded" or data acquisition status is "manual_only".

                          As a formula
                          = OR(Data Acquisition Status = "downloaded", Data Acquisition Status = "manual_only")
                          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                          ●Data Acquisition StatusCandidateStudyCatalog not_started | downloaded | manual_only | blocked
                          Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
                          Σ Data Ready Count CorpusCatalogSummary Σ Encode Pipeline Ready Count CorpusCatalogSummary Catalog Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                          Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                          ● Data Acquisition Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                          TRUE when stratified data is downloaded or available from published tables.

                          Corpus Catalog Summary

                          A corpus catalog summary is identified by its name.

                          Σ Total Catalog Entries Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-298
                          In RuleSpeak

                          A corpus catalog summary's total catalog entries is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

                          In English

                          Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose candidate id is "<>".

                          As a formula
                          = COUNTIFS(Candidate ID, "<>")
                          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                          ●Candidate IDCandidateStudyCatalog Unique slug for this catalog entry.
                          Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                          ● Candidate ID CandidateStudyCatalog

                          All catalog rows.

                          Σ Imported Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-299
                          In RuleSpeak

                          A corpus catalog summary's imported count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

                          In English

                          Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose ingestion status is "imported".

                          As a formula
                          = COUNTIFS(Ingestion Status, "imported")
                          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                          ●Ingestion StatusCandidateStudyCatalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
                          Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
                          ƒ Type Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                          Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                          ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                          Rows already imported.

                          Σ Candidate Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-300
                          In RuleSpeak

                          A corpus catalog summary's candidate count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

                          In English

                          Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose ingestion status is "candidate".

                          As a formula
                          = COUNTIFS(Ingestion Status, "candidate")
                          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                          ●Ingestion StatusCandidateStudyCatalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
                          Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
                          ƒ Import Session Ready CorpusCatalogSummary ƒ Catalog Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                          Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                          ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                          Rows queued for import.

                          Σ Blocked Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-301
                          In RuleSpeak

                          A corpus catalog summary's blocked count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

                          In English

                          Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose ingestion status is "blocked".

                          As a formula
                          = COUNTIFS(Ingestion Status, "blocked")
                          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                          ●Ingestion StatusCandidateStudyCatalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
                          Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                          ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                          Duplicate or blocked rows.

                          Σ Ready to Encode Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-302
                          In RuleSpeak

                          A corpus catalog summary's ready to encode count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

                          In English

                          Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is ready to encode is true().

                          As a formula
                          = COUNTIFS(Is Ready to Encode, TRUE())
                          Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
