                          ƒIs Ready to EncodeCandidateStudyCatalog Ingestion status is "candidate", proposed study id is not "", citation is not "", and stratum variable name is not "".
                          Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
                          ƒ Import Session Ready CorpusCatalogSummary ƒ Catalog Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                          Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                          ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Proposed Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Citation CandidateStudyCatalog ● Stratum Variable Name CandidateStudyCatalog

                          Candidates passing IsReadyToEncode.

                          Σ High Priority Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-303
                          In RuleSpeak

                          A corpus catalog summary's high priority count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's high priorities.

                          In English

                          Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose ingestion status is "candidate" and priority is 1 plus count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose ingestion status is "candidate" and priority is 2.

                          As a formula
                          = COUNTIFS(Ingestion Status, "candidate", Priority, 1) + COUNTIFS(Ingestion Status, "candidate", Priority, 2)
                          Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                          ●Ingestion StatusCandidateStudyCatalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected ●PriorityCandidateStudyCatalog 1=high priority encode next; 5=blocked/duplicate.
                          Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                          ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Priority CandidateStudyCatalog

                          Candidates with Priority <= 2.

                          ƒ Import Session Ready Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-304
                          In RuleSpeak

                          A corpus catalog summary is flagged import session ready if all of the following:

                            In English

                            Candidate count is at least 20 and ready to encode count is at least 15.

                            As a formula
                            = AND(Candidate Count >= 20, Ready to Encode Count >= 15)
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ΣCandidate CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose ingestion status is "candidate". ΣReady to Encode CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is ready to encode is true().
                            Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Catalog Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                            Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Proposed Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Citation CandidateStudyCatalog ● Stratum Variable Name CandidateStudyCatalog

                            TRUE when backlog is large enough for a bulk import session.

                            Σ Type Prediction Match Count Aggregation 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-305
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A corpus catalog summary's type prediction match count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

                            In English

                            Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose type prediction match is true().

                            As a formula
                            = COUNTIFS(Type Prediction Match, TRUE())
                            Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                            ƒType Prediction MatchCandidateStudyCatalog If is imported then observed distortion type equals expected distortion type, otherwise "".
                            Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Type Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Imported catalog rows where ObservedDistortionType exactly matches ExpectedDistortionType.

                            Σ Type Prediction Mismatch Count Aggregation 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-306
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A corpus catalog summary's type prediction mismatch count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's candidate study catalog that are not type prediction match.

                            In English

                            Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is imported is true() and type prediction match is false().

                            As a formula
                            = COUNTIFS(Is Imported, TRUE(), Type Prediction Match, FALSE())
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ƒIs ImportedCandidateStudyCatalog Ingestion status is "imported". ƒType Prediction MatchCandidateStudyCatalog If is imported then observed distortion type equals expected distortion type, otherwise "".
                            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Imported catalog rows where exact type prediction failed.

                            Σ Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-307
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A corpus catalog summary's sign flip prediction eligible count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's candidate study catalog that are expected sign flip.

                            In English

                            Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is imported is true() and expected sign flip is true().

                            As a formula
                            = COUNTIFS(Is Imported, TRUE(), Expected Sign Flip, TRUE())
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ƒIs ImportedCandidateStudyCatalog Ingestion status is "imported". ƒExpected Sign FlipCandidateStudyCatalog Expected distortion type is "A" or expected distortion type is "B".
                            Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                            Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Imported rows where ExpectedDistortionType was A or B.

                            Σ Sign Flip Prediction Match Count Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth integer DR-308
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A corpus catalog summary's sign flip prediction match count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

                            In English

                            Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose sign flip prediction match is true().

                            As a formula
                            = COUNTIFS(Sign Flip Prediction Match, TRUE())
                            Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                            ƒSign Flip Prediction MatchCandidateStudyCatalog If is imported and expected sign flip then observed sign flip type, otherwise "".
                            Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate CorpusCatalogSummary ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Expected A∪B rows where observed type is also A∪B.

                            ƒ Type Prediction Match Rate Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-309
                            In RuleSpeak

                            The corpus catalog summary's type prediction match rate is determined by the following priority:

                            1. an empty string if the imported count is 0
                            2. the type prediction match count divided by the imported count otherwise
                            In English

                            If imported count equals 0 then "", otherwise (type prediction match count divided by imported count).

                            As a formula
                            = IF(Imported Count = 0, "", Type Prediction Match Count / Imported Count)
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ΣImported CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose ingestion status is "imported". ΣType Prediction Match CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose type prediction match is true().
                            Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Exact-match rate across imported catalog rows.

                            ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Calculated 5 hops from ground truth number DR-310
                            In RuleSpeak

                            The corpus catalog summary's sign flip prediction match rate is determined by the following priority:

                            1. an empty string if the sign flip prediction eligible count is 0
                            2. the sign flip prediction match count divided by the sign flip prediction eligible count otherwise
                            In English

                            If sign flip prediction eligible count equals 0 then "", otherwise (sign flip prediction match count divided by sign flip prediction eligible count).

                            As a formula
                            = IF(Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count = 0, "", Sign Flip Prediction Match Count / Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count)
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ΣSign Flip Prediction Eligible CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is imported is true() and expected sign flip is true(). ΣSign Flip Prediction Match CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose sign flip prediction match is true().
                            Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Sign-flip-type match rate among Expected A∪B rows.

                            ƒ Catalog Witness Note Calculated 4 hops from ground truth string DR-311
                            In RuleSpeak

                            The corpus catalog summary's catalog witness note is determined by the following priority:

                            1. the literal “READY: ”, followed by the ready to encode count, followed by the literal “ encode-ready (”, followed by the data ready count, followed by the literal “ data-ready) of ”, followed by the candidate count, followed by the literal “ candidates” if the import session ready flag is set
                            2. the literal “BUILDING: ”, followed by the candidate count, followed by the literal “ candidates — ”, followed by the data ready count, followed by the literal “ data-ready, ”, followed by the ready to encode count, followed by the literal “ encode-ready” otherwise
                            In English

                            If import session ready then "READY: ", ready to encode count, " encode-ready (", data ready count, " data-ready) of ", candidate count, joined with " candidates", otherwise "BUILDING: ", candidate count, " candidates — ", data ready count, " data-ready, ", ready to encode count, joined with " encode-ready".

                            As a formula
                            = IF(Import Session Ready, CONCAT("READY: ", Ready to Encode Count, " encode-ready (", Data Ready Count, " data-ready) of ", Candidate Count, " candidates"), CONCAT("BUILDING: ", Candidate Count, " candidates — ", Data Ready Count, " data-ready, ", Ready to Encode Count, " encode-ready"))
                            Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
                            ƒImport Session ReadyCorpusCatalogSummary Candidate count is at least 20 and ready to encode count is at least 15. ΣReady to Encode CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is ready to encode is true(). ΣData Ready CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is data ready is true() and ingestion status is "candidate". ΣCandidate CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose ingestion status is "candidate".
                            Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Proposed Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Citation CandidateStudyCatalog ● Stratum Variable Name CandidateStudyCatalog ● Data Acquisition Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Human-readable readiness summary.

                            ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note Calculated 6 hops from ground truth string DR-312
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A corpus catalog summary's catalog prediction witness note is computed as the value of CONCAT("exact=", TypePredictionMatchCount, "/", ImportedCount, " (", ROUND(TypePredictionMatchRate * 100, 1), "%); flipPred=", SignFlipPredictionMatchCount, "/", SignFlipPredictionEligibleCount, " (", ROUND(SignFlipPredictionMatchRate * 100, 1), "%)")).

                            In English

                            CONCAT("exact=", {{TypePredictionMatchCount}}, "/", {{ImportedCount}}, " (", ROUND({{TypePredictionMatchRate}} * 100, 1), "%); flipPred=", {{SignFlipPredictionMatchCount}}, "/", {{SignFlipPredictionEligibleCount}}, " (", ROUND({{SignFlipPredictionMatchRate}} * 100, 1), "%)")).

                            As a formula
                            = CONCAT("exact=", Type Prediction Match Count, "/", Imported Count, " (", ROUND(Type Prediction Match Rate * 100, 1), "%); flipPred=", Sign Flip Prediction Match Count, "/", Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count, " (", ROUND(Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate * 100, 1), "%)"))
                            Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
                            ΣType Prediction Match CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose type prediction match is true(). ΣImported CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose ingestion status is "imported". ƒType Prediction Match RateCorpusCatalogSummary If imported count equals 0 then "", otherwise (type prediction match count divided by imported count). ΣSign Flip Prediction Match CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose sign flip prediction match is true(). ΣSign Flip Prediction Eligible CountCorpusCatalogSummary Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is imported is true() and expected sign flip is true(). ƒSign Flip Prediction Match RateCorpusCatalogSummary If sign flip prediction eligible count equals 0 then "", otherwise (sign flip prediction match count divided by sign flip prediction eligible count).
                            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Linked Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Expected Distortion Type CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Loop-65 type-prediction audit summary.

                            Σ Data Ready Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-313
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A corpus catalog summary's data ready count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's candidate study catalog that have a ingestion status of “candidate”.

                            In English

                            Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is data ready is true() and ingestion status is "candidate".

                            As a formula
                            = COUNTIFS(Is Data Ready, TRUE(), Ingestion Status, "candidate")
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ƒIs Data ReadyCandidateStudyCatalog Data acquisition status is "downloaded" or data acquisition status is "manual_only". ●Ingestion StatusCandidateStudyCatalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
                            Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Catalog Witness Note CorpusCatalogSummary
                            Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Data Acquisition Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Candidates with IsDataReady=TRUE.

                            Σ Encode Pipeline Ready Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-314
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A corpus catalog summary's encode pipeline ready count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's candidate study catalog that are data readies.

                            In English

                            Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose is ready to encode is true() and is data ready is true().

                            As a formula
                            = COUNTIFS(Is Ready to Encode, TRUE(), Is Data Ready, TRUE())
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ƒIs Ready to EncodeCandidateStudyCatalog Ingestion status is "candidate", proposed study id is not "", citation is not "", and stratum variable name is not "". ƒIs Data ReadyCandidateStudyCatalog Data acquisition status is "downloaded" or data acquisition status is "manual_only".
                            Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Proposed Study ID CandidateStudyCatalog ● Citation CandidateStudyCatalog ● Stratum Variable Name CandidateStudyCatalog ● Data Acquisition Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Candidates both data-ready and encode-ready.

                            Σ Expansion Candidate Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-315
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A corpus catalog summary's expansion candidate count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

                            In English

                            Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1".

                            As a formula
                            = COUNTIFS(Expansion Wave, "expansion-wave-1")
                            Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                            ●Expansion WaveCandidateStudyCatalog Catalog wave tag; loop-67 expansion rows = expansion-wave-1.
                            Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Expansion Wave CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Loop-67 expansion-wave-1 candidates.

                            Domain Expansion Target

                            A domain expansion target is identified by its name.

                            ● Domain Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Domain tag matching Studies.Domain.

                            ● Legacy Domain Alias Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Studies.Domain slug for legacy imported corpus rows.

                            ● Target Min Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Minimum real studies desired in this domain.

                            Σ Current Imported Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-316
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A domain">domain expansion target's current imported count is the number of the domain expansion target's studies that are not synthetics.

                            In English

                            Count of Studies whose domain">domain is if legacy domain alias is not "" then legacy domain alias, otherwise domain and is synthetic is false().

                            As a formula
                            = COUNTIFS(Domain, IF(Legacy Domain Alias<>"", Legacy Domain Alias, Domain), Is Synthetic, FALSE())
                            Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
                            ●DomainStudies Research domain: medicine | epidemiology | social-science | education | legal | sports | economics | synthetic. Enables cross-domain SignalPurity stratification. ●Legacy Domain AliasDomainExpansionTargets Studies.Domain slug for legacy imported corpus rows. ●DomainDomainExpansionTargets Domain tag matching Studies.Domain. ●Is SyntheticStudies TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.
                            Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
                            ƒ Projected Count DomainExpansionTargets Gap Count DomainExpansionTargets Is Under Represented DomainExpansionTargets
                            Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Domain Studies ● Legacy Domain Alias DomainExpansionTargets ● Domain DomainExpansionTargets ● Is Synthetic Studies

                            Real studies in corpus matching LegacyDomainAlias (or Domain when no alias).

                            Σ Candidate Queued Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-317
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A domain">domain expansion target's candidate queued count is the number of the domain expansion target's candidate study catalog that have a ingestion status of “candidate”.

                            In English

                            Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose domain">domain is domain and ingestion status is "candidate".

                            As a formula
                            = COUNTIFS(Domain, Domain, Ingestion Status, "candidate")
                            Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
                            ●DomainCandidateStudyCatalog Domain tag: medicine, epidemiology, legal, sports, education, economics, social-science. ●DomainDomainExpansionTargets Domain tag matching Studies.Domain. ●Ingestion StatusCandidateStudyCatalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
                            Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
                            ƒ Projected Count DomainExpansionTargets Gap Count DomainExpansionTargets Is Under Represented DomainExpansionTargets
                            Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Domain CandidateStudyCatalog ● Domain DomainExpansionTargets ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Catalog candidates in this domain.

                            ƒ Projected Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-318
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A domain expansion target's projected count is computed as the current imported count plus the candidate queued count.

                            In English

                            Current imported count plus candidate queued count.

                            As a formula
                            = Current Imported Count + Candidate Queued Count
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ΣCurrent Imported CountDomainExpansionTargets Count of Studies whose domain is if legacy domain alias is not "" then legacy domain alias, otherwise domain and is synthetic is false(). ΣCandidate Queued CountDomainExpansionTargets Count of CandidateStudyCatalog whose domain is domain and ingestion status is "candidate".
                            Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Gap Count DomainExpansionTargets ƒ Is Under Represented DomainExpansionTargets
                            Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Domain Studies ● Legacy Domain Alias DomainExpansionTargets ● Domain DomainExpansionTargets ● Is Synthetic Studies ● Domain CandidateStudyCatalog ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Current + queued candidates.

                            ƒ Gap Count Calculated 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-319
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A domain expansion target's gap count is computed as the largest of 0 and the target min count minus the projected count.

                            In English

                            The largest of 0 and (target min count minus projected count).

                            As a formula
                            = MAX(0, Target Min Count - Projected Count)
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ●Target Min CountDomainExpansionTargets Minimum real studies desired in this domain. ƒProjected CountDomainExpansionTargets Current imported count plus candidate queued count.
                            Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Target Min Count DomainExpansionTargets ● Domain Studies ● Legacy Domain Alias DomainExpansionTargets ● Domain DomainExpansionTargets ● Is Synthetic Studies ● Domain CandidateStudyCatalog ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog

                            Studies still needed to hit target after queued imports.

                            ƒ Is Under Represented Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-320
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A domain expansion target is considered under represented if the projected count is less than the target min count.

                            In English

                            Projected count is less than target min count.

                            As a formula
                            = Projected Count < Target Min Count
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ƒProjected CountDomainExpansionTargets Current imported count plus candidate queued count. ●Target Min CountDomainExpansionTargets Minimum real studies desired in this domain.
                            Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Domain Studies ● Legacy Domain Alias DomainExpansionTargets ● Domain DomainExpansionTargets ● Is Synthetic Studies ● Domain CandidateStudyCatalog ● Ingestion Status CandidateStudyCatalog ● Target Min Count DomainExpansionTargets

                            TRUE when projected count still below target.

                            Study Import Template

                            A study import template is identified by its name.

                            ● Sort Order Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Order to execute during import.

                            ● Target Table Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Rulebook table to write.

                            ● Row Description Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            What rows to create.

                            ● Required Fields Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Key columns that must be populated.

                            ● Mechanical Check Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            How to verify step complete.

                            Sweep Study Config

                            A sweep study config is identified by its name.

                            ● Sweep Stratum Label Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Stratum whose A/B allocation is swept (highest |AllocationBias| in original data).

                            ● N Sweep Stratum Total Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Total cases in the sweep stratum (fixed across sweep rows).

                            ● Sweep Rate a Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Success rate for treatment A in the sweep stratum.

                            ● Sweep Rate B Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Success rate for treatment B in the sweep stratum.

                            ● N Fixed a Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            A cases in all non-swept strata (fixed).

                            ● N Fixed B Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            B cases in all non-swept strata (fixed).

                            ● Fixed Rate a Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Weighted success rate for A in fixed strata.

                            ● Fixed Rate B Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Weighted success rate for B in fixed strata.

                            ● Original Alloc Fraction a Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Observed fraction of sweep-stratum cases assigned to treatment A.

                            ● Sweep Corrected Gap Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Allocation-free gap: sum_s (n_s/N)*(rate_As-rate_Bs). Invariant under within-stratum reallocation.

                            Substrate Conformance Field

                            A substrate conformance field is identified by its name.

                            ● Source Table Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            TreatmentRankings | StratumSummaries | StratumVariables

                            ● Field Name Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            PascalCase rulebook field name.

                            ● Pg Column Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            snake_case vw_* column.

                            ● Owl Local Name Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            camelCase OWL property local name.

                            ● Data Type Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            float | int | bool | str

                            ● Assert From Postgres Ground truth boolean

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            TRUE when OWL cannot derive this field yet and Postgres value is asserted before SHACL fixpoint.

                            ● In Compare Set Ground truth boolean

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            TRUE when reason.py diffs this field between substrates.

                            Discovery Hypothes

                            A discovery hypothes is identified by its name.

                            ● Statement Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Natural-language claim under test.

                            ● Expected Outcome Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Pass criterion in plain language.

                            ● Registered in Loop Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            LoopId where this hypothesis was registered.

                            ● Epistemic Tier Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            consistency-check (definition-linked regression) | corpus-hypothesis (contingent corpus pattern)

                            Discovery Finding

                            A discovery finding is identified by its name.

                            ↗ Hypothesis Statement Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-321
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A discovery finding's hypothesis statement is the value of LOOKUP(HypothesisId, DiscoveryHypotheses[HypothesisId], DiscoveryHypotheses[Statement]).

                            In English

                            LOOKUP({{HypothesisId}}, DiscoveryHypotheses[HypothesisId], DiscoveryHypotheses[Statement]).

                            As a formula
                            = LOOKUP(Hypothesis ID, DiscoveryHypotheses[HypothesisId], DiscoveryHypotheses[Statement])
                            Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                            ●Hypothesis IDDiscoveryFindings FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId.
                            Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Hypothesis ID DiscoveryFindings

                            Lookup: DiscoveryHypotheses.Statement.

                            ƒ Observed Metric Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-322
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A discovery finding's observed metric is computed as the value of IF(HypothesisId = "H-latent-d", CONCAT("fraction=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-purity", CONCAT("maxPurity=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-small-effect", CONCAT("stable=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]), " latent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-econ-zero", CONCAT("flips=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-dist", CONCAT("epi=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyAvgDistortion]), " edu=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationAvgDistortion])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-causal-manifest", CONCAT("confFlip=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), " collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-causal-latent", CONCAT("collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]), " collLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionLatentOnlyCount]), " collN=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-explained-confounder", CONCAT("ExplainedConfounderCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]), ", ConfounderSignFlipCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-unexplained-nonconfounder", CONCAT("ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-catalog-exact-match", CONCAT("exactRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-catalog-flip-prediction", CONCAT("flipPredRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled", CONCAT("econHighImbFlips=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsHighImbalanceSignFlipCount]), "; epiRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]), "; legal=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LegalHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]), "; sports=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsHighImbalanceSignFlipRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-no-manifest-v2", CONCAT("collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-cplus-magnitude", CONCAT("C+=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]), " C-=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CMinusAvgDistortion]), " D=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypeDAvgDistortion])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-econ-encoding-selection", CONCAT("EconExpectedAMismatchRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsExpectedAMismatchRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-profiles-stable", CONCAT("eduLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationLatentFraction]), " sportsLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsLatentFraction]), " econFlipRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-corrected-gap-invariant", CONCAT("maxRange=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[MaxStudySweepCorrectedGapRange]), " fails=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CorrectedGapInvariantFailCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-explained-bidirectional", CONCAT("explained=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]), " confFlip=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), " falsePos=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[FalsePositiveExplainedCount]), " unexplained=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-no-manifest-theorem", CONCAT("collN=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount]), " collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-theorem-portfolio", CONCAT("theoremCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TheoremCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-age-identity-flip-rate", CONCAT("rate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityManifestFlipRate]), ", n=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityStudyCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-identity-map-coverage", CONCAT("coverage=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[IdentityMapCoverageRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-severity-medicine-manifest", CONCAT("medicine_flip_rate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityMedicineManifestFlipRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-severity-epi-latent-only", CONCAT("epi_manifest_count=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityEpiManifestFlipCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-mechanism-other-drain", CONCAT("other_count=", LOOKUP("cluster-id-mechanism-other", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-selection-frailty-zero-manifest", CONCAT("manifest=", LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipCount]), ", n=", LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-identity-low-manifest", CONCAT("rate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderIdentityManifestFlipRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-geographic-stable-type-d", CONCAT("type_d_frac=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1",                             Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                            ●Hypothesis IDDiscoveryFindings FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId.
                            Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Evidence DiscoveryFindings
                            Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Hypothesis ID DiscoveryFindings

                            Witnessed metric value from ModelSummary for this hypothesis.

                            ƒ Is Confirmed Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-323
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A discovery finding is considered confirmed if the value of IF(HypothesisId = "H-latent-d", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction]) > 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-purity", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax]) < 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-small-effect", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD]), IF(HypothesisId = "H-econ-zero", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipCount]) = 0, IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-dist", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationAvgDistortion]), IF(HypothesisId = "H-causal-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]) >= 10), IF(HypothesisId = "H-causal-latent", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionLatentOnlyCount]) >= 5), IF(HypothesisId = "H-explained-confounder", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) = LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) >= 10), IF(HypothesisId = "H-unexplained-nonconfounder", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount]) = 0, IF(HypothesisId = "H-catalog-exact-match", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-catalog-flip-prediction", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsHighImbalanceSignFlipCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]) > 0.15), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-no-manifest-v2", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, IF(HypothesisId = "H-cplus-magnitude", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CMinusAvgDistortion]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypeDAvgDistortion])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-econ-encoding-selection", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsExpectedAMismatchRate]) > 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-profiles-stable", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationLatentFraction]) > 0.5, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsLatentFraction]) > 0.5, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipRate]) < 0.05), IF(HypothesisId = "H-corrected-gap-invariant", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[MaxStudySweepCorrectedGapRange]) < 0.0001, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CorrectedGapInvariantFailCount]) = 0), IF(HypothesisId = "H-explained-bidirectional", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) = LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[FalsePositiveExplainedCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount]) = 0), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-no-manifest-theorem", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount]) >= 10), IF(HypothesisId = "H-theorem-portfolio", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TheoremCount]) >= 4, IF(HypothesisId = "H-age-identity-flip-rate", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityManifestFlipRate]) >= 0.40, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityStudyCount]) >= 10), IF(HypothesisId = "H-identity-map-coverage", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[IdentityMapCoverageRate]) >= 0.95, IF(HypothesisId = "H-severity-medicine-manifest", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityMedicineManifestFlipRate]) >= 0.45, IF(HypothesisId = "H-severity-epi-latent-only", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityEpiManifestFlipCount]) = 0, IF(HypothesisId = "H-mechanism-other-drain", LOOKUP("cluster-id-mechanism-other", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) <= 5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-selection-frailty-zero-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 3), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-identity-low-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderIdentityManifestFlipRate]) <= 0.15, LOOKUP("cluster-id-collider-proxy", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 5), IF(HypothesisId = "H-geographic-stable-type-d", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicTypeDFraction]) >= 0.25, LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 3), IF(HypothesisId = "H-unexplained-flips-only-nonconfounders", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount]) = 0, FALSE()))))))))))))))))))))))))))))).

                            In English

                            IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-latent-d", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction]) > 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-purity", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-small-effect", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-age-identity-flip-rate", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityManifestFlipRate]) >= 0.40, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1",                             Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                            ●Hypothesis IDDiscoveryFindings FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId.
                            Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
                            ƒ Evidence DiscoveryFindings
                            Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Hypothesis ID DiscoveryFindings

                            TRUE when the witnessed metric satisfies the pre-registered ExpectedOutcome.

                            ƒ Evidence Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-324
                            In RuleSpeak

                            The discovery finding's evidence is determined by the following priority:

                            1. the literal “PASS: ”, followed by the observed metric if the is confirmed is true
                            2. the literal “FAIL: ”, followed by the observed metric if the is confirmed is false
                            3. an empty string otherwise
                            In English

                            If is confirmed equals true() then "PASS: " joined with observed metric, otherwise if is confirmed equals false() then "FAIL: " joined with observed metric, otherwise "".

                            As a formula
                            = IF(Is Confirmed = TRUE(), CONCAT("PASS: ", Observed Metric), IF(Is Confirmed = FALSE(), CONCAT("FAIL: ", Observed Metric), ""))
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ƒIs ConfirmedDiscoveryFindings IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-latent-d", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction]) > 0.5,                             Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Hypothesis ID DiscoveryFindings

                            Pass/fail witness string for UI and conclusions.

                            ● Witnessed in Loop Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            LoopId where this finding was witnessed.

                            Corpus Domain

                            A corpus domain is identified by its name.

                            ● Display Name Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Human-readable domain name.

                            ● Expansion Target Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Target study count from corpus-expansion-plan.md.

                            ● Legacy Studies Domain Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Studies.Domain alias for counting already-imported corpus rows.

                            ● Plan Source Section Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Section heading in corpus-expansion-plan.md.

                            Confounder Identity

                            A confounder identity is identified by its name.

                            ● Display Name Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Human-readable archetype name.

                            ● Mechanism Class Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Mechanism taxonomy token.

                            ● Description Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Narrative description of confounding mechanism.

                            ● Policy Default Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Default analytic policy for this confounding archetype.

                            Σ Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-325
                            In RuleSpeak

                            A confounder identity's study count is the number of the confounder identity's studies.

                            In English

                            Count(map id, confounder identity, confounder identity id).

                            As a formula
                            = COUNT(Map ID, ConfounderIdentity, Confounder Identity ID)
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ●Map IDStratumVariableIdentityMaps Unique slug. ●Confounder Identity IDConfounderIdentities Unique slug (e.g. id-age-composition).
                            Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Map ID StratumVariableIdentityMaps ● Confounder Identity ID ConfounderIdentities

                            Studies with a StratumVariable mapped to this identity.

                            Stratum Variable Identity Map

                            A stratum variable identity map is identified by its name and is related to a stratum variable and a confounder identity.

                            ⇢ Stratum Variable Relationship string

                            A pointer to Stratum Variable. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

                            FK → StratumVariables.

                            ⇢ Confounder Identity Relationship string

                            A pointer to Confounder Identity. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

                            FK → ConfounderIdentities.

                            ● Variable Name Normalized Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Lowercase normalized variable token for dedup audit.

                            Identity Cluster Summary

                            An identity cluster summary is identified by its name and is related to a confounder identity.

                            ⇢ Confounder Identity Relationship string

                            A pointer to Confounder Identity. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

                            FK → ConfounderIdentities.

                            ↗ Display Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-326
                            In RuleSpeak

                            An identity cluster summary's display name is the value of LOOKUP(ConfounderIdentity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName]).

                            In English

                            LOOKUP({{ConfounderIdentity}}, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName]).

                            As a formula
                            = LOOKUP(Confounder Identity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName])
                            Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                            ⇢Confounder IdentityIdentityClusterSummaries FK → ConfounderIdentities.
                            Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ⇢ Confounder Identity IdentityClusterSummaries

                            Lookup archetype display name.

                            ● Study Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Real studies in cluster (customize SQL).

                            ● Manifest Flip Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            IsSignFlip=TRUE count in cluster.

                            ● Latent Flip Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            LatentFlipPotential=TRUE without manifest flip.

                            ● Type D Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Type-D studies in cluster.

                            ● Avg Allocation Distortion Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Mean allocation distortion in cluster.

                            ● Latent Fraction Among Type D Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Fraction of Type-D rows with LatentFlipPotential.

                            ● Domain Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Distinct Studies.Domain values in cluster.

                            ƒ Manifest Flip Rate Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-327
                            In RuleSpeak

                            The identity cluster summary's manifest flip rate is determined by the following priority:

                            1. an empty string if the study count is 0
                            2. the manifest flip count divided by the study count otherwise
                            In English

                            If study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (manifest flip count divided by study count).

                            As a formula
                            = IF(Study Count=0, "", Manifest Flip Count / Study Count)
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ●Study CountIdentityClusterSummaries Real studies in cluster (customize SQL). ●Manifest Flip CountIdentityClusterSummaries IsSignFlip=TRUE count in cluster.
                            Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Study Count IdentityClusterSummaries ● Manifest Flip Count IdentityClusterSummaries

                            ManifestFlipCount / StudyCount.

                            Identity Domain Cell

                            An identity domain cell is identified by its name and is related to a confounder identity.

                            ⇢ Confounder Identity Relationship string

                            A pointer to Confounder Identity. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

                            FK → ConfounderIdentities.

                            ● Domain Ground truth string

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Studies.Domain value (e.g. medicine, epidemiology).

                            ● Study Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Studies with this identity in this domain (customize SQL).

                            ● Manifest Flip Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            IsSignFlip=TRUE count in cell.

                            ● Latent Flip Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            LatentFlipPotential=TRUE without manifest flip.

                            ● Type D Count Ground truth integer

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Type-D studies in cell.

                            ● Latent Fraction Among Type D Ground truth number

                            Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

                            Fraction of Type-D rows with LatentFlipPotential in cell.

                            ƒ Manifest Flip Rate Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-328
                            In RuleSpeak

                            The identity domain cell's manifest flip rate is determined by the following priority:

                            1. an empty string if the study count is 0
                            2. the manifest flip count divided by the study count otherwise
                            In English

                            If study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (manifest flip count divided by study count).

                            As a formula
                            = IF(Study Count=0, "", Manifest Flip Count / Study Count)
                            Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
                            ●Study CountIdentityDomainCells Studies with this identity in this domain (customize SQL). ●Manifest Flip CountIdentityDomainCells IsSignFlip=TRUE count in cell.
                            Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ● Study Count IdentityDomainCells ● Manifest Flip Count IdentityDomainCells

                            ManifestFlipCount / StudyCount.

                            ↗ Identity Display Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-329
                            In RuleSpeak

                            An identity domain cell's identity display name is the value of LOOKUP(ConfounderIdentity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName]).

                            In English

                            LOOKUP({{ConfounderIdentity}}, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName]).

                            As a formula
                            = LOOKUP(Confounder Identity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName])
                            Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
                            ⇢Confounder IdentityIdentityDomainCells FK → ConfounderIdentities.
                            Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
                            ⇢ Confounder Identity IdentityDomainCells

                            Lookup archetype display name.

                            Rendered from the rulebook by the shared RuleSpeak engine (rulebook-rulespeak-core). The same engine narrates the React Explainer DAG, so English, Formula and RuleSpeak read identically across both.

                            ● Ground truth ƒ Calculated Σ Aggregation ↗ Lookup ⇢ Relationship
